[out] {"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_6","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"nl -ba medulla/README.md | sed -n '221,380p'\"","aggregated_output":"   221\t\n   222\tA fixed delay separates attempts and the fallback switch (`MEDULLA_RETRY_DELAY_S`, default 2s — retry storms hit provider rate limits).\n   223\t\n   224\tCrash codes: `E_VALIDATION` (schema/XOR/unknown target/boolean node names/bare keys in pool routing/defaults-inherited self-edges), `E_RENDER` (broken decision-node template: missing file, depth > 10, empty required render), `E_DEADLINE` (pipeline timeout), `E_INPUTS` (source exited non-zero, or emitted mixed-kind/array elements — a broken producer is not an empty queue), `E_INPUTS_LIMIT` (> 10k), `E_HARNESS`, `E_INTERNAL`.\n   225\t\n   226\t`E_HARNESS` is razor-thin by design: **only** \"harness binary missing / unresolvable\". An agent process dying unexpectedly (OOM, segfault, API hiccup) is always a non-zero exit — class B, retryable. If that boundary drifts, transient flakes start crashing whole runs.\n   227\t\n   228\t#### Handling errors in the graph\n   229\t\n   230\tHandlers are **ordinary nodes routed before the terminal** — never a body on the terminal (`kill -9` makes exit hooks an illusion; a node has a timeout, attempts and its own log). The error payload arrives through the same channels as all data: `$MEDULLA_LAST_*` for shell, `{{last.*}}` for prompts. Global catch-all is three lines of `defaults`:\n   231\t\n   232\t```yaml\n   233\tdefaults:\n   234\t  on_signal: {__failed__: notify, __default__: notify}   # root supervisor\n   235\t\n   236\tnodes:\n   237\t  notify:\n   238\t    shell: 'curl -s \"$HOOK\" -d \"text=[medulla] $MEDULLA_LAST_NODE ($MEDULLA_LAST_SIGNAL): $MEDULLA_LAST_MESSAGE\"'\n   239\t    timeout: 30\n   240\t    on_signal: {__default__: __exit_fail__, __failed__: __exit_fail__}   # own dunders explicit!\n   241\t```\n   242\t\n   243\tA node-level edge beats the defaults catch-all, which beats built-ins — a three-tier supervision chain. The validator rejects a defaults-inherited edge that points at its own node (`notify` failing into `notify` would loop); explicit self-loops remain legal (retry pattern). A pool's `__failed__` message is pre-aggregated: `\"2/5 inputs ok (min_success=3); rc!=0 x2, timeout x1, render x0\"` — the manifest has the rest. Class A is not catchable in the graph by definition: the graph itself is what's broken.\n   244\t\n   245\tThe error **message is the body of the signal** that routed to `__exit_fail__` (engine facts carry their own: `__failed__` → rc/join stats, `__empty__` → \"source returned 0 inputs\", `__default__` → tail of stdout). Every run ends with an atomic `outcome.json`:\n   246\t\n   247\t```json\n   248\t{\"outcome\": \"failed\", \"exit_code\": 2,\n   249\t \"error\": {\"code\": \"SIGNAL_FAIL\", \"message\": \"no repo access: git clone rc=128\",\n   250\t           \"node\": \"plan\", \"step\": 12, \"signal\": \"blocked\"},\n   251\t \"steps\": 12, \"duration_s\": 4180}\n   252\t```\n   253\t\n   254\t### Lifecycle\n   255\t\n   256\t**Boot** — parse CLI → load yaml (`version: \"2\"` or a friendly migration error) → validate everything (`E_VALIDATION` before any run dir exists) → create `runs/<ts>-<run_id>/`, snapshot the config, seed vars, prune old runs → set the deadline.\n   257\t\n   258\t**Node loop** (single program counter) — per node: deadline check → materialize inputs (no key → phantom; list → snapshot into the step dir; source → render, execute, sniff, check) → run the pool (`max_parallel` workers; per input: render once → attempts → fallback; stream stdout to the step log with realtime signal extraction) → classify (decision: signal | `__failed__` | `__default__`; pool: `__done__` | `__failed__` | `__empty__`) → resolve the edge (node `on_signal` → `defaults.on_signal` → built-ins) → append the journal row → next node or terminal.\n   259\t\n   260\t**Finish** — atomic `outcome.json`, exit 0/2; crashes write `outcome.json` with the `E_*` code and exit 1; SIGINT → best-effort outcome, exit 130.\n   261\t\n   262\t**Resume** — pick the latest run without `outcome.json`, or with `outcome: interrupted` **or `crashed`** (deliberate extension: the #1 resume trigger is the `E_DEADLINE` crash; config-class crashes simply crash again identically) — or `--run <dir>`: reload the **snapshot** config (a run's config is immutable), vars, journal position; an interrupted pool continues from the manifest done-mask (input identity = `(index, hash)`; sources are never re-executed on resume); an interrupted decision node re-runs whole (body idempotence is the author's concern). The deadline is fresh per invocation.\n   263\t\n   264\t### Layout\n   265\t\n   266\tA pipeline is a self-contained directory — contract, code, history:\n   267\t\n   268\t```\n   269\t.medulla/pipelines/<name>/\n   270\t  pipeline.yaml            # the contract\n   271\t  prompts/  scripts/       # its code\n   272\t  harness/                 # phase 2: harness HOME (sessions), shared by all runs\n   273\t  runs/<ts>-<run_id>/      # one directory per run\n   274\t    pipeline.yaml          # config snapshot as loaded (immutable for the run)\n   275\t    journal.jsonl          # graph chronology, append-only (step, node, rc, signal, message, duration)\n   276\t    vars.yaml              # variables (updated on var signals)\n   277\t    outcome.json           # written only on completion (atomic); absent = running or hard-killed\n   278\t    steps/\n   279\t      001-triage/                        # every step gets a directory\n   280\t      002-plan/prompt.md                 # rendered agent input (what the agent actually saw)\n   281\t      002-plan/attempt-1-codex.txt       # raw CLI stream per attempt\n   282\t      004-apply/inputs.json              # inputs snapshot (resume)\n   283\t      004-apply/input-2-sonnet.txt       # per-input logs\n   284\t      004-apply/manifest.jsonl           # {index, key, input, ok, reason, signal, message,\n   285\t                                         #  rc, timed_out, attempts, fallback, harness, model,\n   286\t                                         #  vars, updates, duration_s, log}\n   287\t```\n   288\t\n   289\tRetention: on start, keep the newest `keep_runs` finished runs; directories without `outcome.json` younger than the pipeline timeout are never pruned. History browsing needs no CLI: `ls runs/`, `cat outcome.json`.\n   290\t\n   291\t**Phase 2 (designed, not in phase 1):** pipeline-scoped harness sessions — `harness/` as the container HOME, session ids captured into journal/manifest from the CLI streams the engine already parses, and a `resume:` field on the agent block to continue a node's last successful session (current run first, then previous runs, newest first). Docker: everything lives under `/workspace` (already mounted RW); credentials are copied once from the read-only `/mnt/*` mounts.\n   292\t\n   293\t### Canonical patterns\n   294\t\n   295\t- **Budget gate** (bounded rework — the engine has no per-node visit caps; cycle semantics belong to the workflow):\n   296\t  ```yaml\n   297\t  gate:\n   298\t    shell: |\n   299\t      n=$(( ${PLAN_TRIES:-0} + 1 ))\n   300\t      echo \"<signal:var key=PLAN_TRIES>$n</signal:var>\"\n   301\t      [ \"$n\" -le 3 ] && echo \"<signal:go>ok</signal:go>\" || echo \"<signal:budget_out>ok</signal:budget_out>\"\n   302\t    on_signal: {go: plan, budget_out: escalate}\n   303\t  ```\n   304\t- **Crawler**: producer node → pool → probe node → edge back; the source re-runs on each new node run.\n   305\t- **Heterogeneous pool**: inputs carry `role`/`prompt` fields; the node prompt is the template (code, vars resolve), input fragments are inserted inert.\n   306\t- **Entry cleanup** (crash-only): there is no `finally`; exit hooks are an illusion under `kill -9`. Clean stale state idempotently at the start of the node/run that needs it clean.\n   307\t- **Parallel tickets**: the engine guarantees vars isolation; file/git isolation is the body's job (`git worktree` per input) and disjointness is the producer script's contract.\n   308\t- **DLQ (repair node)**: failed pool inputs are structured data — re-enqueue only them, never the whole pool:\n   309\t  ```yaml\n   310\t  retry-failed:\n   311\t    inputs: {shell: \"jq -c 'select(.ok|not) | .input' $MEDULLA_MANIFEST_APPLY\"}\n   312\t    max_parallel: 2\n   313\t    shell: 'bash scripts/fix_one.sh \"$MEDULLA_INPUT_ID\"'\n   314\t    on_signal: {__done__: report, __empty__: report}\n   315\t  ```\n   316\t\n   317\t### Migrating from v1\n   318\t\n   319\tThe v1 engine is removed; this table is the dictionary for porting old pipelines.\n   320\t\n   321\t| v1 | v2 |\n   322\t|---|---|\n   323\t| `stages` / `starting` | `nodes` / `start` |\n   324\t| `runner:` / `llm:` / `executor`+`command` | action fields directly on the node: `agent: {harness, model, effort, args}` xor `shell` |\n   325\t| `loop:` + `list:` + `fetch:` + `parallel: true` | `inputs:` on the node + `max_parallel` |\n   326\t| `done: __next_item__` + `loop_done` | pool joins route `__done__` / `__failed__` / `__empty__` via `min_success` |\n   327\t| `max_iterations` / `reset_iterations` / `on_max` | removed — budget gate pattern (vars) |\n   328\t| hardcoded `max_rounds=500` | pipeline `timeout` (wall-clock deadline) |\n   329\t| `round_timeout` / `fallback_runner` | `defaults: {timeout, fallback}` |\n   330\t| `__exit__` | `__exit_ok__` / `__exit_fail__` |\n   331\t| `ignore_rc` | `ignore_exit_code` (decision nodes only) |\n   332\t| `{{__item__}}` / `{{__list_item__}}` | `{{input}}` family + `MEDULLA_INPUT_*` env |\n   333\t| `MEDULLA_TASK_ID` | `MEDULLA_RUN_ID` |\n   334\t| `.medulla/vars.<task>.yaml` | `.medulla/pipelines/<name>/runs/<id>/` |\n   335\t| `--stage` | `--node` |\n   336\t| gemini executor | removed (use `agy`) |\n   337\t| exit codes 0/1/2/3 ad-hoc | 0 = ok, 1 = engine crash, 2 = workflow fail, 130 = interrupt |\n   338\t\n   339\t### Reserved (designed, not implemented — in priority order)\n   340\t\n   341\t`stall_timeout` (no-stdout watchdog for hung agents; partially covered today by per-harness flags — codex `stream_idle_timeout_ms`, agy `--print-timeout`) · manifest attempt states (`claimed|started|completed`) + per-node resume policy `rerun|skip|probe` (exactly-once is impossible; make duplicate side effects detectable) · `resume:` (continue a node's session — phase 2) · `cancel_rest` (race joins) · `on_input_fail: abort` (fail-fast pools) · `format:` on sources (override sniffing) · `finally` (best-effort only, if reality ever demands it) · agent-block defaults.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

[out] {"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"nl -ba medulla/README.md | sed -n '1,220p'\"","aggregated_output":"     1\t# medulla\n     2\t\n     3\t**medulla is a state machine for AI agents.** You describe work as a YAML pipeline — a graph of **nodes** — and medulla runs each node's action on a shell command or an agent harness (`claude-code`, `codex`, `opencode`, `agy`). Bodies emit **signals**; signals route the graph. Built in: retries, fallback to another model, pools with a success threshold, and crash-safe resume.\n     4\t\n     5\t```\n     6\tnode  = action (shell | agent) [× inputs] → signals → next node\n     7\tgraph = nodes + on_signal edges + two terminals (__exit_ok__ / __exit_fail__)\n     8\t```\n     9\t\n    10\t**One machine.** Internally every node is a pool: a node without `inputs:` runs over a single phantom input. Policy (timeouts, attempts, fallback) works identically in both modes. The only thing `inputs:` switches is the **signal contract**: without inputs the body talks to the graph (decision node); with inputs the body writes to the manifest and only the join result routes (pool node).\n    11\t\n    12\t> **Status**: v2 contract (frozen), engine implemented — the v1 engine is **deleted**; v1 pipelines are not supported. `version: \"2\"` is required in every pipeline; the engine rejects anything else with a pointer to the migration table below. Implementation phases: **1** — engine (this document), **2** — harness sessions & run history polish.\n    13\t\n    14\t## Usage\n    15\t\n    16\t```bash\n    17\tmedulla -w .medulla/pipelines/<name>              # run a pipeline\n    18\tmedulla -w ... --var KEY=VALUE                    # set/override vars\n    19\tmedulla -w ... --node <name>                      # dev: start from a specific node\n    20\tmedulla -w ... --resume                           # continue the latest unfinished run\n    21\tmedulla -w ... --run <dir>                        # continue a specific run\n    22\tmedulla --docker -w ...                           # run inside the pipeline's Docker image\n    23\t```\n    24\t\n    25\t## Example\n    26\t\n    27\t```yaml\n    28\tversion: \"2\"\n    29\tstart: triage\n    30\ttimeout: 86400                # whole-run deadline (default 24h; 0 = unlimited)\n    31\tvars: {BRANCH: main}\n    32\t\n    33\tdefaults:                     # policy defaults for every action (\"field absent → take from here\")\n    34\t  timeout: 1800\n    35\t  fallback: {agent: {harness: claude-code, model: opus}}\n    36\t\n    37\tnodes:\n    38\t\n    39\t  # decision node: body signals route the graph\n    40\t  triage:\n    41\t    shell: |\n    42\t      git worktree prune; rm -f .locks/*        # crash-only: clean up on entry\n    43\t      n=$(rg -l \"FIXME\" src/ | wc -l | tr -d ' ')\n    44\t      [ \"$n\" -gt 0 ] && echo \"<signal:found>ok</signal:found>\" || echo \"<signal:clean>ok</signal:clean>\"\n    45\t    timeout: 60\n    46\t    on_signal: {found: plan, clean: __exit_ok__}\n    47\t\n    48\t  plan:\n    49\t    agent: {harness: codex, model: gpt-5.5, effort: xhigh}\n    50\t    prompt: |\n    51\t      {{file:prompts/plan.md}}\n    52\t      Branch: {{var:BRANCH}}. Write the plan to plan.md and emit signal planned.\n    53\t    max_attempts: 2\n    54\t    fallback: {agent: {harness: claude-code, model: sonnet}}\n    55\t    on_signal:\n    56\t      planned: panel\n    57\t      __failed__: __exit_fail__    # body died (rc != 0 after attempts+fallback)\n    58\t\n    59\t  # pool node: inputs switch the contract — the join routes, bodies write to the manifest\n    60\t  panel:\n    61\t    inputs:\n    62\t      - {slug: gpt5,   harness: codex,       model: gpt-5.5, effort: xhigh}\n    63\t      - {slug: sonnet, harness: claude-code, model: sonnet}\n    64\t      - {slug: gem,    harness: agy,         model: \"Gemini 3.1 Pro (High)\"}\n    65\t    max_parallel: all\n    66\t    min_success: 1\n    67\t    agent: {harness: \"{{input.harness}}\", model: \"{{input.model}}\", effort: \"{{input.effort:-}}\"}\n    68\t    prompt: |\n    69\t      You are panelist {{input.slug}} ({{input_index}}/{{input_count}}).\n    70\t      Critique the plan: {{file:plan.md}}\n    71\t      Write your verdict to reviews/{{input.slug}}.md\n    72\t    post: 'test -s \"reviews/{{input.slug}}.md\"'   # verdict = artifact exists, not agent mood\n    73\t    on_signal: {__done__: apply}\n    74\t\n    75\t  apply:\n    76\t    inputs: {shell: \"python3 scripts/tickets.py --json\", timeout: 60}\n    77\t    max_parallel: 3\n    78\t    min_success: 2\n    79\t    shell: |\n    80\t      bash scripts/fix_one.sh \"$MEDULLA_INPUT_ID\" \"$MEDULLA_INPUT_TITLE\"\n    81\t    max_attempts: 2\n    82\t    on_signal:\n    83\t      __done__: report\n    84\t      __empty__: report        # empty queue is legal here — route it explicitly\n    85\t\n    86\t  report:\n    87\t    shell: |\n    88\t      ok=$(jq -s 'map(select(.ok))|length' \"$MEDULLA_MANIFEST_APPLY\")\n    89\t      total=$(jq -s length \"$MEDULLA_MANIFEST_APPLY\")\n    90\t      [ \"$ok\" -eq \"$total\" ] && echo \"<signal:ready>ok</signal:ready>\" || echo \"<signal:rework>ok</signal:rework>\"\n    91\t    on_signal: {ready: __exit_ok__, rework: plan}\n    92\t```\n    93\t\n    94\t## Reference\n    95\t\n    96\t### Pipeline level\n    97\t\n    98\t| Field | Default | Meaning |\n    99\t|---|---|---|\n   100\t| `version` | required | must be `\"2\"` |\n   101\t| `start` | required | first node |\n   102\t| `vars` | `{}` | initial variables (also exported to env; see blacklist below) |\n   103\t| `timeout` | 86400 | wall-clock deadline for the whole run; `0` = unlimited. The engine clamps every child timeout to the remaining budget |\n   104\t| `defaults` | — | policy defaults for actions: `timeout`, `max_attempts`, `ignore_exit_code`, `fallback`, `on_signal` (per-key merge). Flat scalars only — never merged deep |\n   105\t| `keep_runs` | 20 | auto-prune of run history on start |\n   106\t\n   107\t### Node = action [+ pool] + routing\n   108\t\n   109\tAction (exactly one of `shell` / `agent`):\n   110\t\n   111\t| Field | Meaning |\n   112\t|---|---|\n   113\t| `shell` | shell command; its config *is* the command. `prompt` here is a validation error |\n   114\t| `agent` | `{harness, model, effort, args}` — one entity, one block. Scalar shortcut: `agent: codex` ≡ `agent: {harness: codex}`. `args` is a raw CLI escape hatch — non-portable across harnesses |\n   115\t| `prompt` | agent input (not config). Never quote signal syntax literally in prompts — describe it (\"emit signal planned\"); the engine delivers the syntax to the agent |\n   116\t| `timeout` | per **attempt**, seconds |\n   117\t| `max_attempts` | attempts per runner, default 1. Primary gets N, then fallback gets N. Retryable outcomes: non-zero exit, timeout (recorded as rc 124), and **silence** — an agent body exiting 0 with no known signal (the most common agent flake: the work happened, the tag didn't). Silence retries on the **primary only** and never triggers fallback (another model drops the tag just as often; blind fallback duplicates side effects); after all attempts it classifies as `__default__`. Shell silence is deterministic and is not retried. **Silence handling is decision-node behavior**: in pools the body is not expected to signal — silence at rc 0 is the normal ok outcome (see the pool rows) |\n   118\t| `fallback` | alternate agent action after primary attempts are exhausted. Agent-only (a fallback for shell is meaningless); a fallback has no fallback |\n   119\t| `ignore_exit_code` | rc != 0 doesn't classify the body as failed; outcome comes from signals. **Forbidden in pool nodes** — `min_success` already owns that role |\n   120\t\n   121\tHooks around the body — `pre` / `post` (shell-only; rendered like everything else). These are not lifecycle hooks but **deterministic voices in the signal channel** — the only way to put shell before/after an agent body (you can't append a line to a prompt):\n   122\t\n   123\t| `pre` — once per node run, before the body renders | Effect |\n   124\t|---|---|\n   125\t| emits a routing signal | body and `post` are **skipped**, the signal routes (guard: \"already done / not needed\") |\n   126\t| emits `var` | applied **before** the body renders — env prep, the prompt sees fresh vars |\n   127\t| exits non-zero | `__failed__` (preparation broke), body never runs |\n   128\t| exits 0, silent | normal path |\n   129\t\n   130\t| `post` — after **every** attempt, before signal resolution; sees `MEDULLA_BODY_RC` / `MEDULLA_BODY_SIGNAL` | Effect |\n   131\t|---|---|\n   132\t| exits non-zero | the **attempt failed** → body+post retry within `max_attempts`, then fallback (\"try until the artifact exists\": `post: test -s out.md`) |\n   133\t| exits 0 + emits a signal | **overrides** the body's signal (the agent said planned, the plan is garbage → route needs_rework) |\n   134\t| exits 0, silent | the body's outcome stands |\n   135\t\n   136\tIn pools both run per input: a `pre` guard skips already-done inputs (manifest `ok: true` with the guard's signal); a `post` check is the truth channel for agent inputs — CLIs exit 0 even when the model did nothing, so without `post` a pool's `min_success` counts air.\n   137\t\n   138\tPool (presence of `inputs` turns the action into a pool):\n   139\t\n   140\t| Field | Meaning |\n   141\t|---|---|\n   142\t| `inputs` | YAML list = **data** (scalars or objects, one kind per pool, arrays forbidden), or `{shell: \"cmd\", timeout: 60}` = **source**. Output sniffing by first byte: `[` JSON array, `{` JSON-lines, else plain lines. A bare string is a hard validation error (data vs code ambiguity). Hard cap: 10 000 inputs |\n   143\t| `max_parallel` | pool cap: `1` (default, sequential) \\| N \\| `all` |\n   144\t| `min_success` | join threshold: `all` (default) \\| N ≥ 1. Input ok = rc == 0. No short-circuiting ever — all inputs run (side effects have value) |\n   145\t\n   146\tRouting:\n   147\t\n   148\t| Field | Meaning |\n   149\t|---|---|\n   150\t| `on_signal` | map of signal → target. Targets are plain strings: a node name, `__exit_ok__`, `__exit_fail__` |\n   151\t\n   152\t### The reserved namespace — all 8 names\n   153\t\n   154\tLeft-side law: **a bare name is a signal your code emits; a dunder is an engine key.** Right-side law: **terminals are `__exit_*`.**\n   155\t\n   156\tRouting signals (left side):\n   157\t\n   158\t| Name | Emitted by | When | Built-in route |\n   159\t|---|---|---|---|\n   160\t| `__done__` | pool join | successes ≥ `min_success` | **none — must be routed explicitly** (it's a real edge) |\n   161\t| `__failed__` | engine | decision node: body died after all attempts+fallback. Pool: join below threshold | `__exit_fail__` |\n   162\t| `__empty__` | engine | source returned rc 0 and zero inputs (bodies never run) | `__exit_fail__` — loud by default; route explicitly where emptiness is legal |\n   163\t| `__default__` | nobody (matcher) | body exited rc 0 with no known signal (\"said nothing\") | `__exit_fail__` |\n   164\t\n   165\tChannel signals (emitted in stdout, never route; reserved bare words — using them as `on_signal` keys is a validation error):\n   166\t\n   167\t| Name | Meaning |\n   168\t|---|---|\n   169\t| `var` | `<signal:var key=K>value</signal:var>` — set a variable (applied per the fold law) |\n   170\t| `update` | `<signal:update>msg</signal:update>` — progress message |\n   171\t\n   172\tTerminal nodes (right side):\n   173\t\n   174\t| Name | Action |\n   175\t|---|---|\n   176\t| `__exit_ok__` | end the run, exit 0 |\n   177\t| `__exit_fail__` | end the run, exit 2; the routing signal's body becomes the error message |\n   178\t\n   179\tSignals are read from **stdout only**; stderr goes verbatim to the attempt log (a traceback that happens to echo signal text must never route the graph). A known signal emitted before a non-zero exit wins over the exit code. Pool bodies' signals never route — they are recorded in the manifest (law of layers: inputs produce data, joins produce transitions). Bare `done`/`failed` on decision nodes are ordinary user signals with no special meaning. User nodes may not be named `__*__` or `on/off/yes/no/true/false` (YAML 1.1 boolean traps).\n   180\t\n   181\t### Templates & environment\n   182\t\n   183\t| Template | Meaning |\n   184\t|---|---|\n   185\t| `{{var:KEY}}`, `{{var:KEY:-default}}` | variable, with optional default |\n   186\t| `{{file:path}}` | file inclusion, recursive (depth ≤ 10, exceeding = render error with the inclusion chain; missing file = error). Relative paths resolve against the **including file's** directory. Paths are static — no vars inside |\n   187\t| `{{input}}` | the input; objects render as compact JSON |\n   188\t| `{{input.a.b:-default}}` | dot-walk into object inputs. Missing field without a default = hard render error |\n   189\t| `{{input_index}}` (1-based), `{{input_count}}` | position / total |\n   190\t| `{{last.node}}`, `{{last.signal}}`, `{{last.message}}`, `{{last.rc}}` | outcome of the previously completed node — transient tokens (not vars: no persistence, no fold-law constraints). The bridge for agent-bodied handlers whose prompts can't read env |\n   191\t\n   192\tRender model: phase 1 — file inclusion; phase 2 — one simultaneous, **inert** pass of var/input substitution. **Files are code, values are data**: mustache inside included files resolves fully; mustache inside var/input *values* stays literal (injection-safe by construction). Every scalar field of an action is a template (that's why an ensemble is just a pool with per-input `harness`/`model`). A field rendering to an empty string counts as absent — for optional agent fields only; empty `shell`/`prompt`/`harness` is an error. Rendering happens **once per node run**, before attempt 1; retries reuse the same rendered text. A render error on a decision node (phantom input) is an engine crash (`E_RENDER`) — the template itself is broken. A render error for **one pool input** fails that input only (manifest row `reason: render` with the template path and input index) — one malformed row of producer data must not kill the other inputs.\n   193\t\n   194\tEnvironment (data should flow to shell via env, templates are for slugs/paths — quoting-safe):\n   195\t\n   196\t| Variable | Meaning |\n   197\t|---|---|\n   198\t| `MEDULLA_INPUT` | the input as JSON |\n   199\t| `MEDULLA_INPUT_INDEX` / `MEDULLA_INPUT_COUNT` | position / total |\n   200\t| `MEDULLA_INPUT_<KEY>` | each flat scalar field of an object input, uppercased |\n   201\t| `MEDULLA_INPUT_KEY` / `MEDULLA_ATTEMPT_ID` | stable input identity `(index, hash)` / unique attempt id — the idempotency keys for bodies that mutate the outside world (branches, PRs, tickets) |\n   202\t| `MEDULLA_LAST_NODE` / `_SIGNAL` / `_MESSAGE` / `_RC` | outcome of the previously completed node, published before every transition (after a pool, `_RC` is empty — a join has no single rc; read the manifest); `MEDULLA_LAST_EVENT_JSON` carries the same as one JSON object. Timeout is recognizable as rc 124 |\n   203\t| `MEDULLA_MANIFEST_<NODE>` | path to a pool node's manifest (dashes → underscores) |\n   204\t| `MEDULLA_RUN_ID` / `MEDULLA_RUN_DIR` | run id (settable from outside for correlation; else generated) / this run's directory. Put artifacts in `$MEDULLA_RUN_DIR/artifacts/` |\n   205\t| `MEDULLA_TIMEOUT_S` | resolved step timeout, for CLIs that need to size their own |\n   206\t\n   207\tAll pipeline vars are exported to child processes. Reserved names (`PATH`, `HOME`, `SHELL`, `LD_*`, …) are rejected by the validator.\n   208\t\n   209\tVar-signal semantics (fold law): variables are mutable state, and state mutation requires ordering — var signals are **applied only at `max_parallel: 1`** (which includes every decision node), in input order, atomically from the **successful** attempt only. At `max_parallel > 1` they are recorded in the manifest instead.\n   210\t\n   211\t### Errors & exit codes\n   212\t\n   213\tTwo failure classes. The test: *can it be fixed by changing data/prompts/retrying?* → workflow failure, the graph decides. *Does it require editing the pipeline/environment?* → engine crash.\n   214\t\n   215\t| Exit | Class | Meaning |\n   216\t|---|---|---|\n   217\t| 0 | — | `__exit_ok__` |\n   218\t| 1 | engine crash | the pipeline itself is broken — fix the pipeline, retrying is pointless |\n   219\t| 2 | workflow failure | the graph routed to `__exit_fail__` — fix the task/inputs |\n   220\t| 130 | interrupt | — |\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

  pre -> [guard?] -> body attempts (primary -> fallback) with post per attempt.\n   257\t    # The seam never mutates engine state: `apply_pre_vars` is the caller's policy\n   258\t    # (decision: apply to self.vars so the body render sees them; pool at\n   259\t    # a known signal decides\n    23\t    SILENT = \"silent\"    # body finished ok but said nothing routable\n    24\t    RETRY = \"retry\"      # attempt failed mechanically (rc!=0 / timeout / post failed)\n    25\t\n    26\t\n    27\t@dataclass\n    28\tclass AttemptDecision:\n    29\t    verdict: Verdict\n    30\t    signal: str | None = None        # \"shell\" | \"agent\"\n    36\t    rc: int,\n    37\t    timed_out: bool,\n    38\t    body_signal: str | None,      # first KNOWN signal from stdout, or None\n    39\t    post_rc: int | None,          # signal beats rc\n    49\t    if timed_out:\n    50\t        return AttemptDecision(Verdict.RETRY, failure_class=\"timeout\")\n    51\t    if rc == 0 or ignore_exit_code:\n    52\t        return AttemptDecision(Verdict.SILENT)\n    53\t    return AttemptDecision(Verdict.RETRY, failure_class=\"rc\")\n    54\t\n    55\t\n    56\tclass Move(Enum):\n    57\t    DONE = \"done\"                    # node outcome decided: .signal\n    58\t    RETRY_SAME = \"retry_same\"        # re-run current runner\n    59\t    SWITCH_FALLBACK = \"switch\"       # move to fallback runner, attempt counter resets\n    60\t\n    61\t\n    62\t@dataclass\n    63\tclass LoopMove:\n    64\t    move: Move\n    65\t    signal: str | None = None\n    66\t\n    67\t\n    68\tdef next_move(\n    69\t    decision: AttemptDecision,\n    70\t    kind: str,                 # of the CURRENT runner\n    71\t    phase: str,                # \"primary\" | \"fallback\"\n    72\t    attempt: int,              # 1-based, within the current phase\n    73\t    max_attempts: int,\n    74\t    has_fallback: bool,\n    75\t    pool_mode: bool = False,\n    76\t) -> LoopMove:\n    77\t    if decision.verdict is Verdict.ROUTE:\n    78\t        return LoopMove(Move.DONE, decision.signal)\n    79\t\n    80\t    if decision.verdict is Verdict.SILENT:\n    81\t        if pool_mode:\n    82\t            # decision nodes: silence is a failure to communicate — agent retries on\n    86\t        # the PRIMARY only; shell silence is deterministic\n    87\t        if kind == \"agent\" and phase == \"primary\" and attempt < max_attempts:\n    88\t            return LoopMove(Move.RETRY_SAME)\n    89\t        return LoopMove(Move.DONE, SIG_DEFAULT)\n    90\t\n    91\t    # The flock is held for process lifetime and dies with the process.\n     6\t\"\"\"\n     7\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n     8\t\n     9\timport datetime\n    10\timport fcntl\n    11\timport json\n    12\timport os\n    13\timport shutil\n    14\timport threading\n    15\timport uuid\n    16\tfrom pathlib import Path\n    17\t\n    18\timport yaml\n    19\t\n    20\t\n    21\tclass RunLocked(Exception):\n    22\t    \"\"\"Another process holds this run's lock.\"\"\"\n    23\t\n    24\t\n    25\tclass RunStore:\n    26\t    def __init__(self, run_dir: Path, run_id: str):\n    27\t        self.dir = run_dir\n    28\t        self.run_id = run_id\n    29\t        self.steps_dir = run_dir / \"steps\"\n    30\t        self._journal_lock = threading.Lock()\n    31\t        self._step_counter = 0\n    32\t        self._lock_fd = None\n    33\t\n    34\t    def _acquire_flock(self) -> None:\n    35\t        fd = os.open(self.dir / \".lock\", os.O_CREAT | os.O_RDWR)\n    36\t        try:\n    37\t            fcntl.flock(fd, fcntl.LOCK_EX | fcntl.LOCK_NB)\n    38\t        except OSError:\n    39\t            os.close(fd)\n    40\t            raise RunLocked(f\"run {self.run_id} is already in progress\")\n    41\t        os.write(fd, str(os.getpid()).encode())\n    42\t        self._lock_fd = fd                     # ── steps ──\n   108\t    def new_step_dir(self, node_name: str) -> tuple[int, Path]:\n   109\t        self._step_counter += 1\n   110\t        step_dir = self.steps_dir / f\"{self._step_counter:03d}-{node_name}\"\n   111\t        step_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   112\t        return self._step_counter, step_dir\n   113\t\n   114\t    # ── pool manifest: crash-safe done-mask, one complete line per write ──\n   123\t    def manifest_append(self, manifest_path: Path, row: dict) -> None:\n   124\t        line = json.dumps(row, ensure_ascii=False)\n   125\t        with self._journal_lock:\n   126\t            with open(manifest_path, \"a\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n   127\t                f.write(line + \"\\n\")   # single write: a crash can only truncate the tail\n   128\t\n   129\t    # ── vars ──\n   130\t    def write_vars(self, vars_map: dict[str, str]) -> None:\n   131\t        tmp = self.dir / \".vars.tmp\"\n   132\t        tmp.write_text(yaml.safe_dump(dict(vars_map), sort_keys=False), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   133\t        tmp.replace(self.dir / \"vars.yaml\")\n   134\t\n   135\t    # ── outcome: appears only on completion, atomically ──\n   136\t    def write_outcome(self, outcome: dict) -> None:\n   137\t        tmp = self.dir / \".outcome.tmp\"\n   138\t        tmp.write_text(json.dumps(outcome, ensure_ascii=False, indent=1) + \"\\n\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   139\t        tmp.replace(self.dir / \"outcome.json\")\n   140\t\n   141\t\n   142\tdef _now() -> str:\n   143\t    return datetime.datetime.now().strftime(\"%H:%M:%S\")\n   144\t\n   145\t\n   146\tdef _read_jsonl_tolerant(path: Path, what: str) -> list[dict]:\n   147\t    lines = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines()\n   148\t    rows: list[dict] = []\n   149\t    for n, line in enumerate(lines):\n   150\t        line = line.strip()\n   151\t        if not line:\n   152\t            continue\n   153\t        try:\n   154\t            rows.append(json.loads(line))\n   155\t        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n   156\t            if n == len(lines) - 1:\n   157\t                return rows                    # crash-truncated tail: drop, re-run covers it\n   158\t            raise RuntimeError(f\"corrupt {what} at {path}:{n + 1} (non-final line)\")\n   159\t    return rows\n   160\t\n   161\t\n   162\tdef prune_runs(pipeline_dir: Path, keep_runs: int, pipeline_timeout: int | None) -> None:\n   163\t    \"\"\"On boot, after the new run dir exists. Finished (has outcome.json): keep the\n   164\t    newest keep_runs. Unfinished: never touch while younger than the pipeline\n   165\t    timeout (the active-run shield); timeout 0/None = never auto-prune unfinished.\"\"\"\n   166\t    runs_dir = pipeline_dir / \"runs\"\n   167\t    if not runs_dir.is_dir():\n   168\t        return\n   169\t    finished: list[Path] = []\n   170\t    now = datetime.datetime.now()\n   171\t    for run in runs_dir.iterdir():\n   172\t        if not run.is_dir():\n   173\t            continue\n   174\t        if (run / \"outcome.json\").is_file():\n   175\t            finished.append(run)\n   176\t            continue\n   177\t        if pipeline_timeout:\n   178\t            try:\n   179\t                ts = datetime.datetime.strptime(run.name.rsplit(\"-\", 1)[0], \"%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S\")\n   180\t            except ValueError:\n   181\t                continue                       # unrecognized name: leave it alone\n   182\t            if (now - ts).total_seconds() > pipeline_timeout * 2:\n   183\t                shutil.rmtree(run, ignore_errors=True)   # certainly dead: deadline long past\n   184\t    for run in sorted(finished, key=lambda p: p.name, reverse=True)[keep_runs:]:\n   185\t        shutil.rmtree(run, ignore_errors=True)\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Stages emit signals; signals decide the next stage. Built in: retry, per-stage fallback to another model, and crash recovery.\n     4\t\n     5\tUse it to chain agents into a repeatable workflow instead of driving them by hand.\n     6\t\n     7\tThis repository contains:\n     8\t\n     9\t- `cli-agent/` — the runner, executors, scripts, and the `spar` workflow\n    10\t\n    11\t## Setup\n    12\t\n    13\t```bash\n    14\t# Install from GitHub\n    15\tpip install git+https://github.com/skopanev/medulla.git\n    16\t\n    17\t# Install a bundled skill — copies the workflow AND provisions the runtime\n    18\t# (.medulla/medulla + .medulla/scripts are symlinked to the live install)\n    19\tmedulla install-skill spar\n    20\t\n    21\t# Run any workflow\n    22\tmedulla -w <workflow>\n    23\tmedulla -w <workflow>\n    24\tmedulla -w <workflow> --var KEY=VALUE\n    25\t\n    26\t# Run in Docker with mounted repos\n    27\tmedulla --docker -w <workflow> --build \\\n    28\t  --mount ../keys \\\n    29\t  --mount-rw ../backend\n    30\t\n    31\t# Host-builder (separate terminal - bridges java, gradle, maestro, xcodebuild to host)\n    32\t.medulla/scripts/host-builder.sh\n    33\t```\n    34\t\n    35\t## CLI Reference\n    36\t\n    37\t`medulla` supports two invocation styles (the `medulla` command comes from pip; from a source checkout use the repo-root `./medulla` launcher).\n    38\t\n    39\t- Workflow mode: `medulla -w <workflow> [options]`\n    40\t- Direct pipeline mode: `medulla <run|validate|graph> <pipeline.yaml> [options]`\n    41\t\n    42\t### All CLI keys\n    43\t\n    44\t- `-w`, `--workflow <name>` - resolve `.medulla/workflows/<name>/pipeline.yaml` and run that installed workflow.\n    45\t- `run <pipeline.yaml>` - run a pipeline by explicit file path.\n    46\t- `validate <pipeline.yaml>` - validate a pipeline and exit without running stages.\n    47\t- `graph <pipeline.yaml>` - generate and open a graph for a pipeline file.\n    48\t- `--var KEY=VALUE` - override a pipeline variable; repeatable; values split on the first `=` only.\n    49\t- `--docker` - run through the Docker wrapper instead of directly on the host.\n    50\t- `--mount PATH` - Docker mode only; mount an existing extra directory **read-only** at `/workspace/<dirname>`.\n    51\t- `--mount-rw PATH` - Docker mode only; mount an existing extra directory **read-write** at `/workspace/<dirname>`.\n    52\t- `--host` - auto-start `.medulla/scripts/host-builder.sh`, the host bridge for native tools such as `xcodebuild`, `cargo`, `gradle`, `java`, and `maestro`.\n    53\t- `--stage STAGE` - skip to a specific stage (bypass earlier rounds; requires vars from a prior run or `--var`).\n    54\t- `--dry-run` - load/resolve the pipeline without executing stage commands.\n    55\t- `--verbose` - enable verbose logging and executor diagnostics.\n    56\t- `--build` - Docker mode only; force image rebuild via `scripts/docker.py` with `--no-cache`.\n    57\t- `graph` generates a graph file for the pipeline.\n    58\t- If neither `-w` nor a valid `<command> <pipeline>` pair is provided, `medulla` exits with usage help.\n    59\t\n    60\t### Common command patterns\n    61\t\n    62\t```bash\n    63\t# Run an installed workflow\n    64\tmedulla -w <workflow>\n    65\t\n    66\t# Run a workflow with variables\n    67\tmedulla -w <workflow> \\\n    68\t  --var KEY=VALUE \\\n    69\t  --var OTHER_KEY=OTHER_VALUE\n    70\t\n    71\t# Dry-run a workflow locally\n    72\tmedulla -w <workflow> --dry-run --verbose\n    73\t\n    74\t# Validate a raw pipeline file\n    75\tmedulla validate path/to/pipeline.yaml\n    76\t\n    77\t# Generate a graph for a raw pipeline file\n    78\tmedulla graph path/to/pipeline.yaml\n    79\t\n    80\t# Run in Docker with extra mounts\n    81\tmedulla -w <workflow> --docker --build \\\n    82\t  --mount ../readonly-dir \\\n    83\t  --mount-rw ../writable-dir\n    84\t```\n    85\t\n    86\t### Docker wrapper details\n    87\t\n    88\tWhen `--docker` is used, the wrapper additionally supports these behaviors:\n    89\t\n    90\t- Image name comes from `MEDULLA_IMAGE` or defaults to `medulla:latest`.\n    91\t- Dockerfile resolution prefers `workflows/<workflow-root>/Dockerfile`, then falls back to the root `Dockerfile`.\n    92\t- Local config mounts are added automatically when present: `~/.claude`, `~/.codex`, `~/.gemini`, opencode config/auth, `~/.gitconfig`, and the host-builder bridge at `/tmp/medulla-bridge`.\n    93\t\n    94\tForwarded environment variables:\n    95\t\n    96\t- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` - Anthropic credential.\n    97\t- `OPENAI_API_KEY` - OpenAI credential.\n    98\t- `ZHIPU_API_KEY` - Zhipu credential.\n    99\t- `GEMINI_API_KEY` - Gemini credential.\n   100\t- `GOOGLE_API_KEY` - Google API credential.\n   101\t- `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` - Google Cloud project id.\n   102\t- `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` - path to Google credentials.\n   103\t\n   104\tFor custom pipeline inputs, use `--var KEY=VALUE`.\n   105\t\n   106\t### Companion commands\n   107\t\n   108\t| Command                          | Description                                                                                                                           |\n   109\t| -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n   110\t| `medulla init`                | Provision the `.medulla/` runtime (symlinks `medulla/` + `scripts/` to the live install, creates `snapshot/`). `install-skill` runs this for you. |\n   111\t| `medulla upgrade`             | Self-upgrade by reinstalling the latest from GitHub.                                                                                 |\n   112\t| `medulla-graph -w <workflow>`       | Render a workflow pipeline as a graph.                                                                                                |\n   113\t\n   114\t## Workflow Structure\n   115\t\n   116\tA workflow is a directory with a `pipeline.yaml` entry point plus any prompts/scripts it references.\n   117\t\n   118\tTypical layout:\n   119\t\n   120\t```text\n   121\tworkflows/<workflow>/\n   122\t├── pipeline.yaml\n   123\t├── prompts/\n   124\t└── scripts/\n   125\t└── ... any other fodlers you need\n   126\t```\n   127\t\n   128\tMinimal pipeline shape:\n   129\t\n   130\t```yaml\n   131\tversion: \"0.1\"\n   132\tstarting: first-stage\n   133\tround_timeout: 1500 # global timeout per stage\n   134\t\n   135\tvars: # global variables available to all stages\n   136\t  KEY: default\n   137\t\n   138\tfallback_runner: # global fallback runner if a stage fails\n   139\t  llm: claude-code\n   140\t  model: sonnet\n   141\t\n   142\tstages:\n   143\t  first-stage:\n   144\t    runner:\n   145\t      llm: claude-code\n   146\t      model: sonnet\n   147\t      prompt: |\n   148\t        Do the task.\n   149\t    on_signal:\n   150\t      completed: __exit__ # transition to exit stage\n   151\t      failed: stage-name # transition to a named stage\n   152\t      default: __exit__\n   153\t```\n   154\t\n   155\tMain top-level fields:\n   156\t\n   157\t- `version` - pipeline format/version label.\n   158\t- `starting` - name of the first stage.\n   159\t- `round_timeout` - default timeout budget for stage runs.\n   160\t- `vars` - default pipeline variables.\n   161\t- `fallback_runner` - optional pipeline-wide fallback runner if a stage runner fails.\n   162\t- `stages` - map of stage definitions.\n   163\t\n   164\t### Stage shape\n   165\t\n   166\tEach stage is a named block under `stages`.\n   167\t\n   168\t```yaml\n   169\tsome-stage:\n   170\t  max_iterations: 3\n   171\t  runner:\n   172\t    shell: ./scripts/do-work.sh\n   173\t    timeout: 60\n   174\t  fallback_runner:\n   175\t    llm: opencode\n   176\t    model: openai/gpt-5.4\n   177\t  on_signal:\n   178\t    ready: next-stage\n   179\t    failed: __exit__\n   180\t    default: __exit__\n   181\t```\n   182\t\n   183\tStage fields:\n   184\t\n   185\t- `runner` - required; defines how the stage executes.\n   186\t- `finally` - optional cleanup runner; executes after every stage exit (success, failure, timeout, on_max) before transitioning.\n   187\t- `fallback_runner` - optional stage-local fallback.\n   188\t- `on_signal` - required; transition table keyed by emitted signal name.\n   189\t- `max_iterations` - optional safety cap for repeated returns to the same stage.\n   190\t\n   191\t### Runner types\n   192\t\n   193\tA stage runner must define exactly one of these:\n   194\t\n   195\t- `shell` - run a shell command.\n   196\t- `llm` - run an LLM executor.\n   197\t- `loop` - iterate over a list and run an inner runner for each item.\n   198\t\n   199\tShell runner:\n   200\t\n   201\t```yaml\n   202\trunner:\n   203\t  shell: |\n   204\t    python3 scripts/check.py\n   205\t  timeout: 60\n   206\t```\n   207\t\n   208\tLLM runner:\n   209\t\n   210\t```yaml\n   211\trunner:\n   212\t  llm: claude-code\n   213\t  model: sonnet\n   214\t  prompt: |\n   215\t    {{file:prompts/task.md}}\n   216\t  timeout: 1800\n   217\t```\n   218\t\n   219\tLoop runner:\n   220\t\n   221\t```yaml\n   222\trunner:\n   223\t  loop:\n   224\t    list: \"python3 scripts/list_items.py\"\n   225\t    fetch: \"cat {{__list_item__}}\"\n   226\t    runner:\n   227\t      llm: claude-code\n   228\t      model: sonnet\n   229\t      prompt: |\n   230\t        Item: {{__item__}}\n   231\t```\n   232\t\n   233\tLoop fields:\n   234\t\n   235\t- `list` - shell command that produces items to iterate.\n   236\t- `fetch` - optional shell command that loads the current item contents into `{{__item__}}`.\n   237\t- `runner` - inner shell/llm runner executed for each item.\n   238\t\n   239\tLoop stages must map one signal to `__next_item__` and must define `loop_done` in `on_signal`.\n   240\t\n   241\t### Variables and templating\n   242\t\n   243\tVariables come from three places:\n   244\t\n   245\t- `vars` in `pipeline.yaml`\n   246\t- CLI overrides from `--var KEY=VALUE`\n   247\t- runtime `<signal:var>` emissions from stages\n   248\t\n   249\tTemplates supported inside prompts and shell commands:\n   250\t\n   251\t- `{{var:KEY}}` - insert variable value.\n   252\t- `{{var:KEY:-default}}` - insert variable with default fallback.\n   253\t- `{{file:path/to/file}}` - inline file contents.\n   254\t- `{{__list_item__}}` - current loop item identifier/path.\n   255\t- `{{__item__}}` - fetched content for the current loop item.\n   256\t\n   257\tVariable signals look like this:\n   258\t\n   259\t```xml\n   260\t<signal:var key=\"TARGET_DIR\">backend</signal:var>\n   261\t```\n   262\t\n   263\tThat stores `TARGET_DIR=backend` and makes it available to later stages.\n   264\t\n   265\t### Calling LLMs\n   266\t\n   267\tLLM stages use `runner.llm` plus an optional `model`.\n   268\t\n   269\tExamples:\n   270\t\n   271\t- `llm: claude-code`\n   272\t- `llm: claude-code` with `model: sonnet`\n   273\t- `llm: opencode` with `model: openai/gpt-5.4`\n   274\t- `llm: gemini`\n   275\t\n   276\tThe `prompt` field is the full rendered prompt sent to that executor.\n   277\t\n   278\t### Signals and transitions\n   279\t\n   280\tStages communicate by emitting XML-like signals in stdout/stderr output:\n   281\t\n   282\t```xml\n   283\t<signal:ready>done</signal:ready>\n   284\t<signal:failed>something broke</signal:failed>\n   285\t<signal:update>progress message</signal:update>\n   286\t```\n   287\t\n   288\t`on_signal` maps those signals to the next action.\n   289\t\n   290\tSimple transition:\n   291\t\n   292\t```yaml\n   293\ton_signal:\n   294\t  ready: next-stage\n   295\t  failed: __exit__\n   296\t  default: __exit__\n   297\t```\n   298\t\n   299\tTransition with options:\n   300\t\n   301\t```yaml\n   302\ton_signal:\n   303\t  approved:\n   304\t    stage: next-stage\n   305\t    reset_iterations: true\n   306\t```\n   307\t\n   308\tTransition with hook steps before moving on:\n   309\t\n   310\t```yaml\n   311\ton_signal:\n   312\t  approved:\n   313\t    - runner:\n   314\t        shell: python3 scripts/post_process.py\n   315\t    - stage: next-stage\n   316\t```\n   317\t\n   318\tSpecial targets:\n   319\t\n   320\t- `__exit__` - stop the pipeline.\n   321\t- `__next_item__` - move to the next loop item.\n   322\t\n   323\tSpecial signals:\n   324\t\n   325\t- `default` - used when nothing matched.\n   326\t- `loop_done` - required for loop stages when iteration completes.\n   327\t- `on_max` - optional route when `max_iterations` is exceeded.\n   328\t- `update` - progress only; does not transition.\n   329\t- `var` - stores variables; does not transition.\n   330\t\n   331\t### Fallbacks\n   332\t\n   333\tFallbacks let the runner retry a failed stage with another runner.\n   334\t\n   335\t- pipeline-level `fallback_runner` applies globally\n   336\t- stage-level `fallback_runner` overrides it for one stage\n   337\t\n   338\tUse this when the main executor is flaky, overloaded, or you want a second model as backup.\n   339\t\n   340\t## Repository Structure\n   341\t\n   342\t```text\n   343\t.\n   344\t└── cli-agent/\n   345\t    ├── medulla/                # runner, executors, parsing, output, Its headless interface follows\n     5\tClaude Code conventions, NOT Gemini's:\n     6\t\n     7\t  - `--print` TAKES A VALUE (the prompt). It is NOT a boolean mode flag: the\n     8\t    token right after `--print` is consumed as the prompt. Therefore `--print`\n     9\t    must be the LAST flag and the prompt its value — e.g.\n    10\t    `agy <flags> --print \"<prompt>\"`. The earlier layout\n    11\t    `agy --print --dangerously-skip-permissions ... <prompt>` silently fed\n    12\t    `--dangerously-skip-permissions` to `--print` as the prompt, so the skip\n    13\t    flag never took effect (no tool perms → agy wrote no OUTPUT file and\n    14\t    emitted nothing useful). Verified on v1.0.4.\n    15\t  - `--dangerously-skip-permissions` auto-approves tools (required for our\n    16\t    file-editing pipelines — agy writes the OUTPUT file via its own file tool),\n    17\t    replacing gemini's `--approval-mode yolo`.\n    18\t  - output is PLAIN TEXT on stdout — there is no `--output-format`/JSON mode.\n    19\t  - per-invocation model IS supported as of v1.0.4: `--model \"<name>\"` where\n    20\t    <name> is one of `agy models` (e.g. \"Gemini 3.1 Pro (High)\",\n    21\t    \"Claude Opus 4.6 (Thinking)\", \"GPT-OSS 120B (Medium)\"). Reasoning effort is\n    22\t    encoded in the name suffix — (Low)/(Medium)/(High)/(Thinking). `runner.model`\n    23\t    is passed through verbatim to `--model`; if omitted, agy uses the global\n    24\t    \"model\" in `~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/settings.json`. A short-slug alias map\n    25\t    (see _MODEL_ALIASES) lets pipelines use `gemini-3.1-pro-high` style ids too.\n    26\t  - workspace trust is NOT a flag: the cwd must be in `trustedWorkspaces` in the\n    27\t    global settings.json, otherwise `agy --dangerously-skip-permissions` HANGS\n    28\t    waiting for interactive trust. We pre-flight that and fail fast instead.\n    29\t  - `--print-timeout` defaults to 5m, too short for real agent runs — we raise it.\n    30\t\"\"\"\n    31\t\n    32\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n    33\t\n    34\timport json\n    35\timport os\n    36\timport threading\n    37\t\n    38\timport subprocess\n    39\t\n    40\tfrom pilot.signals import SignalScanner\n    41\tfrom pilot.executors.result import ExecutorResult, kill_process_group, start_cancel_watchdog\n    42\t\n    43\t# agy reads/writes its global config here. trustedWorkspaces lives in this file.\n    44\t_SETTINGS_PATH = os.path.expanduser(\"~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/settings.json\")\n    45\t\n    46\t# Generous cap for long agent runs (Go duration string). agy's default is 5m.\n    47\t_PRINT_TIMEOUT = \"60m\"\n    48\t\n    49\t# Convenience slugs → exact `agy models` names. same question in parallel\n#\n# Run directly:\n#   pilot run .pilot/ensemble/pipeline.yaml --var QUESTION=\"your question\"\n#\n# Or install as a Claude Code skill, then /ensemble fires when Claude\n# decides it needs outside perspective:\n#   pilot init-skill ensemble\n#\n# Output: synthesized.md inside .pilot/ensemble/runs/<timestamp>-XXXX/\nversion: \"0.1\"\nstarting: spar\n\n# Skill template — `pilot init-skill ensemble` writes this verbatim to\n# .claude/skills/ensemble/SKILL.md. The skill instructs Claude (the\n# caller) to construct the panel prompt itself, fitted to the\n# situation, then run pilot via its Bash tool. The pipeline stays\n# generic — \"ask N models the same thing in parallel and save\".\nskill: |\n  ---\n  name: ensemble\n  description: |\n    A panel of independent models attacks the same decision in parallel —\n    counsel without consensus, scrutiny without sycophancy. Each returns\n    reasoning, verdict, and the strongest counter to whatever's being\n    leaned toward. For decisions that can't be confirmed by facts:\n    strategic, architectural, organizational, career, product. The panel\n    exists to disagree. If a fact lookup or a code change would resolve\n    the question, this is the wrong tool.\n  allowed-tools: Bash(pilot-docker run *) Bash(cat *) Bash(ls *)\n  effort: high\n  ---\n\n  You're consulting an independent panel — outside perspective on a\n  non-trivial call you're working through. The panel does not see\n  this conversation, your codebase, or your prior reasoning.\n  Everything they need to give a useful answer has to be in the prompt\n  you build.\n\n  # Build the prompt\n\n  The prompt is the artifact. Treat it as the brief you'd give a\n  senior outsider you trusted: complete enough to answer without\n  follow-up, sharp enough to provoke real thinking.\n\n  Cover:\n\n  - **The problem.** What's actually being decided. State it precisely.\n  - **Progress so far.** What you've tried, what you've ruled out,\n    what you currently think and why. Make your leaning explicit so\n    the panel can attack it.\n  - **The stuck point.** Where your reasoning runs out, what data\n    you don't have, what you genuinely cannot decide alone.\n  - **Files in the repo (use them).** The panel runs as full agents —\n    claude-code, codex, gemini — each with file-read tools and your\n    working directory inherited as their cwd. Any path you reference\n    resolves the same way it does for you. Files matter not just for\n    the *answer* but for the *context the panel needs to answer well*:\n    the code the question is about, strategy or architecture docs,\n    prior decisions, design notes, planning artifacts, README files.\n    **Instruct the panel to read what's relevant** — be concrete about\n    file paths, line ranges, grep patterns. Don't paste large snippets\n    into the prompt when you can point at the source; pasting wastes\n    tokens the panel could spend reasoning.\n  - **Framing.** Pick what fits:\n     - Sparring with verdicts (COMMIT / DRAW / INSUFFICIENT) for\n       binary or near-binary calls with stakes.\n     - Multiple-lens roleplay (assign each panelist a distinct role —\n       skeptic, devil's advocate, operator, contrarian) for relational,\n       organizational, or judgment calls.\n     - Red-team for plans about to execute.\n     - Whatever else suits the situation. These are starting points,\n       not a menu.\n  - **Demands.** Depth, dissent, the strongest counter, the blind\n    spot you're least likely to see. Forbid sycophancy. Mark cited\n    facts `(R)` for confident recall, `(G)` for guess. Cap each\n    panelist at ~500 words.\n\n  # Run the panel\n\n  Mechanical contract — invoke verbatim, substituting your built\n  prompt for the QUESTION value:\n\n      pilot-docker run .pilot/ensemble/pipeline.yaml --var \"QUESTION=<your prompt>\" >&2\n      cat \"$(ls -td .pilot/ensemble/runs/* | head -1)/synthesized.md\"\n\n  The cat'd output is the panel's combined takes, separated by headers.\n\n  # Use the result\n\n  You called the panel because you needed perspective. Now you have\n  takes from outside your context. Read them, then:\n\n  1. Notice where the panel converges — rare; worth flagging.\n  2. Notice where they diverge, and identify which divergence most\n     matters for the specific decision you're working through.\n  3. Notice what the panel collectively missed because they didn't\n     see your conversation, your codebase, or your actual constraints.\n\n  Do not soften their verdicts when integrating into your reasoning.\n  Do not flatten dissent into consensus through restatement. The\n  disagreement is the signal — the entire point of running this.\n\npre_pipeline: |\n  if [ -z \"${QUESTION:-}\" ]; then\n    echo \"ERROR: provide a question via --var QUESTION=\\\"your question\\\"\"\n    exit 1\n  fi\n\nstages:\n  spar:\n    pre_step: |\n      mkdir -p \"$PILOT_CONFIG_DIR/runs\"\n      ROUND_DIR=$(mktemp -d \"$PILOT_CONFIG_DIR/runs/$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)-XXXX\")\n      printf '%s\\n' \"$QUESTION\" > \"$ROUND_DIR/question.md\"\n      echo \"<signal:var key=ROUND_DIR>$ROUND_DIR</signal:var>\"\n      echo \"<signal:var key=OUTPUT_GLM5>$ROUND_DIR/glm5.md</signal:var>\"\n      echo \"<signal:var key=OUTPUT_GPT5>$ROUND_DIR/gpt5.md</signal:var>\"\n      echo \"<signal:var key=OUTPUT_SONNET>$ROUND_DIR/sonnet.md</signal:var>\"\n      echo \"<signal:var key=OUTPUT_AGY>$ROUND_DIR/agy.md</signal:var>\"\n    prompt: |\n      {{file:prompts/spar.md}}\n    runners:\n      - executor: opencode\n        model: zai-coding-plan/glm-5.1\n        vars:\n          OUTPUT: \"{{var:OUTPUT_GLM5}}\"\n      - executor: codex\n        model: gpt-5.5\n        vars:\n          OUTPUT: \"{{var:OUTPUT_GPT5}}\"\n      - executor: claude-code\n        model: sonnet\n        vars:\n          OUTPUT: \"{{var:OUTPUT_SONNET}}\"\n      - executor: antigravity\n        model: gemini-3.1-pro-high\n        vars:\n          OUTPUT: \"{{var:OUTPUT_AGY}}\"\n    parallel: true\n    min_success: 1\n    per_runner_timeout: 1800\n    on_signal:\n      default: synthesize\n\n  synthesize:\n    runner:\n      executor: shell\n      command: |\n        {\n          for f in \"$ROUND_DIR\"/*.md; do\n            [ -f \"$f\" ] || continue\n            name=$(basename \"$f\" .md)\n            case \"$name\" in\n              synthesized|question) continue ;;\n            esac\n            echo \"# $name\"\n            echo\n            cat \"$f\"\n            echo\n            echo \"---\"\n            echo\n          done\n        } > \"$ROUND_DIR/synthesized.md\"\n        echo \"synthesized -> $ROUND_DIR/synthesized.md\"\n    on_signal:\n      default: __succeed__\npilot/pilot/defaults/plan/pipeline.yaml\n=== pilot/pilot/defaults/plan/pipeline.yaml\n# Plan pipeline — PRD → epics → tasks, one epic per round\n# Setup: create_epics (once)\n# Loop: no tasks left — done\n      failed: __fail__      # fatal error\n      default: __fail__         # retry\n\n  # Implement the task (codex primary, claude fallback)\n  implement:\n    prompt: |\n      {{file:prompts/implement.md}}\n    runner:\n      executor: codex\n      model: gpt-5.4\n    fallback_runner:\n      executor: claude-code\n      model: sonnet\n    on_signal:\n      failed: __fail__      # checks pass — send to reviewer\n      rejected: fix         # checks fail — note added, send to fix\n      failed: __fail__      # fatal error\n      default: review       # no signal — retry\n\n  # Code review — approve or reject\n  review:\n    prompt: |\n      {{file:prompts/review.md}}\n    runner:\n      executor: claude-code\n      model: opus\n    on_signal:\n      approved: merge       # pass — squash merge\n      rejected: fix         # fail — send back for fixes\n      stuck: escalate       # Catch-22 detected — escalate to chairman\n      failed: __fail__      # agent failed\n      default: review       # no signal — retry review\n\n  # Squash merge feature branch, close task, clean up\n  merge:\n    runner:\n      executor: shell\n      command: |\n        {{file:scripts/merge.sh}}\n    on_signal:\n      failed: __fail__      # contradictions detected — escalate to chairman\n      failed: __fail__      # agent failed\n      default: verify       # always back through verify gate\n\n  # Escalation — \"chairman\" resolves stuck fix/review loops\n  escalate:\n    prompt: |\n      {{file:prompts/escalate.md}}\n    runner:\n      executor: claude-code\n      model: opus\n    on_signal:\n      approve: merge        #
