{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01ETtEh7UDUzL24N7A7Jb84V\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"1\\t# medulla\\n2\\t\\n3\\t**medulla is a state machine for AI agents.** You describe work as a YAML pipeline — a graph of **nodes** — and medulla runs each node's action on a shell command or an agent harness (`claude-code`, `codex`, `opencode`, `agy`). Bodies emit **signals**; signals route the graph. Built in: retries, fallback to another model, pools with a success threshold, and crash-safe resume.\\n4\\t\\n5\\t```\\n6\\tnode  = action (shell | agent) [× inputs] → signals → next node\\n7\\tgraph = nodes + on_signal edges + two terminals (__exit_ok__ / __exit_fail__)\\n8\\t```\\n9\\t\\n10\\t**One machine.** Internally every node is a pool: a node without `inputs:` runs over a single phantom input. Policy (timeouts, attempts, fallback) works identically in both modes. The only thing `inputs:` switches is the **signal contract**: without inputs the body talks to the graph (decision node); with inputs the body writes to the manifest and only the join result routes (pool node).\\n11\\t\\n12\\t> **Status**: v2 contract (frozen), engine implemented — the v1 engine is **deleted**; v1 pipelines are not supported. `version: \\\"2\\\"` is required in every pipeline; the engine rejects anything else with a pointer to the migration table below. Implementation phases: **1** — engine (this document), **2** — harness sessions & run history polish.\\n13\\t\\n14\\t## Usage\\n15\\t\\n16\\t```bash\\n17\\tmedulla -w .medulla/pipelines/<name>              # run a pipeline\\n18\\tmedulla -w ... --var KEY=VALUE                    # set/override vars\\n19\\tmedulla -w ... --node <name>                      # dev: start from a specific node\\n20\\tmedulla -w ... --resume                           # continue the latest unfinished run\\n21\\tmedulla -w ... --run <dir>                        # continue a specific run\\n22\\tmedulla --docker -w ...                           # run inside the pipeline's Docker image\\n23\\t```\\n24\\t\\n25\\t## Example\\n26\\t\\n27\\t```yaml\\n28\\tversion: \\\"2\\\"\\n29\\tstart: triage\\n30\\ttimeout: 86400                # whole-run deadline (default 24h; 0 = unlimited)\\n31\\tvars: {BRANCH: main}\\n32\\t\\n33\\tdefaults:                     # policy defaults for every action (\\\"field absent → take from here\\\")\\n34\\t  timeout: 1800\\n35\\t  fallback: {agent: {harness: claude-code, model: opus}}\\n36\\t\\n37\\tnodes:\\n38\\t\\n39\\t  # decision node: body signals route the graph\\n40\\t  triage:\\n41\\t    shell: |\\n42\\t      git worktree prune; rm -f .locks/*        # crash-only: clean up on entry\\n43\\t      n=$(rg -l \\\"FIXME\\\" src/ | wc -l | tr -d ' ')\\n44\\t      [ \\\"$n\\\" -gt 0 ] && echo \\\"<signal:found>ok</signal:found>\\\" || echo \\\"<signal:clean>ok</signal:clean>\\\"\\n45\\t    timeout: 60\\n46\\t    on_signal: {found: plan, clean: __exit_ok__}\\n47\\t\\n48\\t  plan:\\n49\\t    agent: {harness: codex, model: gpt-5.5, effort: xhigh}\\n50\\t    prompt: |\\n51\\t      {{file:prompts/plan.md}}\\n52\\t      Branch: {{var:BRANCH}}. Write the plan to plan.md and emit signal planned.\\n53\\t    max_attempts: 2\\n54\\t    fallback: {agent: {harness: claude-code, model: sonnet}}\\n55\\t    on_signal:\\n56\\t      planned: panel\\n57\\t      __failed__: __exit_fail__    # body died (rc != 0 after attempts+fallback)\\n58\\t\\n59\\t  # pool node: inputs switch the contract — the join routes, bodies write to the manifest\\n60\\t  panel:\\n61\\t    inputs:\\n62\\t      - {slug: gpt5,   harness: codex,       model: gpt-5.5, effort: xhigh}\\n63\\t      - {slug: sonnet, harness: claude-code, model: sonnet}\\n64\\t      - {slug: gem,    harness: agy,         model: \\\"Gemini 3.1 Pro (High)\\\"}\\n65\\t    max_parallel: all\\n66\\t    min_success: 1\\n67\\t    agent: {harness: \\\"{{input.harness}}\\\", model: \\\"{{input.model}}\\\", effort: \\\"{{input.effort:-}}\\\"}\\n68\\t    prompt: |\\n69\\t      You are panelist {{input.slug}} ({{input_index}}/{{input_count}}).\\n70\\t      Critique the plan: {{file:plan.md}}\\n71\\t      Write your verdict to reviews/{{input.slug}}.md\\n72\\t    post: 'test -s \\\"reviews/{{input.slug}}.md\\\"'   # verdict = artifact exists, not agent mood\\n73\\t    on_signal: {__done__: apply}\\n74\\t\\n75\\t  apply:\\n76\\t    inputs: {shell: \\\"python3 scripts/tickets.py --json\\\", timeout: 60}\\n77\\t    max_parallel: 3\\n78\\t    min_success: 2\\n79\\t    shell: |\\n80\\t      bash scripts/fix_one.sh \\\"$MEDULLA_INPUT_ID\\\" \\\"$MEDULLA_INPUT_TITLE\\\"\\n81\\t    max_attempts: 2\\n82\\t    on_signal:\\n83\\t      __done__: report\\n84\\t      __empty__: report        # empty queue is legal here — route it explicitly\\n85\\t\\n86\\t  report:\\n87\\t    shell: |\\n88\\t      ok=$(jq -s 'map(select(.ok))|length' \\\"$MEDULLA_MANIFEST_APPLY\\\")\\n89\\t      total=$(jq -s length \\\"$MEDULLA_MANIFEST_APPLY\\\")\\n90\\t      [ \\\"$ok\\\" -eq \\\"$total\\\" ] && echo \\\"<signal:ready>ok</signal:ready>\\\" || echo \\\"<signal:rework>ok</signal:rework>\\\"\\n91\\t    on_signal: {ready: __exit_ok__, rework: plan}\\n92\\t```\\n93\\t\\n94\\t## Reference\\n95\\t\\n96\\t### Pipeline level\\n97\\t\\n98\\t| Field | Default | Meaning |\\n99\\t|---|---|---|\\n100\\t| `version` | required | must be `\\\"2\\\"` |\\n101\\t| `start` | required | first node |\\n102\\t| `vars` | `{}` | initial variables (also exported to env; see blacklist below) |\\n103\\t| `timeout` | 86400 | wall-clock deadline for the whole run; `0` = unlimited. The engine clamps every child timeout to the remaining budget |\\n104\\t| `defaults` | — | policy defaults for actions: `timeout`, `max_attempts`, `ignore_exit_code`, `fallback`, `on_signal` (per-key merge). Flat scalars only — never merged deep |\\n105\\t| `keep_runs` | 20 | auto-prune of run history on start |\\n106\\t\\n107\\t### Node = action [+ pool] + routing\\n108\\t\\n109\\tAction (exactly one of `shell` / `agent`):\\n110\\t\\n111\\t| Field | Meaning |\\n112\\t|---|---|\\n113\\t| `shell` | shell command; its config *is* the command. `prompt` here is a validation error |\\n114\\t| `agent` | `{harness, model, effort, args}` — one entity, one block. Scalar shortcut: `agent: codex` ≡ `agent: {harness: codex}`. `args` is a raw CLI escape hatch — non-portable across harnesses |\\n115\\t| `prompt` | agent input (not config). Never quote signal syntax literally in prompts — describe it (\\\"emit signal planned\\\"); the engine delivers the syntax to the agent |\\n116\\t| `timeout` | per **attempt**, seconds |\\n117\\t| `max_attempts` | attempts per runner, default 1. Primary gets N, then fallback gets N. Retryable outcomes: non-zero exit, timeout (recorded as rc 124), and **silence** — an agent body exiting 0 with no known signal (the most common agent flake: the work happened, the tag didn't). Silence retries on the **primary only** and never triggers fallback (another model drops the tag just as often; blind fallback duplicates side effects); after all attempts it classifies as `__default__`. Shell silence is deterministic and is not retried. **Silence handling is decision-node behavior**: in pools the body is not expected to signal — silence at rc 0 is the normal ok outcome (see the pool rows) |\\n118\\t| `fallback` | alternate agent action after primary attempts are exhausted. Agent-only (a fallback for shell is meaningless); a fallback has no fallback |\\n119\\t| `ignore_exit_code` | rc != 0 doesn't classify the body as failed; outcome comes from signals. **Forbidden in pool nodes** — `min_success` already owns that role |\\n120\\t\\n121\\tHooks around the body — `pre` / `post` (shell-only; rendered like everything else). These are not lifecycle hooks but **deterministic voices in the signal channel** — the only way to put shell before/after an agent body (you can't append a line to a prompt):\\n122\\t\\n123\\t| `pre` — once per node run, before the body renders | Effect |\\n124\\t|---|---|\\n125\\t| emits a routing signal | body and `post` are **skipped**, the signal routes (guard: \\\"already done / not needed\\\") |\\n126\\t| emits `var` | applied **before** the body renders — env prep, the prompt sees fresh vars |\\n127\\t| exits non-zero | `__failed__` (preparation broke), body never runs |\\n128\\t| exits 0, silent | normal path |\\n129\\t\\n130\\t| `post` — after **every** attempt, before signal resolution; sees `MEDULLA_BODY_RC` / `MEDULLA_BODY_SIGNAL` | Effect |\\n131\\t|---|---|\\n132\\t| exits non-zero | the **attempt failed** → body+post retry within `max_attempts`, then fallback (\\\"try until the artifact exists\\\": `post: test -s out.md`) |\\n133\\t| exits 0 + emits a signal | **overrides** the body's signal (the agent said planned, the plan is garbage → route needs_rework) |\\n134\\t| exits 0, silent | the body's outcome stands |\\n135\\t\\n136\\tIn pools both run per input: a `pre` guard skips already-done inputs (manifest `ok: true` with the guard's signal); a `post` check is the truth channel for agent inputs — CLIs exit 0 even when the model did nothing, so without `post` a pool's `min_success` counts air.\\n137\\t\\n138\\tPool (presence of `inputs` turns the action into a pool):\\n139\\t\\n140\\t| Field | Meaning |\\n141\\t|---|---|\\n142\\t| `inputs` | YAML list = **data** (scalars or objects, one kind per pool, arrays forbidden), or `{shell: \\\"cmd\\\", timeout: 60}` = **source**. Output sniffing by first byte: `[` JSON array, `{` JSON-lines, else plain lines. A bare string is a hard validation error (data vs code ambiguity). Hard cap: 10 000 inputs |\\n143\\t| `max_parallel` | pool cap: `1` (default, sequential) \\\\| N \\\\| `all` |\\n144\\t| `min_success` | join threshold: `all` (default) \\\\| N ≥ 1. Input ok = rc == 0. No short-circuiting ever — all inputs run (side effects have value) |\\n145\\t\\n146\\tRouting:\\n147\\t\\n148\\t| Field | Meaning |\\n149\\t|---|---|\\n150\\t| `on_signal` | map of signal → target. Targets are plain strings: a node name, `__exit_ok__`, `__exit_fail__` |\\n151\\t\\n152\\t### The reserved namespace — all 8 names\\n153\\t\\n154\\tLeft-side law: **a bare name is a signal your code emits; a dunder is an engine key.** Right-side law: **terminals are `__exit_*`.**\\n155\\t\\n156\\tRouting signals (left side):\\n157\\t\\n158\\t| Name | Emitted by | When | Built-in route |\\n159\\t|---|---|---|---|\\n160\\t| `__done__` | pool join | successes ≥ `min_success` | **none — must be routed explicitly** (it's a real edge) |\\n161\\t| `__failed__` | engine | decision node: body died after all attempts+fallback. Pool: join below threshold | `__exit_fail__` |\\n162\\t| `__empty__` | engine | source returned rc 0 and zero inputs (bodies never run) | `__exit_fail__` — loud by default; route explicitly where emptiness is legal |\\n163\\t| `__default__` | nobody (matcher) | body exited rc 0 with no known signal (\\\"said nothing\\\") | `__exit_fail__` |\\n164\\t\\n165\\tChannel signals (emitted in stdout, never route; reserved bare words — using them as `on_signal` keys is a validation error):\\n166\\t\\n167\\t| Name | Meaning |\\n168\\t|---|---|\\n169\\t| `var` | `<signal:var key=K>value</signal:var>` — set a variable (applied per the fold law) |\\n170\\t| `update` | `<signal:update>msg</signal:update>` — progress message |\\n171\\t\\n172\\tTerminal nodes (right side):\\n173\\t\\n174\\t| Name | Action |\\n175\\t|---|---|\\n176\\t| `__exit_ok__` | end the run, exit 0 |\\n177\\t| `__exit_fail__` | end the run, exit 2; the routing signal's body becomes the error message |\\n178\\t\\n179\\tSignals are read from **stdout only**; stderr goes verbatim to the attempt log (a traceback that happens to echo signal text must never route the graph). A known signal emitted before a non-zero exit wins over the exit code. Pool bodies' signals never route — they are recorded in the manifest (law of layers: inputs produce data, joins produce transitions). Bare `done`/`failed` on decision nodes are ordinary user signals with no special meaning. User nodes may not be named `__*__` or `on/off/yes/no/true/false` (YAML 1.1 boolean traps).\\n180\\t\\n181\\t### Templates & environment\\n182\\t\\n183\\t| Template | Meaning |\\n184\\t|---|---|\\n185\\t| `{{var:KEY}}`, `{{var:KEY:-default}}` | variable, with optional default |\\n186\\t| `{{file:path}}` | file inclusion, recursive (depth ≤ 10, exceeding = render error with the inclusion chain; missing file = error). Relative paths resolve against the **including file's** directory. Paths are static — no vars inside |\\n187\\t| `{{input}}` | the input; objects render as compact JSON |\\n188\\t| `{{input.a.b:-default}}` | dot-walk into object inputs. Missing field without a default = hard render error |\\n189\\t| `{{input_index}}` (1-based), `{{input_count}}` | position / total |\\n190\\t| `{{last.node}}`, `{{last.signal}}`, `{{last.message}}`, `{{last.rc}}` | outcome of the previously completed node — transient tokens (not vars: no persistence, no fold-law constraints). The bridge for agent-bodied handlers whose prompts can't read env |\\n191\\t\\n192\\tRender model: phase 1 — file inclusion; phase 2 — one simultaneous, **inert** pass of var/input substitution. **Files are code, values are data**: mustache inside included files resolves fully; mustache inside var/input *values* stays literal (injection-safe by construction). Every scalar field of an action is a template (that's why an ensemble is just a pool with per-input `harness`/`model`). A field rendering to an empty string counts as absent — for optional agent fields only; empty `shell`/`prompt`/`harness` is an error. Rendering happens **once per node run**, before attempt 1; retries reuse the same rendered text. A render error on a decision node (phantom input) is an engine crash (`E_RENDER`) — the template itself is broken. A render error for **one pool input** fails that input only (manifest row `reason: render` with the template path and input index) — one malformed row of producer data must not kill the other inputs.\\n193\\t\\n194\\tEnvironment (data should flow to shell via env, templates are for slugs/paths — quoting-safe):\\n195\\t\\n196\\t| Variable | Meaning |\\n197\\t|---|---|\\n198\\t| `MEDULLA_INPUT` | the input as JSON |\\n199\\t| `MEDULLA_INPUT_INDEX` / `MEDULLA_INPUT_COUNT` | position / total |\\n200\\t| `MEDULLA_INPUT_<KEY>` | each flat scalar field of an object input, uppercased |\\n201\\t| `MEDULLA_INPUT_KEY` / `MEDULLA_ATTEMPT_ID` | stable input identity `(index, hash)` / unique attempt id — the idempotency keys for bodies that mutate the outside world (branches, PRs, tickets) |\\n202\\t| `MEDULLA_LAST_NODE` / `_SIGNAL` / `_MESSAGE` / `_RC` | outcome of the previously completed node, published before every transition (after a pool, `_RC` is empty — a join has no single rc; read the manifest); `MEDULLA_LAST_EVENT_JSON` carries the same as one JSON object. Timeout is recognizable as rc 124 |\\n203\\t| `MEDULLA_MANIFEST_<NODE>` | path to a pool node's manifest (dashes → underscores) |\\n204\\t| `MEDULLA_RUN_ID` / `MEDULLA_RUN_DIR` | run id (settable from outside for correlation; else generated) / this run's directory. Put artifacts in `$MEDULLA_RUN_DIR/artifacts/` |\\n205\\t| `MEDULLA_TIMEOUT_S` | resolved step timeout, for CLIs that need to size their own |\\n206\\t\\n207\\tAll pipeline vars are exported to child processes. Reserved names (`PATH`, `HOME`, `SHELL`, `LD_*`, …) are rejected by the validator.\\n208\\t\\n209\\tVar-signal semantics (fold law): variables are mutable state, and state mutation requires ordering — var signals are **applied only at `max_parallel: 1`** (which includes every decision node), in input order, atomically from the **successful** attempt only. At `max_parallel > 1` they are recorded in the manifest instead.\\n210\\t\\n211\\t### Errors & exit codes\\n212\\t\\n213\\tTwo failure classes. The test: *can it be fixed by changing data/prompts/retrying?* → workflow failure, the graph decides. *Does it require editing the pipeline/environment?* → engine crash.\\n214\\t\\n215\\t| Exit | Class | Meaning |\\n216\\t|---|---|---|\\n217\\t| 0 | — | `__exit_ok__` |\\n218\\t| 1 | engine crash | the pipeline itself is broken — fix the pipeline, retrying is pointless |\\n219\\t| 2 | workflow failure | the graph routed to `__exit_fail__` — fix the task/inputs |\\n220\\t| 130 | interrupt | — |\\n221\\t\\n222\\tA fixed delay separates attempts and the fallback switch (`MEDULLA_RETRY_DELAY_S`, default 2s — retry storms hit provider rate limits).\\n223\\t\\n224\\tCrash codes: `E_VALIDATION` (schema/XOR/unknown target/boolean node names/bare keys in pool routing/defaults-inherited self-edges), `E_RENDER` (broken decision-node template: missing file, depth > 10, empty required render), `E_DEADLINE` (pipeline timeout), `E_INPUTS` (source exited non-zero, or emitted mixed-kind/array elements — a broken producer is not an empty queue), `E_INPUTS_LIMIT` (> 10k), `E_HARNESS`, `E_INTERNAL`.\\n225\\t\\n226\\t`E_HARNESS` is razor-thin by design: **only** \\\"harness binary missing / unresolvable\\\". An agent process dying unexpectedly (OOM, segfault, API hiccup) is always a non-zero exit — class B, retryable. If that boundary drifts, transient flakes start crashing whole runs.\\n227\\t\\n228\\t#### Handling errors in the graph\\n229\\t\\n230\\tHandlers are **ordinary nodes routed before the terminal** — never a body on the terminal (`kill -9` makes exit hooks an illusion; a node has a timeout, attempts and its own log). The error payload arrives through the same channels as all data: `$MEDULLA_LAST_*` for shell, `{{last.*}}` for prompts. Global catch-all is three lines of `defaults`:\\n231\\t\\n232\\t```yaml\\n233\\tdefaults:\\n234\\t  on_signal: {__failed__: notify, __default__: notify}   # root supervisor\\n235\\t\\n236\\tnodes:\\n237\\t  notify:\\n238\\t    shell: 'curl -s \\\"$HOOK\\\" -d \\\"text=[medulla] $MEDULLA_LAST_NODE ($MEDULLA_LAST_SIGNAL): $MEDULLA_LAST_MESSAGE\\\"'\\n239\\t    timeout: 30\\n240\\t    on_signal: {__default__: __exit_fail__, __failed__: __exit_fail__}   # own dunders explicit!\\n241\\t```\\n242\\t\\n243\\tA node-level edge beats the defaults catch-all, which beats built-ins — a three-tier supervision chain. The validator rejects a defaults-inherited edge that points at its own node (`notify` failing into `notify` would loop); explicit self-loops remain legal (retry pattern). A pool's `__failed__` message is pre-aggregated: `\\\"2/5 inputs ok (min_success=3); rc!=0 x2, timeout x1, render x0\\\"` — the manifest has the rest. Class A is not catchable in the graph by definition: the graph itself is what's broken.\\n244\\t\\n245\\tThe error **message is the body of the signal** that routed to `__exit_fail__` (engine facts carry their own: `__failed__` → rc/join stats, `__empty__` → \\\"source returned 0 inputs\\\", `__default__` → tail of stdout). Every run ends with an atomic `outcome.json`:\\n246\\t\\n247\\t```json\\n248\\t{\\\"outcome\\\": \\\"failed\\\", \\\"exit_code\\\": 2,\\n249\\t \\\"error\\\": {\\\"code\\\": \\\"SIGNAL_FAIL\\\", \\\"message\\\": \\\"no repo access: git clone rc=128\\\",\\n250\\t           \\\"node\\\": \\\"plan\\\", \\\"step\\\": 12, \\\"signal\\\": \\\"blocked\\\"},\\n251\\t \\\"steps\\\": 12, \\\"duration_s\\\": 4180}\\n252\\t```\\n253\\t\\n254\\t### Lifecycle\\n255\\t\\n256\\t**Boot** — parse CLI → load yaml (`version: \\\"2\\\"` or a friendly migration error) → validate everything (`E_VALIDATION` before any run dir exists) → create `runs/<ts>-<run_id>/`, snapshot the config, seed vars, prune old runs → set the deadline.\\n257\\t\\n258\\t**Node loop** (single program counter) — per node: deadline check → materialize inputs (no key → phantom; list → snapshot into the step dir; source → render, execute, sniff, check) → run the pool (`max_parallel` workers; per input: render once → attempts → fallback; stream stdout to the step log with realtime signal extraction) → classify (decision: signal | `__failed__` | `__default__`; pool: `__done__` | `__failed__` | `__empty__`) → resolve the edge (node `on_signal` → `defaults.on_signal` → built-ins) → append the journal row → next node or terminal.\\n259\\t\\n260\\t**Finish** — atomic `outcome.json`, exit 0/2; crashes write `outcome.json` with the `E_*` code and exit 1; SIGINT → best-effort outcome, exit 130.\\n261\\t\\n262\\t**Resume** — pick the latest run without `outcome.json`, or with `outcome: interrupted` **or `crashed`** (deliberate extension: the #1 resume trigger is the `E_DEADLINE` crash; config-class crashes simply crash again identically) — or `--run <dir>`: reload the **snapshot** config (a run's config is immutable), vars, journal position; an interrupted pool continues from the manifest done-mask (input identity = `(index, hash)`; sources are never re-executed on resume); an interrupted decision node re-runs whole (body idempotence is the author's concern). The deadline is fresh per invocation.\\n263\\t\\n264\\t### Layout\\n265\\t\\n266\\tA pipeline is a self-contained directory — contract, code, history:\\n267\\t\\n268\\t```\\n269\\t.medulla/pipelines/<name>/\\n270\\t  pipeline.yaml            # the contract\\n271\\t  prompts/  scripts/       # its code\\n272\\t  harness/                 # phase 2: harness HOME (sessions), shared by all runs\\n273\\t  runs/<ts>-<run_id>/      # one directory per run\\n274\\t    pipeline.yaml          # config snapshot as loaded (immutable for the run)\\n275\\t    journal.jsonl          # graph chronology, append-only (step, node, rc, signal, message, duration)\\n276\\t    vars.yaml              # variables (updated on var signals)\\n277\\t    outcome.json           # written only on completion (atomic); absent = running or hard-killed\\n278\\t    steps/\\n279\\t      001-triage/                        # every step gets a directory\\n280\\t      002-plan/prompt.md                 # rendered agent input (what the agent actually saw)\\n281\\t      002-plan/attempt-1-codex.txt       # raw CLI stream per attempt\\n282\\t      004-apply/inputs.json              # inputs snapshot (resume)\\n283\\t      004-apply/input-2-sonnet.txt       # per-input logs\\n284\\t      004-apply/manifest.jsonl           # {index, key, input, ok, reason, signal, message,\\n285\\t                                         #  rc, timed_out, attempts, fallback, harness, model,\\n286\\t                                         #  vars, updates, duration_s, log}\\n287\\t```\\n288\\t\\n289\\tRetention: on start, keep the newest `keep_runs` finished runs; directories without `outcome.json` younger than the pipeline timeout are never pruned. History browsing needs no CLI: `ls runs/`, `cat outcome.json`.\\n290\\t\\n291\\t**Phase 2 (designed, not in phase 1):** pipeline-scoped harness sessions — `harness/` as the container HOME, session ids captured into journal/manifest from the CLI streams the engine already parses, and a `resume:` field on the agent block to continue a node's last successful session (current run first, then previous runs, newest first). Docker: everything lives under `/workspace` (already mounted RW); credentials are copied once from the read-only `/mnt/*` mounts.\\n292\\t\\n293\\t### Canonical patterns\\n294\\t\\n295\\t- **Budget gate** (bounded rework — the engine has no per-node visit caps; cycle semantics belong to the workflow):\\n296\\t  ```yaml\\n297\\t  gate:\\n298\\t    shell: |\\n299\\t      n=$(( ${PLAN_TRIES:-0} + 1 ))\\n300\\t      echo \\\"<signal:var key=PLAN_TRIES>$n</signal:var>\\\"\\n301\\t      [ \\\"$n\\\" -le 3 ] && echo \\\"<signal:go>ok</signal:go>\\\" || echo \\\"<signal:budget_out>ok</signal:budget_out>\\\"\\n302\\t    on_signal: {go: plan, budget_out: escalate}\\n303\\t  ```\\n304\\t- **Crawler**: producer node → pool → probe node → edge back; the source re-runs on each new node run.\\n305\\t- **Heterogeneous pool**: inputs carry `role`/`prompt` fields; the node prompt is the template (code, vars resolve), input fragments are inserted inert.\\n306\\t- **Entry cleanup** (crash-only): there is no `finally`; exit hooks are an illusion under `kill -9`. Clean stale state idempotently at the start of the node/run that needs it clean.\\n307\\t- **Parallel tickets**: the engine guarantees vars isolation; file/git isolation is the body's job (`git worktree` per input) and disjointness is the producer script's contract.\\n308\\t- **DLQ (repair node)**: failed pool inputs are structured data — re-enqueue only them, never the whole pool:\\n309\\t  ```yaml\\n310\\t  retry-failed:\\n311\\t    inputs: {shell: \\\"jq -c 'select(.ok|not) | .input' $MEDULLA_MANIFEST_APPLY\\\"}\\n312\\t    max_parallel: 2\\n313\\t    shell: 'bash scripts/fix_one.sh \\\"$MEDULLA_INPUT_ID\\\"'\\n314\\t    on_signal: {__done__: report, __empty__: report}\\n315\\t  ```\\n316\\t\\n317\\t### Migrating from v1\\n318\\t\\n319\\tThe v1 engine is removed; this table is the dictionary for porting old pipelines.\\n320\\t\\n321\\t| v1 | v2 |\\n322\\t|---|---|\\n323\\t| `stages` / `starting` | `nodes` / `start` |\\n324\\t| `runner:` / `llm:` / `executor`+`command` | action fields directly on the node: `agent: {harness, model, effort, args}` xor `shell` |\\n325\\t| `loop:` + `list:` + `fetch:` + `parallel: true` | `inputs:` on the node + `max_parallel` |\\n326\\t| `done: __next_item__` + `loop_done` | pool joins route `__done__` / `__failed__` / `__empty__` via `min_success` |\\n327\\t| `max_iterations` / `reset_iterations` / `on_max` | removed — budget gate pattern (vars) |\\n328\\t| hardcoded `max_rounds=500` | pipeline `timeout` (wall-clock deadline) |\\n329\\t| `round_timeout` / `fallback_runner` | `defaults: {timeout, fallback}` |\\n330\\t| `__exit__` | `__exit_ok__` / `__exit_fail__` |\\n331\\t| `ignore_rc` | `ignore_exit_code` (decision nodes only) |\\n332\\t| `{{__item__}}` / `{{__list_item__}}` | `{{input}}` family + `MEDULLA_INPUT_*` env |\\n333\\t| `MEDULLA_TASK_ID` | `MEDULLA_RUN_ID` |\\n334\\t| `.medulla/vars.<task>.yaml` | `.medulla/pipelines/<name>/runs/<id>/` |\\n335\\t| `--stage` | `--node` |\\n336\\t| gemini executor | removed (use `agy`) |\\n337\\t| exit codes 0/1/2/3 ad-hoc | 0 = ok, 1 = engine crash, 2 = workflow fail, 130 = interrupt |\\n338\\t\\n339\\t### Reserved (designed, not implemented — in priority order)\\n340\\t\\n341\\t`stall_timeout` (no-stdout watchdog for hung agents; partially covered today by per-harness flags — codex `stream_idle_timeout_ms`, agy `--print-timeout`) · manifest attempt states (`claimed|started|completed`) + per-node resume policy `rerun|skip|probe` (exactly-once is impossible; make duplicate side effects detectable) · `resume:` (continue a node's session — phase 2) · `cancel_rest` (race joins) · `on_input_fail: abort` (fail-fast pools) · `format:` on sources (override sniffing) · `finally` (best-effort only, if reality ever demands it) · agent-block defaults.\\n342\\t\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"31358358-0e02-4f11-9160-c99122fc9ba8\",\"uuid\":\"82a66dee-cdc1-46dd-85da-d7c2ba2b45bb\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-07-15T11:04:01.634Z\",\"tool_use_result\":{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"/Users/ako/Work/ai/pilot-wip/medulla/README.md\",\"content\":\"# medulla\\n\\n**medulla is a state machine for AI agents.** You describe work as a YAML pipeline — a graph of **nodes** — and medulla runs each node's action on a shell command or an agent harness (`claude-code`, `codex`, `opencode`, `agy`). Bodies emit **signals**; signals route the graph. Built in: retries, fallback to another model, pools with a success threshold, and crash-safe resume.\\n\\n```\\nnode  = action (shell | agent) [× inputs] → signals → next node\\ngraph = nodes + on_signal edges + two terminals (__exit_ok__ / __exit_fail__)\\n```\\n\\n**One machine.** Internally every node is a pool: a node without `inputs:` runs over a single phantom input. Policy (timeouts, attempts, fallback) works identically in both modes. The only thing `inputs:` switches is the **signal contract**: without inputs the body talks to the graph (decision node); with inputs the body writes to the manifest and only the join result routes (pool node).\\n\\n> **Status**: v2 contract (frozen), engine implemented — the v1 engine is **deleted**; v1 pipelines are not supported. `version: \\\"2\\\"` is required in every pipeline; the engine rejects anything else with a pointer to the migration table below. Implementation phases: **1** — engine (this document), **2** — harness sessions & run history polish.\\n\\n## Usage\\n\\n```bash\\nmedulla -w .medulla/pipelines/<name>              # run a pipeline\\nmedulla -w ... --var KEY=VALUE                    # set/override vars\\nmedulla -w ... --node <name>                      # dev: start from a specific node\\nmedulla -w ... --resume                           # continue the latest unfinished run\\nmedulla -w ... --run <dir>                        # continue a specific run\\nmedulla --docker -w ...                           # run inside the pipeline's Docker image\\n```\\n\\n## Example\\n\\n```yaml\\nversion: \\\"2\\\"\\nstart: triage\\ntimeout: 86400                # whole-run deadline (default 24h; 0 = unlimited)\\nvars: {BRANCH: main}\\n\\ndefaults:                     # policy defaults for every action (\\\"field absent → take from here\\\")\\n  timeout: 1800\\n  fallback: {agent: {harness: claude-code, model: opus}}\\n\\nnodes:\\n\\n  # decision node: body signals route the graph\\n  triage:\\n    shell: |\\n      git worktree prune; rm -f .locks/*        # crash-only: clean up on entry\\n      n=$(rg -l \\\"FIXME\\\" src/ | wc -l | tr -d ' ')\\n      [ \\\"$n\\\" -gt 0 ] && echo \\\"<signal:found>ok</signal:found>\\\" || echo \\\"<signal:clean>ok</signal:clean>\\\"\\n    timeout: 60\\n    on_signal: {found: plan, clean: __exit_ok__}\\n\\n  plan:\\n    agent: {harness: codex, model: gpt-5.5, effort: xhigh}\\n    prompt: |\\n      {{file:prompts/plan.md}}\\n      Branch: {{var:BRANCH}}. Write the plan to plan.md and emit signal planned.\\n    max_attempts: 2\\n    fallback: {agent: {harness: claude-code, model: sonnet}}\\n    on_signal:\\n      planned: panel\\n      __failed__: __exit_fail__    # body died (rc != 0 after attempts+fallback)\\n\\n  # pool node: inputs switch the contract — the join routes, bodies write to the manifest\\n  panel:\\n    inputs:\\n      - {slug: gpt5,   harness: codex,       model: gpt-5.5, effort: xhigh}\\n      - {slug: sonnet, harness: claude-code, model: sonnet}\\n      - {slug: gem,    harness: agy,         model: \\\"Gemini 3.1 Pro (High)\\\"}\\n    max_parallel: all\\n    min_success: 1\\n    agent: {harness: \\\"{{input.harness}}\\\", model: \\\"{{input.model}}\\\", effort: \\\"{{input.effort:-}}\\\"}\\n    prompt: |\\n      You are panelist {{input.slug}} ({{input_index}}/{{input_count}}).\\n      Critique the plan: {{file:plan.md}}\\n      Write your verdict to reviews/{{input.slug}}.md\\n    post: 'test -s \\\"reviews/{{input.slug}}.md\\\"'   # verdict = artifact exists, not agent mood\\n    on_signal: {__done__: apply}\\n\\n  apply:\\n    inputs: {shell: \\\"python3 scripts/tickets.py --json\\\", timeout: 60}\\n    max_parallel: 3\\n    min_success: 2\\n    shell: |\\n      bash scripts/fix_one.sh \\\"$MEDULLA_INPUT_ID\\\" \\\"$MEDULLA_INPUT_TITLE\\\"\\n    max_attempts: 2\\n    on_signal:\\n      __done__: report\\n      __empty__: report        # empty queue is legal here — route it explicitly\\n\\n  report:\\n    shell: |\\n      ok=$(jq -s 'map(select(.ok))|length' \\\"$MEDULLA_MANIFEST_APPLY\\\")\\n      total=$(jq -s length \\\"$MEDULLA_MANIFEST_APPLY\\\")\\n      [ \\\"$ok\\\" -eq \\\"$total\\\" ] && echo \\\"<signal:ready>ok</signal:ready>\\\" || echo \\\"<signal:rework>ok</signal:rework>\\\"\\n    on_signal: {ready: __exit_ok__, rework: plan}\\n```\\n\\n## Reference\\n\\n### Pipeline level\\n\\n| Field | Default | Meaning |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `version` | required | must be `\\\"2\\\"` |\\n| `start` | required | first node |\\n| `vars` | `{}` | initial variables (also exported to env; see blacklist below) |\\n| `timeout` | 86400 | wall-clock deadline for the whole run; `0` = unlimited. The engine clamps every child timeout to the remaining budget |\\n| `defaults` | — | policy defaults for actions: `timeout`, `max_attempts`, `ignore_exit_code`, `fallback`, `on_signal` (per-key merge). Flat scalars only — never merged deep |\\n| `keep_runs` | 20 | auto-prune of run history on start |\\n\\n### Node = action [+ pool] + routing\\n\\nAction (exactly one of `shell` / `agent`):\\n\\n| Field | Meaning |\\n|---|---|\\n| `shell` | shell command; its config *is* the command. `prompt` here is a validation error |\\n| `agent` | `{harness, model, effort, args}` — one entity, one block. Scalar shortcut: `agent: codex` ≡ `agent: {harness: codex}`. `args` is a raw CLI escape hatch — non-portable across harnesses |\\n| `prompt` | agent input (not config). Never quote signal syntax literally in prompts — describe it (\\\"emit signal planned\\\"); the engine delivers the syntax to the agent |\\n| `timeout` | per **attempt**, seconds |\\n| `max_attempts` | attempts per runner, default 1. Primary gets N, then fallback gets N. Retryable outcomes: non-zero exit, timeout (recorded as rc 124), and **silence** — an agent body exiting 0 with no known signal (the most common agent flake: the work happened, the tag didn't). Silence retries on the **primary only** and never triggers fallback (another model drops the tag just as often; blind fallback duplicates side effects); after all attempts it classifies as `__default__`. Shell silence is deterministic and is not retried. **Silence handling is decision-node behavior**: in pools the body is not expected to signal — silence at rc 0 is the normal ok outcome (see the pool rows) |\\n| `fallback` | alternate agent action after primary attempts are exhausted. Agent-only (a fallback for shell is meaningless); a fallback has no fallback |\\n| `ignore_exit_code` | rc != 0 doesn't classify the body as failed; outcome comes from signals. **Forbidden in pool nodes** — `min_success` already owns that role |\\n\\nHooks around the body — `pre` / `post` (shell-only; rendered like everything else). These are not lifecycle hooks but **deterministic voices in the signal channel** — the only way to put shell before/after an agent body (you can't append a line to a prompt):\\n\\n| `pre` — once per node run, before the body renders | Effect |\\n|---|---|\\n| emits a routing signal | body and `post` are **skipped**, the signal routes (guard: \\\"already done / not needed\\\") |\\n| emits `var` | applied **before** the body renders — env prep, the prompt sees fresh vars |\\n| exits non-zero | `__failed__` (preparation broke), body never runs |\\n| exits 0, silent | normal path |\\n\\n| `post` — after **every** attempt, before signal resolution; sees `MEDULLA_BODY_RC` / `MEDULLA_BODY_SIGNAL` | Effect |\\n|---|---|\\n| exits non-zero | the **attempt failed** → body+post retry within `max_attempts`, then fallback (\\\"try until the artifact exists\\\": `post: test -s out.md`) |\\n| exits 0 + emits a signal | **overrides** the body's signal (the agent said planned, the plan is garbage → route needs_rework) |\\n| exits 0, silent | the body's outcome stands |\\n\\nIn pools both run per input: a `pre` guard skips already-done inputs (manifest `ok: true` with the guard's signal); a `post` check is the truth channel for agent inputs — CLIs exit 0 even when the model did nothing, so without `post` a pool's `min_success` counts air.\\n\\nPool (presence of `inputs` turns the action into a pool):\\n\\n| Field | Meaning |\\n|---|---|\\n| `inputs` | YAML list = **data** (scalars or objects, one kind per pool, arrays forbidden), or `{shell: \\\"cmd\\\", timeout: 60}` = **source**. Output sniffing by first byte: `[` JSON array, `{` JSON-lines, else plain lines. A bare string is a hard validation error (data vs code ambiguity). Hard cap: 10 000 inputs |\\n| `max_parallel` | pool cap: `1` (default, sequential) \\\\| N \\\\| `all` |\\n| `min_success` | join threshold: `all` (default) \\\\| N ≥ 1. Input ok = rc == 0. No short-circuiting ever — all inputs run (side effects have value) |\\n\\nRouting:\\n\\n| Field | Meaning |\\n|---|---|\\n| `on_signal` | map of signal → target. Targets are plain strings: a node name, `__exit_ok__`, `__exit_fail__` |\\n\\n### The reserved namespace — all 8 names\\n\\nLeft-side law: **a bare name is a signal your code emits; a dunder is an engine key.** Right-side law: **terminals are `__exit_*`.**\\n\\nRouting signals (left side):\\n\\n| Name | Emitted by | When | Built-in route |\\n|---|---|---|---|\\n| `__done__` | pool join | successes ≥ `min_success` | **none — must be routed explicitly** (it's a real edge) |\\n| `__failed__` | engine | decision node: body died after all attempts+fallback. Pool: join below threshold | `__exit_fail__` |\\n| `__empty__` | engine | source returned rc 0 and zero inputs (bodies never run) | `__exit_fail__` — loud by default; route explicitly where emptiness is legal |\\n| `__default__` | nobody (matcher) | body exited rc 0 with no known signal (\\\"said nothing\\\") | `__exit_fail__` |\\n\\nChannel signals (emitted in stdout, never route; reserved bare words — using them as `on_signal` keys is a validation error):\\n\\n| Name | Meaning |\\n|---|---|\\n| `var` | `<signal:var key=K>value</signal:var>` — set a variable (applied per the fold law) |\\n| `update` | `<signal:update>msg</signal:update>` — progress message |\\n\\nTerminal nodes (right side):\\n\\n| Name | Action |\\n|---|---|\\n| `__exit_ok__` | end the run, exit 0 |\\n| `__exit_fail__` | end the run, exit 2; the routing signal's body becomes the error message |\\n\\nSignals are read from **stdout only**; stderr goes verbatim to the attempt log (a traceback that happens to echo signal text must never route the graph). A known signal emitted before a non-zero exit wins over the exit code. Pool bodies' signals never route — they are recorded in the manifest (law of layers: inputs produce data, joins produce transitions). Bare `done`/`failed` on decision nodes are ordinary user signals with no special meaning. User nodes may not be named `__*__` or `on/off/yes/no/true/false` (YAML 1.1 boolean traps).\\n\\n### Templates & environment\\n\\n| Template | Meaning |\\n|---|---|\\n| `{{var:KEY}}`, `{{var:KEY:-default}}` | variable, with optional default |\\n| `{{file:path}}` | file inclusion, recursive (depth ≤ 10, exceeding = render error with the inclusion chain; missing file = error). Relative paths resolve against the **including file's** directory. Paths are static — no vars inside |\\n| `{{input}}` | the input; objects render as compact JSON |\\n| `{{input.a.b:-default}}` | dot-walk into object inputs. Missing field without a default = hard render error |\\n| `{{input_index}}` (1-based), `{{input_count}}` | position / total |\\n| `{{last.node}}`, `{{last.signal}}`, `{{last.message}}`, `{{last.rc}}` | outcome of the previously completed node — transient tokens (not vars: no persistence, no fold-law constraints). The bridge for agent-bodied handlers whose prompts can't read env |\\n\\nRender model: phase 1 — file inclusion; phase 2 — one simultaneous, **inert** pass of var/input substitution. **Files are code, values are data**: mustache inside included files resolves fully; mustache inside var/input *values* stays literal (injection-safe by construction). Every scalar field of an action is a template (that's why an ensemble is just a pool with per-input `harness`/`model`). A field rendering to an empty string counts as absent — for optional agent fields only; empty `shell`/`prompt`/`harness` is an error. Rendering happens **once per node run**, before attempt 1; retries reuse the same rendered text. A render error on a decision node (phantom input) is an engine crash (`E_RENDER`) — the template itself is broken. A render error for **one pool input** fails that input only (manifest row `reason: render` with the template path and input index) — one malformed row of producer data must not kill the other inputs.\\n\\nEnvironment (data should flow to shell via env, templates are for slugs/paths — quoting-safe):\\n\\n| Variable | Meaning |\\n|---|---|\\n| `MEDULLA_INPUT` | the input as JSON |\\n| `MEDULLA_INPUT_INDEX` / `MEDULLA_INPUT_COUNT` | position / total |\\n| `MEDULLA_INPUT_<KEY>` | each flat scalar field of an object input, uppercased |\\n| `MEDULLA_INPUT_KEY` / `MEDULLA_ATTEMPT_ID` | stable input identity `(index, hash)` / unique attempt id — the idempotency keys for bodies that mutate the outside world (branches, PRs, tickets) |\\n| `MEDULLA_LAST_NODE` / `_SIGNAL` / `_MESSAGE` / `_RC` | outcome of the previously completed node, published before every transition (after a pool, `_RC` is empty — a join has no single rc; read the manifest); `MEDULLA_LAST_EVENT_JSON` carries the same as one JSON object. Timeout is recognizable as rc 124 |\\n| `MEDULLA_MANIFEST_<NODE>` | path to a pool node's manifest (dashes → underscores) |\\n| `MEDULLA_RUN_ID` / `MEDULLA_RUN_DIR` | run id (settable from outside for correlation; else generated) / this run's directory. Put artifacts in `$MEDULLA_RUN_DIR/artifacts/` |\\n| `MEDULLA_TIMEOUT_S` | resolved step timeout, for CLIs that need to size their own |\\n\\nAll pipeline vars are exported to child processes. Reserved names (`PATH`, `HOME`, `SHELL`, `LD_*`, …) are rejected by the validator.\\n\\nVar-signal semantics (fold law): variables are mutable state, and state mutation requires ordering — var signals are **applied only at `max_parallel: 1`** (which includes every decision node), in input order, atomically from the **successful** attempt only. At `max_parallel > 1` they are recorded in the manifest instead.\\n\\n### Errors & exit codes\\n\\nTwo failure classes. The test: *can it be fixed by changing data/prompts/retrying?* → workflow failure, the graph decides. *Does it require editing the pipeline/environment?* → engine crash.\\n\\n| Exit | Class | Meaning |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| 0 | — | `__exit_ok__` |\\n| 1 | engine crash | the pipeline itself is broken — fix the pipeline, retrying is pointless |\\n| 2 | workflow failure | the graph routed to `__exit_fail__` — fix the task/inputs |\\n| 130 | interrupt | — |\\n\\nA fixed delay separates attempts and the fallback switch (`MEDULLA_RETRY_DELAY_S`, default 2s — retry storms hit provider rate limits).\\n\\nCrash codes: `E_VALIDATION` (schema/XOR/unknown target/boolean node names/bare keys in pool routing/defaults-inherited self-edges), `E_RENDER` (broken decision-node template: missing file, depth > 10, empty required render), `E_DEADLINE` (pipeline timeout), `E_INPUTS` (source exited non-zero, or emitted mixed-kind/array elements — a broken producer is not an empty queue), `E_INPUTS_LIMIT` (> 10k), `E_HARNESS`, `E_INTERNAL`.\\n\\n`E_HARNESS` is razor-thin by design: **only** \\\"harness binary missing / unresolvable\\\". An agent process dying unexpectedly (OOM, segfault, API hiccup) is always a non-zero exit — class B, retryable. If that boundary drifts, transient flakes start crashing whole runs.\\n\\n#### Handling errors in the graph\\n\\nHandlers are **ordinary nodes routed before the terminal** — never a body on the terminal (`kill -9` makes exit hooks an illusion; a node has a timeout, attempts and its own log). The error payload arrives through the same channels as all data: `$MEDULLA_LAST_*` for shell, `{{last.*}}` for prompts. Global catch-all is three lines of `defaults`:\\n\\n```yaml\\ndefaults:\\n  on_signal: {__failed__: notify, __default__: notify}   # root supervisor\\n\\nnodes:\\n  notify:\\n    shell: 'curl -s \\\"$HOOK\\\" -d \\\"text=[medulla] $MEDULLA_LAST_NODE ($MEDULLA_LAST_SIGNAL): $MEDULLA_LAST_MESSAGE\\\"'\\n    timeout: 30\\n    on_signal: {__default__: __exit_fail__, __failed__: __exit_fail__}   # own dunders explicit!\\n```\\n\\nA node-level edge beats the defaults catch-all, which beats built-ins — a three-tier supervision chain. The validator rejects a defaults-inherited edge that points at its own node (`notify` failing into `notify` would loop); explicit self-loops remain legal (retry pattern). A pool's `__failed__` message is pre-aggregated: `\\\"2/5 inputs ok (min_success=3); rc!=0 x2, timeout x1, render x0\\\"` — the manifest has the rest. Class A is not catchable in the graph by definition: the graph itself is what's broken.\\n\\nThe error **message is the body of the signal** that routed to `__exit_fail__` (engine facts carry their own: `__failed__` → rc/join stats, `__empty__` → \\\"source returned 0 inputs\\\", `__default__` → tail of stdout). Every run ends with an atomic `outcome.json`:\\n\\n```json\\n{\\\"outcome\\\": \\\"failed\\\", \\\"exit_code\\\": 2,\\n \\\"error\\\": {\\\"code\\\": \\\"SIGNAL_FAIL\\\", \\\"message\\\": \\\"no repo access: git clone rc=128\\\",\\n           \\\"node\\\": \\\"plan\\\", \\\"step\\\": 12, \\\"signal\\\": \\\"blocked\\\"},\\n \\\"steps\\\": 12, \\\"duration_s\\\": 4180}\\n```\\n\\n### Lifecycle\\n\\n**Boot** — parse CLI → load yaml (`version: \\\"2\\\"` or a friendly migration error) → validate everything (`E_VALIDATION` before any run dir exists) → create `runs/<ts>-<run_id>/`, snapshot the config, seed vars, prune old runs → set the deadline.\\n\\n**Node loop** (single program counter) — per node: deadline check → materialize inputs (no key → phantom; list → snapshot into the step dir; source → render, execute, sniff, check) → run the pool (`max_parallel` workers; per input: render once → attempts → fallback; stream stdout to the step log with realtime signal extraction) → classify (decision: signal | `__failed__` | `__default__`; pool: `__done__` | `__failed__` | `__empty__`) → resolve the edge (node `on_signal` → `defaults.on_signal` → built-ins) → append the journal row → next node or terminal.\\n\\n**Finish** — atomic `outcome.json`, exit 0/2; crashes write `outcome.json` with the `E_*` code and exit 1; SIGINT → best-effort outcome, exit 130.\\n\\n**Resume** — pick the latest run without `outcome.json`, or with `outcome: interrupted` **or `crashed`** (deliberate extension: the #1 resume trigger is the `E_DEADLINE` crash; config-class crashes simply crash again identically) — or `--run <dir>`: reload the **snapshot** config (a run's config is immutable), vars, journal position; an interrupted pool continues from the manifest done-mask (input identity = `(index, hash)`; sources are never re-executed on resume); an interrupted decision node re-runs whole (body idempotence is the author's concern). The deadline is fresh per invocation.\\n\\n### Layout\\n\\nA pipeline is a self-contained directory — contract, code, history:\\n\\n```\\n.medulla/pipelines/<name>/\\n  pipeline.yaml            # the contract\\n  prompts/  scripts/       # its code\\n  harness/                 # phase 2: harness HOME (sessions), shared by all runs\\n  runs/<ts>-<run_id>/      # one directory per run\\n    pipeline.yaml          # config snapshot as loaded (immutable for the run)\\n    journal.jsonl          # graph chronology, append-only (step, node, rc, signal, message, duration)\\n    vars.yaml              # variables (updated on var signals)\\n    outcome.json           # written only on completion (atomic); absent = running or hard-killed\\n    steps/\\n      001-triage/                        # every step gets a directory\\n      002-plan/prompt.md                 # rendered agent input (what the agent actually saw)\\n      002-plan/attempt-1-codex.txt       # raw CLI stream per attempt\\n      004-apply/inputs.json              # inputs snapshot (resume)\\n      004-apply/input-2-sonnet.txt       # per-input logs\\n      004-apply/manifest.jsonl           # {index, key, input, ok, reason, signal, message,\\n                                         #  rc, timed_out, attempts, fallback, harness, model,\\n                                         #  vars, updates, duration_s, log}\\n```\\n\\nRetention: on start, keep the newest `keep_runs` finished runs; directories without `outcome.json` younger than the pipeline timeout are never pruned. History browsing needs no CLI: `ls runs/`, `cat outcome.json`.\\n\\n**Phase 2 (designed, not in phase 1):** pipeline-scoped harness sessions — `harness/` as the container HOME, session ids captured into journal/manifest from the CLI streams the engine already parses, and a `resume:` field on the agent block to continue a node's last successful session (current run first, then previous runs, newest first). Docker: everything lives under `/workspace` (already mounted RW); credentials are copied once from the read-only `/mnt/*` mounts.\\n\\n### Canonical patterns\\n\\n- **Budget gate** (bounded rework — the engine has no per-node visit caps; cycle semantics belong to the workflow):\\n  ```yaml\\n  gate:\\n    shell: |\\n      n=$(( ${PLAN_TRIES:-0} + 1 ))\\n      echo \\\"<signal:var key=PLAN_TRIES>$n</signal:var>\\\"\\n      [ \\\"$n\\\" -le 3 ] && echo \\\"<signal:go>ok</signal:go>\\\" || echo \\\"<signal:budget_out>ok</signal:budget_out>\\\"\\n    on_signal: {go: plan, budget_out: escalate}\\n  ```\\n- **Crawler**: producer node → pool → probe node → edge back; the source re-runs on each new node run.\\n- **Heterogeneous pool**: inputs carry `role`/`prompt` fields; the node prompt is the template (code, vars resolve), input fragments are inserted inert.\\n- **Entry cleanup** (crash-only): there is no `finally`; exit hooks are an illusion under `kill -9`. Clean stale state idempotently at the start of the node/run that needs it clean.\\n- **Parallel tickets**: the engine guarantees vars isolation; file/git isolation is the body's job (`git worktree` per input) and disjointness is the producer script's contract.\\n- **DLQ (repair node)**: failed pool inputs are structured data — re-enqueue only them, never the whole pool:\\n  ```yaml\\n  retry-failed:\\n    inputs: {shell: \\\"jq -c 'select(.ok|not) | .input' $MEDULLA_MANIFEST_APPLY\\\"}\\n    max_parallel: 2\\n    shell: 'bash scripts/fix_one.sh \\\"$MEDULLA_INPUT_ID\\\"'\\n    on_signal: {__done__: report, __empty__: report}\\n  ```\\n\\n### Migrating from v1\\n\\nThe v1 engine is removed; this table is the dictionary for porting old pipelines.\\n\\n| v1 | v2 |\\n|---|---|\\n| `stages` / `starting` | `nodes` / `start` |\\n| `runner:` / `llm:` / `executor`+`command` | action fields directly on the node: `agent: {harness, model, effort, args}` xor `shell` |\\n| `loop:` + `list:` + `fetch:` + `parallel: true` | `inputs:` on the node + `max_parallel` |\\n| `done: __next_item__` + `loop_done` | pool joins route `__done__` / `__failed__` / `__empty__` via `min_success` |\\n| `max_iterations` / `reset_iterations` / `on_max` | removed — budget gate pattern (vars) |\\n| hardcoded `max_rounds=500` | pipeline `timeout` (wall-clock deadline) |\\n| `round_timeout` / `fallback_runner` | `defaults: {timeout, fallback}` |\\n| `__exit__` | `__exit_ok__` / `__exit_fail__` |\\n| `ignore_rc` | `ignore_exit_code` (decision nodes only) |\\n| `{{__item__}}` / `{{__list_item__}}` | `{{input}}` family + `MEDULLA_INPUT_*` env |\\n| `MEDULLA_TASK_ID` | `MEDULLA_RUN_ID` |\\n| `.medulla/vars.<task>.yaml` | `.medulla/pipelines/<name>/runs/<id>/` \\\"\\\"\\\"SafeLoader that rejects duplicate mapping keys (PyYAML silently overwrites).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef _no_dup_mapping(loader, deep=False, node=None):\\n    mapping = {}\\n    for key_node, that\\n36\\t# delivery, appended to every agent prompt FILE (never to inherited prompt\\n37\\t# text, so it is stamped exactly once per written file). Found by the first\\n38\\t# live smoke: without it, agents cannot know the tag format -> __default__.\\n39\\tSIGNAL_PROTOCOL = \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n40\\t\\n41\\t## Signal protocol (engine-provided)\\n42\\t\\n43\\tTo emit a signal, print this template on its own line in your final message,\\n44\\tsubstituting {name} with the signal's name and the body with a short message\\n45\\t(no backticks, no quotes, keep the angle brackets exactly as shown):\\n46\\t\\n47\\t<signal:{name}>short message</signal:{name}>\\n48\\t\\n49\\tFor example, a signal named finished would be printed as one line starting\\n50\\twith \\\"<signal:\\\" then \\\"finished>\\\", the message, and the matching closing tag.\\n51\\tEmit a signal only when the task tells you to. Print it as plain text in your\\n52\\tanswer — never via a shell command or a file.\\n53\\t\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n54\\t\\n55\\t\\n56\\tdef log(msg: str) -> None:\\n57\\t    print(f\\\"[medulla] {msg}\\\", file=sys.stderr)\\n58\\t\\n59\\t\\n60\\tdef _tail(text: str, n: int = 400) -> str:\\n61\\t    text = text.strip()\\n62\\t    return text[-n:] if len(text) > n else text\\n63\\t\\n64\\t\\n65\\tdef _retry_delay() -> None:\\n66\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"Fixed pause between attempts (pilot's battle scar: 2s beats a rate-limit\\n67\\t    storm). Env-tunable so tests run at 0.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n68\\t    delay = float(os.environ.get(\\\"MEDULLA_RETRY_DELAY_S\\\", \\\"2\\\"))\\n69\\t    if delay > 0:\\n70\\t        time.sleep(delay)\\n71\\t\\n72\\t\\n73\\tdef _timeout_env(seconds: float) -> str:\\n74\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"Env representation of a clamped timeout: never \\\"0\\\" for a live budget —\\n75\\t    an agent CLI sizing its own timeout from this must not read \\\"no limit\\\".\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n76\\t    return str(max(1, int(round(seconds))))\\n77\\t\\n78\\t\\n79\\tdef _input_hash(value) -> str:\\n80\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"Stable input identity for resume/idempotency. Python's hash() is salted.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n81\\t    import hashlib\\n82\\t    canonical = json.dumps(value, sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False)\\n83\\t    return hashlib.sha256(canonical.encode(\\\"utf-8\\\")).hexdigest()[:16]\\n84\\t\\n85\\t\\n86\\tdef _sniff_inputs(stdout: str, node_name: str) -> list:\\n87\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"First non-blank byte decides: '[' JSON array, '{' JSON-lines, else plain lines.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n88\\t    text = stdout.strip()\\n89\\t    if not text:\\n90\\t        return []\\n91\\t    if text.startswith(\\\"[\\\"):\\n92\\t        try:\\n93\\t            data = json.loads(text)\\n94\\t        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:\\n95\\t            raise EngineCrash(E_INPUTS, f\\\"inputs source: broken JSON array: {exc}\\\",\\n96\\t                              node=node_name)\\n97\\t        if not isinstance(data, list):\\n98\\t            raise EngineCrash(E_INPUTS, \\\"inputs source: JSON is not an array\\\", node=node_name)\\n99\\t        return data\\n100\\t    if text.startswith(\\\"{\\\"):\\n101\\t        rows = []\\n102\\t        for n, line in enumerate(text.splitlines(), start=1):\\n103\\t            line = line.strip()\\n104\\t            if not line:\\n105\\t                continue\\n106\\t            try:\\n107\\t                rows.append(json.loads(line))\\n108\\t            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:\\n109\\t                raise EngineCrash(E_INPUTS, f\\\"inputs source: broken JSON on line {n}: {exc}\\\",\\n110\\t                                  node=node_name)\\n111\\t        return rows\\n112\\t    return [line.strip() for line in text.splitlines() if line.strip()]\\n113\\t\\n114\\t\\n115\\t# ── structured signal scan (foundation for pool manifests) ──────────────────\\n116\\t\\n117\\t@dataclass\\n118\\tclass ScanResult:\\n119\\t    first_known: str | None = None\\n120\\t    first_body: str = \\\"\\\"\\n121\\t    vars: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)\\n122\\t    updates: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\\n123\\t\\n124\\t\\n125\\tdef scan_stdout(stdout: str, known: set[str] | None) -> ScanResult:\\n126\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"stdout only — stderr never routes. Engine facts are excluded from `known`\\n127\\t    upstream: a body printing <signal:__failed__> must never route (namespace law).\\n128\\t\\n129\\t    known=None is pool mode: record the first ANY bare user signal (pool routing\\n130\\t    tables hold only dunders, yet body signals must reach the manifest).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n131\\t    res = ScanResult()\\n132\\t    for name, attrs, body in extract_signals(stdout):\\n133\\t        if name == \\\"update\\\":\\n134\\t            res.updates.append(body)\\n135\\t            continue\\n136\\t        if name == \\\"var\\\":\\n137\\t            key = (attrs or {}).get(\\\"key\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n138\\t            if key and body:\\n139\\t                res.vars[key] = body\\n140\\t            continue\\n141\\t        if res.first_known is not None:\\n142\\t            continue\\n143\\t        if known is None:\\n144\\t            if name not in CHANNEL_SIGNALS and not name.startswith(\\\"__\\\"):\\n145\\t                res.first_known, res.first_body = name, body\\n146\\t        elif name in known:\\n147\\t            res.first_known, res.first_body = name, body\\n148\\t    return res\\n149\\t\\n150\\t\\n151\\t@dataclass\\n152\\tclass AttemptsOutcome:\\n153\\t    signal: str | None               # user signal | __failed__ | __default__ | None (pool silent ok)\\n154\\t    message: str\\n155\\t    attempts: int                    # total executions across phases (0 = pre decided)\\n156\\t    attempts_primary: int = 0\\n157\\t    attempts_fallback: int = 0\\n158\\t    rc: int | None = None\\n159\\t    timed_out: bool = False\\n160\\t    fallback_used: bool = False\\n161\\t    concluding_phase: str | None = None   # \\\"primary\\\" | \\\"fallback\\\" | None (pre decided)\\n162\\t    harness: str | None = None            # harness that produced the concluding outcome\\n163\\t    model: str | None = None\\n164\\t    guarded: bool = False                 # pre emitted a routing signal; body never ran\\n165\\t    failure_class: str | None = None      # for __failed__: \\\"pre\\\" | \\\"rc\\\" | \\\"timeout\\\" | \\\"post\\\"\\n166\\t    recorded_signal: str | None = None    # pool: first bare signal seen (data, never outcome)\\n167\\t    recorded_body: str = \\\"\\\"\\n168\\t    pending_vars: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)  # fold law: caller applies\\n169\\t    updates: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\\n170\\t\\n171\\t\\n172\\tclass Engine:\\n173\\t    def __init__(self, pipeline: Pipeline, store: RunStore, workdir: Path):\\n174\\t        self.p = pipeline\\n175\\t        self.store = store\\n176\\t        self.workdir = workdir\\n177\\t        self.vars: dict[str, str] = dict(pipeline.vars)\\n178\\t        self.last: dict = {}\\n179\\t        self.deadline: float | None = (\\n180\\t            time.monotonic() + pipeline.timeout if pipeline.timeout else None\\n181\\t        )\\n182\\t        self.steps = 0\\n183\\t        self.manifests: dict[str, Path] = {}   # node -> manifest path (engine map, not vars)\\n184\\t\\n185\\t    # ── deadline ──\\n186\\t    def _remaining(self) -> float | None:\\n187\\t        if self.deadline is None:\\n188\\t            return None\\n189\\t        return self.deadline - time.monotonic()\\n190\\t\\n191\\t    def _check_deadline(self) -> None:\\n192\\t        rem = self._remaining()\\n193\\t        if rem is not None and rem <= 0:\\n194\\t            raise EngineCrash(E_DEADLINE, f\\\"pipeline timeout ({self.p.timeout}s) exhausted\\\")\\n195\\t\\n196\\t    def _clamp(self, timeout_s: float) -> float:\\n197\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Clamp a child timeout to the remaining budget (clamp, not crash).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n198\\t        rem = self._remaining()\\n199\\t        if rem is None:\\n200\\t            return float(timeout_s)\\n201\\t        if rem <= 0:\\n202\\t            raise EngineCrash(E_DEADLINE, f\\\"pipeline timeout ({self.p.timeout}s) exhausted\\\")\\n203\\t        return min(float(timeout_s), rem)\\n204\\t\\n205\\t    # ── env ──\\n206\\t    def _base_env(self, vars_map: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> dict[str, str]:\\n207\\t        env = dict(self.vars if vars_map is None else vars_map)\\n208\\t        env[\\\"MEDULLA_RUN_ID\\\"] = self.store.run_id\\n209\\t        env[\\\"MEDULLA_RUN_DIR\\\"] = str(self.store.dir)\\n210\\t        for node_name, path in self.manifests.items():\\n211\\t            env[f\\\"MEDULLA_MANIFEST_{node_name.upper().replace('-', '_')}\\\"] = str(path)\\n212\\t        if self.last:\\n213\\t            env[\\\"MEDULLA_LAST_NODE\\\"] = str(self.last.get(\\\"node\\\", \\\"\\\"))\\n214\\t            env[\\\"MEDULLA_LAST_SIGNAL\\\"] = str(self.last.get(\\\"signal\\\", \\\"\\\"))\\n215\\t            env[\\\"MEDULLA_LAST_MESSAGE\\\"] = str(self.last.get(\\\"message\\\", \\\"\\\"))\\n216\\t            env[\\\"MEDULLA_LAST_RC\\\"] = str(self.last.get(\\\"rc\\\", \\\"\\\"))\\n217\\t            env[\\\"MEDULLA_LAST_EVENT_JSON\\\"] = json.dumps(self.last, ensure_ascii=False)\\n218\\t        return env\\n219\\t\\n220\\t    # ── vars (fold law application point) ──\\n221\\t    @staticmethod\\n222\\t    def _valid_var_key(key: str) -> bool:\\n223\\t        return bool(VAR_NAME_RE.match(key)) and key not in ENV_BLACKLIST_EXACT and \\\\\\n224\\t            not any(key.startswith(p) for p in ENV_BLACKLIST_PREFIX)\\n225\\t\\n226\\t    def _apply_vars(self, pending: dict[str, str]) -> None:\\n227\\t        if not pending:\\n228\\t            return\\n229\\t        for key, value in pending.items():\\n230\\t            if not self._valid_var_key(key):\\n231\\t                log(f\\\"warn: var '{key}' rejected (reserved/invalid name)\\\")\\n232\\t                continue\\n233\\t            self.vars[key] = value\\n234\\t        self.store.write_vars(self.vars)\\n235\\t\\n236\\t    # ── render helpers ──\\n237\\t    def _known(self, node: Node) -> set[str]:\\n238\\t        return self.p.known_signals(node) - set(CHANNEL_SIGNALS) - set(ENGINE_FACTS)\\n239\\t\\n240\\t    def _render_or_crash(self, text: str, node: Node, what: str, required: bool = True) -> str:\\n241\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Decision-context render: any breakage is a pipeline bug -> E_RENDER.\\n242\\t        required=False: an optional field rendering empty counts as absent (contract).\\n243\\t        Part-3 pools pass their own render_fn with fail-the-input semantics.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n244\\t        try:\\n245\\t            rendered = render(text, self.p.dir, self.vars, last=self.last)\\n246\\t        except RenderError as exc:\\n247\\t            raise EngineCrash(E_RENDER, f\\\"{what}: {exc}\\\", node=node.name)\\n248\\t        if required and not rendered.strip():\\n249\\t            raise EngineCrash(E_RENDER, f\\\"{what} rendered empty (broken template or empty field)\\\",\\n250\\t                              node=node.name)\\n251\\t        return rendered\\n252\\t\\n253\\t    # ── the attempts seam ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n254\\t    # Owns the FULL hook machinery (panel: pre and post live INSIDE the seam so\\n255\\t    # part-3 pool workers get identical semantics by calling this per input):\\n256\\t    #   pre -> [guard?] -> body attempts (primary -> fallback) with post per attempt.\\n257\\t    # The seam never mutates engine state: `apply_pre_vars` is the caller's policy\\n258\\t    # (decision: apply to self.vars so the body render sees them; pool at\\n259\\t    # max_parallel>1: record to manifest + local ctx), and body/post vars come\\n260\\t    # back as pending_vars for the caller to apply per the fold law.\\n261\\t    def _run_attempts(\\n262\\t        self,\\n263\\t        node: Node,\\n264\\t        step_dir: Path,\\n265\\t        render_fn,\\n266\\t        apply_pre_vars,\\n267\\t        attempt_ns: str,\\n268\\t        known: set[str] | None,\\n269\\t        env_fn=None,\\n270\\t        pool_mode: bool = False,\\n271\\t    ) -> AttemptsOutcome:\\n272\\t        # env_fn: callable -> dict, the base env for hooks and bodies. Decision nodes\\n273\\t        # default to self._base_env (pre vars land in self.vars and are picked up on\\n274\\t        # the next call); part-3 pool workers pass their own (base + input ctx + local\\n275\\t        # pre-vars overlay) so parallel inputs never touch shared engine state.\\n276\\t        if env_fn is None:\\n277\\t            env_fn = self._base_env\\n278\\t        action = node.action\\n279\\t\\n280\\t        pre_updates: list[str] = []\\n281\\t        primary_tag = \\\"shell\\\" if action.kind == \\\"shell\\\" else action.agent.harness\\n282\\t        if node.pre is not None:\\n283\\t            pre_rendered = render_fn(node.pre, \\\"pre\\\")\\n284\\t            hook_timeout = self._clamp(HOOK_TIMEOUT_S)\\n285\\t            pre_env = {**env_fn(),\\n286\\t                       \\\"MEDULLA_TIMEOUT_S\\\": _timeout_env(hook_timeout),\\n287\\t                       \\\"MEDULLA_HARNESS\\\": primary_tag}\\n288\\t            pre_res = proc_run(pre_rendered, self.workdir, hook_timeout,\\n289\\t                               extra_env=pre_env, log_path=step_dir / \\\"pre.txt\\\")\\n290\\t            pre_scan = scan_stdout(pre_res.stdout, known)\\n291\\t            pre_updates = pre_scan.updates\\n292\\t            # a known signal wins over rc — same grammar as everywhere else\\n293\\t            if pre_scan.first_known is not None:\\n294\\t                apply_pre_vars(pre_scan.vars)     # env prep applies before the guard routes\\n295\\t                return AttemptsOutcome(           # guard: body and post are skipped\\n296\\t                    signal=pre_scan.first_known, message=pre_scan.first_body,\\n297\\t                    attempts=0, rc=pre_res.rc, guarded=True, updates=pre_updates,\\n298\\t                )\\n299\\t            if pre_res.rc != 0:\\n300\\t                return AttemptsOutcome(\\n301\\t                    signal=SIG_FAILED,\\n302\\t                    message=f\\\"pre hook failed: rc={pre_res.rc}; stderr: {_tail(pre_res.stderr)}\\\",\\n303\\t                    attempts=0, rc=pre_res.rc, timed_out=pre_res.timed_out,\\n304\\t                    failure_class=\\\"pre\\\", updates=pre_updates,\\n305\\t                )\\n306\\t            apply_pre_vars(pre_scan.vars)   # env prep BEFORE the body renders\\n307\\t\\n308\\t        post_rendered = render_fn(node.post, \\\"post\\\") if node.post else None\\n309\\t\\n310\\t        current = action\\n311\\t        phase = \\\"primary\\\"\\n312\\t        fallback = self.p.action_fallback(action) if action.kind == \\\"agent\\\" else None\\n313\\t        fallback_used = False\\n314\\t        # contract: \\\"primary gets N, then fallback gets N\\\" — a fallback without its\\n315\\t        # own max_attempts inherits the primary's effective budget\\n316\\t        phase_budget = self.p.action_max_attempts(action)\\n317\\t\\n318\\t        invoke, prompt_text, agent_spec = self._prepare_body(\\n319\\t            current, node, step_dir, render_fn, phase)\\n320\\t        harness_name = agent_spec.harness if agent_spec else None\\n321\\t\\n322\\t        attempt = 0\\n323\\t        total = 0\\n324\\t        n_primary = 0\\n325\\t        n_fallback = 0\\n326\\t        last_failure_class: str | None = None\\n327\\t        while True:\\n328\\t            attempt += 1\\n329\\t            total += 1\\n330\\t            if phase == \\\"primary\\\":\\n331\\t                n_primary += 1\\n332\\t            else:\\n333\\t                n_fallback += 1\\n334\\t            self._check_deadline()\\n335\\t            eff = self._clamp(self.p.action_timeout(current))\\n336\\t            attempt_id = f\\\"{attempt_ns}.{phase[0]}{attempt}\\\"\\n337\\t            tag = \\\"shell\\\" if current.kind == \\\"shell\\\" else agent_spec.harness\\n338\\t            env = {**env_fn(),\\n339\\t                   \\\"MEDULLA_TIMEOUT_S\\\": _timeout_env(eff),\\n340\\t                   \\\"MEDULLA_ATTEMPT_ID\\\": extra_env=env,\\n345\\t                              log_path=step_dir / f\\\"attempt-{total}-{tag}.txt\\\",\\n346\\t                              stdin_data=invoke.stdin, \\\"agent\\\":\\n351\\t                raw_text = resolve_harness(agent_spec).filter_stdout(raw_text)\\n352\\t            body_scan = scan_stdout(raw_text, known)\\n353\\t\\n354\\t            post_rc = post_signal = None\\n355\\t            post_scan = ScanResult()\\n356\\t            if post_rendered is not None:\\n357\\t                hook_timeout = self._clamp(HOOK_TIMEOUT_S)\\n358\\t                post_env = {**env,\\n359\\t                            \\\"MEDULLA_TIMEOUT_S\\\": _timeout_env(hook_timeout),\\n360\\t                            \\\"MEDULLA_BODY_RC\\\":                                 hook_timeout, extra_env=post_env,\\n364\\t                                    log_path=step_dir / f\\\"post-{total}.txt\\\")\\n365\\t                post_scan = scan_stdout(post_res.stdout, known)\\n366\\t                post_rc, post_signal = post_res.rc, post_scan.first_known\\n367\\t\\n368\\t            # Pool conjunction law: ok = rc==0 AND no timeout AND no post veto.\\n369\\t            # Signals are DATA in pools — they are recorded, they never classify\\n370\\t            # (\\\"echo <signal:x>; exit 7\\\" must not become ok). ignore_exit_code\\n371\\t            # never reaches pools, not even via defaults (min_success owns that).\\n372\\t            decision = classify_attempt(\\n373\\t                kind=current.kind, rc=result.rc, timed_out=result.timed_out,\\n374\\t                body_signal=None if pool_mode else body_scan.first_known,\\n375\\t                post_rc=post_rc,\\n376\\t                post_signal=None if pool_mode else post_signal,\\n377\\t                ignore_exit_code=(False if pool_mode\\n378\\t                                  else self.p.action_ignore_exit_code(current)),\\n379\\t            )\\n380\\t            move = next_move(\\n381\\t                decision, kind=current.kind, phase=phase, attempt=attempt,\\n382\\t                max_attempts=phase_budget,\\n383\\t                has_fallback=fallback is not None,\\n384\\t                pool_mode=pool_mode,\\n385\\t            )\\n386\\t            if decision.failure_class is not None:\\n387\\t                last_failure_class = decision.failure_class\\n388\\t\\n389\\t            if move.move is Move.RETRY_SAME:\\n390\\t                log(f\\\"attempt {attempt_id} failed (rc={result.rc}), retrying\\\")\\n391\\t                _retry_delay()      # a zero-delay retry on a 429 is a provider-ban request\\n392\\t                continue\\n393\\t            if move.move is Move.SWITCH_FALLBACK:\\n394\\t                log(f\\\"attempt {attempt_id} failed (rc={result.rc}), switching to fallback\\\")\\n395\\t                _retry_delay()\\n396\\t                current = fallback\\n397\\t                phase = \\\"fallback\\\"\\n398\\t                attempt = 0\\n399\\t                fallback_used = True\\n400\\t                if fallback.max_attempts is not None:\\n401\\t                    phase_budget = fallback.max_attempts\\n402\\t                invoke, prompt_text, agent_spec = self._prepare_body(\\n403\\t                    current, node, step_dir, render_fn, phase, inherited_prompt=prompt_text)\\n404\\t                harness_name = agent_spec.harness if agent_spec else None\\n405\\t                continue\\n406\\t\\n407\\t            # DONE — state signals are collected from a successful outcome only:\\n408\\t            # a ROUTED signal, or a pool's silent-ok (its vars ARE successful vars —\\n409\\t            # dropping them would make row.vars lie). A __default__ conclusion is a\\n410\\t            # communication failure: its vars must not leak (fold law).\\n411\\t            pending: dict[str, str] = {}\\n412\\t            updates = pre_updates + body_scan.updates + post_scan.updates\\n413\\t            silent_ok = pool_mode and decision.verdict is Verdict.SILENT\\n414\\t            if decision.verdict is Verdict.ROUTE or silent_ok:\\n415\\t                pending = {**body_scan.vars, **post_scan.vars}   # post wins on conflict\\n416\\t\\n417\\t            signal = move.signal\\n418\\t            if signal == SIG_FAILED:\\n419\\t                message = (f\\\"body died: rc={result.rc}, {total} attempt(s)\\\"\\n420\\t                           f\\\"{' (fallback tried)' if fallback_used else ''}; \\\"\\n421\\t                           f\\\"stderr: {_tail(result.stderr)}\\\")\\n422\\t                if current.kind == \\\"agent\\\":\\n423\\t                    # harness-mined failure detail (codex error/turn.failed lives in\\n424\\t                    # stdout JSON the signal filter rightly drops)\\n425\\t                    detail = resolve_harness(agent_spec).extract_error(result.stdout)\\n426\\t                    if detail:\\n427\\t                        message += f\\\"; {detail}\\\"\\n428\\t            elif signal == SIG_DEFAULT:\\n429\\t                message = f\\\"no known signal emitted; stdout: {_tail(result.stdout)}\\\"\\n430\\t            elif signal is None:\\n431\\t                message = \\\"\\\"                        # pool silent ok\\n432\\t            elif post_signal is not None and signal == post_signal:\\n433\\t                message = post_scan.first_body\\n434\\t            else:\\n435\\t                message = body_scan.first_body\\n436\\t            return AttemptsOutcome(\\n437\\t                signal=signal, message=message, attempts=total,\\n438\\t                attempts_primary=n_primary, attempts_fallback=n_fallback,\\n439\\t                rc=result.rc, timed_out=result.timed_out,\\n440\\t                fallback_used=fallback_used, concluding_phase=phase,\\n441\\t                harness=harness_name,\\n442\\t                model=agent_spec.model if agent_spec else None,\\n443\\t                failure_class=last_failure_class if signal == SIG_FAILED else None,\\n444\\t                recorded_signal=post_scan.first_known or body_scan.first_known,\\n445\\t                recorded_body=post_scan.first_body or body_scan.first_body,\\n446\\t                pending_vars=pending, updates=updates,\\n447\\t            )\\n448\\t\\n449\\t    def _prepare_body(self, action: Action, node: Node, step_dir: Path,\\n450\\t                      render_fn, phase: str, inherited_prompt: str | None = None):\\n451\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Returns (Invoke, rendered_prompt_text_or_None, rendered_AgentSpec_or_None).\\n452\\t\\n453\\t        Every scalar agent field is a template (contract: an ensemble is just a pool\\n454\\t        with per-input harness/model). Optional fields rendering empty count as absent.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n455\\t        from .harness import Invoke\\n456\\t        if action.kind == \\\"shell\\\":\\n457\\t            shell = os.environ.get(\\\"SHELL\\\", \\\"bash\\\")\\n458\\t            rendered = render_fn(action.shell, \\\"shell\\\")\\n459\\t            return Invoke(argv=[shell, \\\"-lc\\\", rendered]), None, None\\n460\\t\\n461\\t        spec = action.agent\\n462\\t        harness = render_fn(spec.harness, \\\"agent.harness\\\").strip()\\n463\\t        model = render_fn(spec.model, \\\"agent.model\\\", required=False) if spec.model else None\\n464\\t        effort = render_fn(spec.effort, \\\"agent.effort\\\", required=False) if spec.effort else None\\n465\\t        # an arg rendering empty is absent (never the literal template text back)\\n466\\t        args = [r for a in spec.args\\n467\\t                if (r := render_fn(a, \\\"agent.args\\\", required=False)).strip()]\\n468\\t        from .model import AgentSpec\\n469\\t        rendered_spec = AgentSpec(harness=harness, model=model or None,\\n470\\t                                  effort=effort or None, args=args)\\n471\\t\\n472\\t        adapter = resolve_harness(rendered_spec)\\n473\\t        adapter.prepare(rendered_spec, self.workdir)   # idempotent preflight (agy trust, opencode.json)\\n474\\t        if action.prompt is not None:\\n475\\t            prompt_text = render_fn(action.prompt, \\\"prompt\\\")\\n476\\t        elif inherited_prompt is not None:\\n477\\t            prompt_text = inherited_prompt      # fallback reuses the primary's rendered prompt\\n478\\t        else:\\n479\\t            raise EngineCrash(E_RENDER, \\\"agent action has no prompt\\\", node=node.name)\\n480\\t        prompt_file = step_dir / (\\\"prompt.md\\\" if phase == \\\"primary\\\" else \\\"prompt-fallback.md\\\")\\n481\\t        # the protocol must ride in whatever text actually reaches the agent —\\n482\\t        # file (claude), stdin (codex) AND argv (opencode/agy). Battle test t2\\n483\\t        # found it riding in the file only: stdin/argv harnesses never saw it.\\n484\\t        # inherited prompt_text stays clean so a fallback doesn't double-stamp.\\n485\\t        full_prompt = prompt_text + SIGNAL_PROTOCOL\\n486\\t        prompt_file.write_text(full_prompt, encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n487\\t        timeout_s =        known = self._known(node)\\n494\\t        render_fn = lambda text, what, required=True: self._render_or_crash(\\n495\\t            text, node, what, required)\\n496\\t        outcome =   # ── pool machinery ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n518\\t\\n519\\t    def _materialize_inputs(self, node: Node, step_dir: Path) -> list:\\n520\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Snapshot inputs into steps/NNN-<node>/inputs.json (resume foundation).\\n521\\t        An existing snapshot short-circuits everything: sources are NEVER\\n522\\t        re-executed on                extra_env=self._base_env(),\\n533\\t                           log_path=step_dir / \\\"inputs-source.txt\\\")\\n534\\t            if res.timed_out:\\n535\\t                rem = node=node.name)\\n561\\t        (step_dir / \\\"inputs.json\\\").write_text(\\n562\\t            json.dumps(inputs, ensure_ascii=False, indent=1), encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n563\\t        return inputs\\n564\\t\\n565\\t    def _run_one_input(self, node: Node, step_dir: Path, step_no: int,\\n566\\t                       idx: int, value, count: int,\\n567\\t                       pool_vars: input_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\\n573\\t        key = f\\\"{idx}:{_input_hash(value)}\\\"\\n574\\t        local_ctx: dict[str, str] = {}\\n575\\t\\n576\\t        input_env = {\\n577\\t            \\\"MEDULLA_INPUT\\\": value if isinstance(value, str) else json.dumps(value, ensure_ascii=False),\\n578\\t            \\\"MEDULLA_INPUT_INDEX\\\": str(idx),\\n579\\t            \\\"MEDULLA_INPUT_COUNT\\\": str(count),\\n580\\t            \\\"MEDULLA_INPUT_KEY\\\": key,\\n581\\t        }\\n582\\t        if isinstance(value, dict):\\n583\\t            for k, v in str, required: bool = True) -> str:\\n590\\t            # pool-input render semantics: breakage fails THIS input, never the run\\n591\\t            merged = {**pool_vars, **local_ctx} if not sequential else self.vars\\n592\\t            rendered = render(text, self.p.dir, merged,\\n593\\t                              input_value=value, has_input=True,\\n594\\t    return rendered\\n598\\t\\n599\\t        def env_fn() -> dict[str, str]:\\n600\\t            base = self._base_env(None if sequential else {**pool_vars, **local_ctx})\\n601\\t            return {**base, **input_env}\\n602\\t\\n603\\t        def apply_pre_vars(pending: dict[str, str]) -> None:\\n604\\t            if sequential:\\n605\\t                self._apply_vars(pending)             # fold: ordered, not transactional\\n606\\t            else:\\n607\\t                # same blacklist as the sequential path — a parallel pre emitting\\n608\\t                # <signal:var key=PATH> must not poison this worker's subprocess env\\n609\\t                for k, v in pending.items():\\n610\\t                    if self._valid_var_key(k):\\n611\\t                        local_ctx[k] = \\\"input\\\": value}\\n616\\t        try:\\n617\\t            outcome = self._run_attempts(\\n618\\t                node, input_dir, render_fn, apply_pre_vars,\\n619\\t
