timer = threading.Timer(float(timeout), cancel.set)\\n445\\t                timer.daemon = True\\n446\\t                timer.start()\\n447\\t\\n448\\t            worker_start = time.monotonic()\\n449\\t            run_result: ExecutorResult | None = None\\n450\\t            try:\\n451\\t                for attempt in range(1, 3):\\n452\\t                    run_result = executor.run(\\n453\\t                        worker_prompt, model=runner.model, known_signals=known,\\n454\\t                        on_output=on_output, on_signal=on_signal,\\n455\\t                        cancel=cancel,\\n456\\t                        args=runner.args,\\n457\\t                    )\\n458\\t                    if run_result.exit_code == 0:\\n459\\t                        break\\n460\\t                    if cancel.is_set():\\n461\\t                        break\\n462\\t                    tag = (\\n463\\t                        f\\\"{runner.executor}/{runner.model}\\\"\\n464\\t                        if runner.model else runner.executor\\n465\\t                    )\\n466\\t                    self.display.warn(\\n467\\t                        f\\\"[{source}] {tag} failed \\\"\\n468\\t                        f\\\"(attempt {attempt}/2, exit {run_result.exit_code})\\\"\\n469\\t                    )\\n470\\t                    if attempt < 2:\\n471\\t                        time.sleep(self.delay)\\n472\\t            finally:\\n473\\t                if timer:\\n474\\t                    timer.cancel()\\n475\\t\\n476\\t            # If the timer fired, mark as timeout — but only when we don't\\n477\\t            # already have a successful result. A clean exit_code 0 wins\\n478\\t            # over a late-firing cancel (race window during executor cleanup).\\n479\\t            if cancel.is_set() and (run_result is None or run_result.exit_code != 0):\\n480\\t                run_result = ExecutorResult(\\n481\\t                    output=run_result.output if run_result else \\\"\\\",\\n482\\t                    exit_code=124,\\n483\\t                    error=f\\\"per_runner_timeout exceeded ({timeout}s)\\\",\\n484\\t                    signals=run_result.signals if run_result else [],\\n485\\t                )\\n486\\t\\n487\\t try:\\n491\\t                    with open(os.path.join(log_round_dir, f\\\"{source}.txt\\\"), \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n492\\t                        f.write(run_result.output or \\\"\\\")\\n493\\t     ],\\n499\\t                            f,\\n500\\t                            indent=2,\\n501\\t                        )\\n502\\t                except OSError as e:\\n503\\t                    self.display.warn(f\\\"[{source}] failed to write log: {e}\\\")\\n504\\t\\n505\\t            elapsed = int(time.monotonic() - worker_start)\\n506\\t            self.display.ensemble_runner_done(\\n507\\t                source,\\n508\\t                run_result.exit_code,\\n509\\t                elapsed,\\n510\\t                len(run_result.signals or []),\\n511\\t            )\\n512\\t            return source, run_result\\n513\\t\\n514\\t        if stage.parallel and len(descriptors) > 1:\\n515\\t            with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=len(descriptors)) as pool:\\n516\\t                futures = [pool.submit(worker, r, slug) for r, slug in descriptors]\\n517\\t                for fut in                src, res = worker(r, slug)\\n523\\t                results[src] = res\\n524\\t\\n525\\t        successes = sum(1 for r in results.values() if r.exit_code == 0)\\n526\\t        failures = len(runners) - successes\\n527\\t\\n528\\t        threshold = stage.min_success or len(runners)\\n529\\t        round_ok = successes >= threshold\\n530\\t\\n531\\t        return ExecutorResult(\\n532\\t            output=\\\"\\\",\\n533\\t            exit_code=0 if round_ok else 1,\\n534\\t            error=(\\n535\\t                None if round_ok\\n536\\t                else f\\\"{failures}/{len(runners)} runner(s) failed; \\\"\\n537\\t                     f\\\"need >= {threshold} success\\\"\\n538\\t            ),\\n539\\t            signals=list(self._live_signals),\\n540\\t        )\\n541\\t\\n542\\t    def _get_raw_prompt(self, stage: Stage) -> str:\\n543\\t        \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the unresolved prompt (or shell command) for os.path.join(self.config_dir, \\\".env\\\")\\n        if not os.path.isfile(env_path):\\n            return\\n        with open(env_path) as f:\\n            for line in f:\\n                line = \\\"\\\"\\\"Export vars to file and os.environ.\\n\\n        Writes config vars as defaults (won't overwrite agent-set values),\\n        then loads everything into os.environ.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        from pilot.vars import read_vars\\n\\n        existing = read_vars(self.vars_path)\\n        for key, value in self.config.vars.items():\\n            if key not in existing:\\n                write_var(self.vars_path, key, value)\\n\\n        export_vars(self.vars_path)\\n\\n    def _apply_var(self, key: str, value: str) -> None:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Persist a var to disk and into os.environ in one atomic step.\\n\\n        Used for both agent-emitted <signal:var> tags and shell-step\\n        signal-scanning so vars become live for downstream consumers in\\n        the same round.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        write_var(self.vars_path, key, value)\\n        os.environ[key] = value\\n\\n    def _handle_var_signal(self, sig: Signal, source: str | None = None) -> bool:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"If `sig` is a `<signal:var key=K>V</signal:var>` tag, persist it\\n        and emit a display line. Returns True iff the signal was a var.\\n\\n        Centralises the var-handling logic shared by `_on_signal` (executor\\n        callbacks) and `_run_step` (shell-hook stdout scanning).\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        if sig.name != \\\"var\\\" or \\\"key\\\" not in sig.attrs:\\n            return False\\n        self._apply_var(sig.attrs[\\\"key\\\"], sig.content)\\n        key = rich_escape(sig.attrs[\\\"key\\\"])\\n        val = rich_escape(sig.content)\\n        prefix = f\\\"\\\\\\\\[{source}] \\\" if source else \\\"\\\"\\n        self.display.info(f\\\"{prefix}[dim]var[/] {key}[dim]=[/]{val}\\\")\\n        return True\\n\\n    @staticmethod\\n    def _fmt_duration(seconds: int) -> str:\\n        if seconds >= 3600:\\n            return f\\\"{seconds // 3600}h{(seconds % 3600) // 60:02d}m\\\"\\n        if seconds >= 60:\\n            return f\\\"{seconds // 60}m{seconds % 60:02d}s\\\"\\n        return f\\\"{seconds}s\\\"\\n\\n    def run(self) -> None:\\n        # Export config dir so scripts and prompts can reference it\\n        abs_config_dir = os.path.abspath(self.config_dir)\\n        os.environ[\\\"PILOT_CONFIG_DIR\\\"] = abs_config_dir\\n        os.environ[\\\"PILOT_PIPELINE_NAME\\\"] = os.path.basename(abs_config_dir)\\n        write_var(self.vars_path, \\\"PILOT_CONFIG_DIR\\\", abs_config_dir)\\n\\n        # Load .env (secrets) before anything\\n        self._load_env_file()\\n\\n        # CLI vars: applied before _sync_env so they take precedence over\\n        # pipeline-yaml `vars:` defaults (which _sync_env writes only when\\n        # absent). They remain mutable by pre_step / agent signals at runtime.\\n        for key, value in self.cli_vars.items():\\n            self._apply_var(key, value)\\n\\n        # Sync config vars so pre_pipeline can use them\\n        self._sync_env()\\n\\n        # Pipeline pre_pipeline\\n        if self.config.pre_pipeline:\\n            if not self._run_step(\\\"pre_pipeline\\\", self.config.pre_pipeline, show_output=True):\\n                raise PipelineError(\\\"pre_pipeline failed\\\")\\n\\n        self._pipeline_rounds = 0\\n        self._pipeline_last_stage = self.state.stage\\n        pipeline_start = time.monotonic()\\n\\n        exit_status = \\\"failed\\\"\\n        try:\\n            exit_status = self._main_loop()\\n        finally:\\n            # Expose pipeline metadata as env vars for hooks\\n            total = int(time.monotonic() - pipeline_start)\\n            os.environ[\\\"PILOT_EXIT_STATUS\\\"] = exit_status\\n            os.environ[\\\"PILOT_DURATION\\\"] = self._fmt_duration(total)\\n            os.environ[\\\"PILOT_ROUNDS\\\"] = str(self._pipeline_rounds)\\n            os.environ[\\\"PILOT_LAST_STAGE\\\"] = self._pipeline_last_stage\\n\\n            # Clear state before conditional hooks (avoid stale state if chaining)\\n            if exit_status == \\\"success\\\":\\n                clear_state(self.state_path)\\n                clear_vars(self.vars_path)\\n\\n            # Conditional hooks (notify / chain / cleanup)\\n            if exit_status == \\\"success\\\" and self.config.on_pipeline_success:\\n                if not self._run_step(\\\"on_pipeline_success\\\", self.config.on_pipeline_success, show_output=True):\\n                    self.display.warn(\\\"on_pipeline_success failed\\\")\\n            elif exit_status == \\\"failed\\\" and self.config.on_pipeline_failure:\\n                if not self._run_step(\\\"on_pipeline_failure\\\", self.config.on_pipeline_failure, show_output=True):\\n                    self.display.warn(\\\"on_pipeline_failure failed\\\")\\n\\n    def _main_loop(self) -> str:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Run the main pipeline loop. Returns 'success' or 'failed'.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        consecutive_failures = 0\\n        round_num = 0\\n        pipeline_start = time.monotonic()\\n\\n        while True:\\n            round_num += 1\\n            round_start = time.monotonic()\\n            self._sync_env()\\n\\n            stage = self.config.stages.get(self.state.stage)\\n            if not stage:\\n                raise PipelineError(f\\\"Unknown stage: {self.state.stage}\\\")\\n            self._pipeline_rounds = round_num\\n            self._pipeline_last_stage = stage.name\\n\\n            if stage.is_ensemble:\\n                runners_info = [(r.executor, r.model) for r in stage.runners]\\n                self.display.round_header_ensemble(round_num, stage.name, runners_info)\\n            else:\\n                self.display.round_header(\\n                    round_num,\\n                    stage.name,\\n                    stage.runner.executor,\\n                    stage.runner.model,\\n                )\\n\\n            # pre_step — shell command before main executor; may emit\\n            # <signal:var> tags that become live for the executor below.\\n            if stage.pre_step:\\n                pre_ok = self._run_step(\\\"pre_step\\\", stage.pre_step)\\n                if not pre_ok:\\n                    consecutive_failures += 1\\n                    self.display.warn(\\n                        f\\\"pre_step failed, \\\"\\n                        f\\\"consecutive failures {consecutive_failures}/3\\\"\\n                    )\\n                    if consecutive_failures >= 3:\\n                        raise PipelineError(\\\"3 consecutive round failures\\\")\\n                    self._wait()\\n                    continue\\n\\n            # Per-runner stages defer template resolution until each worker\\n            # spawns, so runner.vars overrides apply correctly.\\n            raw_prompt = self._get_raw_prompt(stage)\\n\\n            # Known signals for this stage. Ensemble stages capture every\\n            # signal name (informational tags like <signal:done verdict=...>\\n            # belong on disk even though routing always falls through to\\n            # 'default').\\n            if stage.is_ensemble:\\n                known = None\\n            else:\\n                known = set(stage.on_signal.keys()) | BUILTIN_SIGNALS\\n\\n            # Run executor(s)\\n            self._live_signals = []\\n            if stage.is_ensemble:\\n                result = self._run_ensemble(stage, raw_prompt, known, round_num)\\n            else:\\n                result = self._run_with_retries(stage, raw_prompt, known)\\n\\n            # post_step — shell command after main executor (always runs)\\n            if stage.post_step:\\n                post_ok = self._run_step(\\\"post_step\\\", stage.post_step)\\n                if not post_ok:\\n                    self.display.warn(\\\"post_step failed (continuing)\\\")\\n\\n            elapsed = int(time.monotonic() - round_start)\\n            self.display.info(f\\\"[dim]round {round_num} · {self._fmt_duration(elapsed)}[/]\\\")\\n\\n            if result.exit_code != 0:\\n                consecutive_failures += 1\\n                self.display.warn(\\n                    f\\\"Round failed, \\\"\\n                    f\\\"consecutive failures {consecutive_failures}/3\\\"\\n                )\\n                if result.error:\\n                    self.display.warn(result.error)\\n                if consecutive_failures >= 3:\\n                    raise PipelineError(\\\"3 consecutive round failures\\\")\\n                self._wait()\\n                continue\\n\\n            consecutive_failures = 0\\n\\n            # Signals already displayed in real-time via _on_signal callback\\n            signals = self._live_signals\\n\\n            # Find first domain signal for routing (ensemble stages route via 'default' only)\\n            domain = None\\n            for s in signals:\\n                if s.name not in (\\\"var\\\", \\\"update\\\"):\\n                    domain = s\\n                    break\\n\\n            # Route\\n            if domain and domain.name in stage.on_signal:\\n                transition = stage.on_signal[domain.name]\\n            elif \\\"default\\\" in stage.on_signal:\\n                transition = stage.on_signal[\\\"default\\\"]\\n            else:\\n                raise PipelineError(f\\\"No default in stage '{stage.name}'\\\")\\n\\n            # Transition — exit pipeline\\n            if transition.to is None:\\n                total = int(time.monotonic() - pipeline_start)\\n                summary = domain.content if domain else \\\"complete\\\"\\n                if transition.fail:\\n                    self.display.error(f\\\"Pipeline stopped: {summary}\\\")\\n                    return \\\"failed\\\"\\n                else:\\n                    self.display.done(summary, f\\\"{round_num} rounds, {self._fmt_duration(total)}\\\")\\n                    return \\\"success\\\"\\n\\n            # Transition — next stage\\n            old_stage = self.state.stage\\n            self.state.stage = transition.to\\n            write_state(self.state_path, self.state)\\n            self.display.transition(old_stage, transition.to)\\n            self._wait()\\n\\n        return \\\"failed\\\"  # unreachable, loop exits via return or exception\\n\\n    def _make_callbacks(self, source: str | None):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Build (on_output, on_signal) closures.\\n\\n        source: per-runner tag for ensemble stages (e.g. \\\"claude-code/opus\\\"),\\n                None for single-runner stages.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        def on_output(text: str) -> None:\\n            self.display.executor_output(text, source=source)\\n\\n        def on_signal(sig: Signal) -> None:\\n            if source:\\n                sig.attrs = {**sig.attrs, \\\"source\\\": source}\\n            self._on_signal(sig)\\n\\n        return on_output, on_signal\\n\\n    def _resolve_runner_vars(self, runner: Runner) -> dict[str, str]:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Resolve each runner.vars value as a template against global vars.\\n\\n        This lets ensemble runners reference pre_step-emitted vars\\n        (`{{var:ROUND_DIR}}`) inside their per-runner config without any\\n        new template namespace.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        if not runner.vars:\\n            return {}\\n        resolved: dict[str, str] = {}\\n        for k, v in runner.vars.items():\\n            resolved[k] = resolve_templates(v, self.config_dir, self.vars_path)\\n        return resolved\\n\\n    def _resolve_prompt(self, raw: str, runner: Runner) -> str:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Resolve a raw prompt with runner.vars merged as overrides.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        return resolve_templates(\\n            raw, self.config_dir, self.vars_path,\\n            vars_overrides=self._resolve_runner_vars(runner) or None,\\n        )\\n\\n    def _run_with_retries(self, stage: Stage, raw_prompt: str,\\n                          known: set[str] | None) -> ExecutorResult:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Try primary runner twice, then fallback runner twice.\\n\\n        Returns the first successful result, or the last failed result.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        runners = [(stage.runner, \\\"primary\\\")]\\n        if stage.fallback_runner:\\n            runners.append((stage.fallback_runner, \\\"fallback\\\"))\\n\\n        on_output, on_signal = self._make_callbacks(None)\\n\\n        result: ExecutorResult | None = None\\n        for runner, label in runners:\\n            try:\\n                prompt = self._resolve_prompt(raw_prompt, runner)\\n            except TemplateError as e:\\n                raise PipelineError(f\\\"Template error: {e}\\\") from e\\n\\n            executor = self.executors.get(runner.executor)\\n            for attempt in range(1, 3):\\n                # Reset live signals on retry — only keep the final attempt's signals\\n                self._live_signals = []\\n                result = executor.run(\\n                    prompt, model=runner.model, known_signals=known,\\n                    on_output=on_output,\\n                    on_signal=on_signal,\\n                    args=runner.args,\\n                )\\n                if result.exit_code == 0:\\n                    return result\\n\\n                tag = f\\\"{runner.executor}/{runner.model}\\\" if runner.model else runner.executor\\n                self.display.warn(\\n                    f\\\"{tag} failed \\\"\\n                    f\\\"(attempt {attempt}/2, exit {result.exit_code})\\\"\\n                )\\n                if result.error:\\n                    self.display.warn(f\\\"stderr: {result.error}\\\")\\n                if attempt < 2:\\n                    self._wait()\\n\\n            # Switch to fallback\\n            if label == \\\"primary\\\" and stage.fallback_runner:\\n                fb = stage.fallback_runner\\n                fb_tag = f\\\"{fb.executor}/{fb.model}\\\" if fb.model else fb.executor\\n                self.display.fallback(tag, fb_tag)\\n\\n        return result\\n\\n    def _run_ensemble(self, stage: Stage, raw_prompt: str,\\n                      known: set[str] | None,\\n                      round_num: int) -> ExecutorResult:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Run all runners on the same prompt and write per-runner internal logs.\\n\\n        Each worker resolves the prompt with its own runner.vars overrides.\\n        Where the model writes its formatted artifact is entirely the\\n        pipeline author's choice (declared via `runner.vars` and\\n        referenced inside the prompt as `{{var:NAME}}`).\\n\\n        The engine still keeps an internal log of each runner's stdout +\\n        parsed signals under <log_dir>/round-NNN-<stage>/ for debugging,\\n        but does not expose that path to the pipeline.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        log_round_dir: str | None = None\\n        if self.log_dir:\\n            log_round_dir = os.path.join(\\n                self.log_dir, f\\\"round-{round_num:03d}-{stage.name}\\\"\\n            )\\n            os.makedirs(log_round_dir, exist_ok=True)\\n\\n        runners = stage.runners\\n        timeout = stage.per_runner_timeout\\n\\n        descriptors: list[tuple[Runner, str]] = []\\n        seen_slugs: set[str] = set()\\n          i = 2\\n                while f\\\"{slug}-{i}\\\" in seen_slugs:\\n                    i += 1\\n                slug = f\\\"{slug}-{i}\\\"\\n            seen_slugs.add(slug)\\n            descriptors.append((r, slug))\\n\\n        results: dict[str, ExecutorResult] = {}\\n\\n        def worker(runner: Runner, source: str) -> tuple[str, ExecutorResult]:\\n            on_output, on_signal = self._make_callbacks(source)\\n            try:\\n                worker_prompt = self._resolve_prompt(raw_prompt, runner)\\n            except TemplateError as e:\\n                self.display.warn(f\\\"[{source}] template error: {e}\\\")\\n                return source, ExecutorResult(\\n                    output=\\\"\\\", exit_code=1, error=str(e), signals=[],\\n                )\\n\\n            executor = self.executors.get(runner.executor)\\n            cancel = threading.Event()\\n            timer: threading.Timer | None = None\\n            if timeout:\\n                timer = threading.Timer(float(timeout), cancel.set)\\n                timer.daemon = True\\n                timer.start()\\n\\n            worker_start = time.monotonic()\\n            run_result: ExecutorResult | None = None\\n            try:\\n                for attempt in range(1, 3):\\n                    run_result = executor.run(\\n                        worker_prompt, model=runner.model, known_signals=known,\\n                        on_output=on_output, on_signal=on_signal,\\n                        cancel=cancel,\\n                        args=runner.args,\\n                    )\\n                    if run_result.exit_code == 0:\\n                        break\\n                    if cancel.is_set():\\n                        break\\n                    tag = (\\n                        f\\\"{runner.executor}/{runner.model}\\\"\\n                        if runner.model else runner.executor\\n                    )\\n                    self.display.warn(\\n                        f\\\"[{source}] {tag} failed \\\"\\n                        f\\\"(attempt {attempt}/2, exit {run_result.exit_code})\\\"\\n                    )\\n                    if attempt < 2:\\n                        time.sleep(self.delay)\\n            finally:\\n                if timer:\\n                    timer.cancel()\\n\\n            # If the timer fired, mark as timeout — but only when we don't\\n            # already have a successful result. A clean exit_code 0 wins\\n            # over a late-firing cancel (race window during executor cleanup).\\n            if cancel.is_set() and (run_result is None or run_result.exit_code != 0):\\n                run_result = ExecutorResult(\\n                    output=run_result.output if run_result else \\\"\\\",\\n                    exit_code=124,\\n                    error=f\\\"per_runner_timeout exceeded ({timeout}s)\\\",\\n                    signals=run_result.signals if run_result else [],\\n                )\\n\\n            # Internal debug logs only — NOT the user's artifact, which\\n            # the model writes to wherever runner.vars told it to.\\n            if log_round_dir:\\n                try:\\n                    with open(os.path.join(log_round_dir, f\\\"{source}.txt\\\"), \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n                        f.write(run_result.output or \\\"\\\")\\n                    with open(os.path.join(log_round_dir, f\\\"{source}.signals.json\\\"), \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n                        json.dump(\\n                            [\\n                                {\\\"name\\\": s.name, \\\"content\\\": s.content, \\\"attrs\\\": s.attrs}\\n                                for s in (run_result.signals or [])\\n                            ],\\n                            f,\\n                            indent=2,\\n                        )\\n                except OSError as e:\\n                    self.display.warn(f\\\"[{source}] failed to write log: {e}\\\")\\n\\n            elapsed = int(time.monotonic() - worker_start)\\n            self.display.ensemble_runner_done(\\n                source,\\n                run_result.exit_code,\\n                elapsed,\\n                len(run_result.signals or []),\\n            )\\n            return source, run_result\\n\\n        if stage.parallel and len(descriptors) > 1:\\n            with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=len(descriptors)) as pool:\\n                futures = [pool.submit(worker, r, slug) for r, slug in descriptors]\\n                for fut in as_completed(futures):\\n                    src, res = fut.result()\\n                    results[src] = res\\n        else:\\n            for r, slug in descriptors:\\n                src, res = worker(r, slug)\\n                results[src] = res\\n\\n        successes = sum(1 for r in results.values() if r.exit_code == 0)\\n        failures = len(runners) - successes\\n\\n        threshold = stage.min_success or len(runners)\\n        round_ok = successes >= threshold\\n\\n        return ExecutorResult(\\n            output=\\\"\\\",\\n            exit_code=0 if round_ok else 1,\\n            error=(\\n                None if round_ok\\n                else f\\\"{failures}/{len(runners)} runner(s) failed; \\\"\\n                     f\\\"need >= {threshold} success\\\"\\n            ),\\n            signals=list(self._live_signals),\\n        )\\n\\n    def _get_raw_prompt(self, stage: Stage) -> str:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the unresolved prompt (or shell command) for a stage.\\n\\n        Resolution is deferred to per-runner time so runner.vars overrides\\n        compose correctly.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        if stage.is_ensemble:\\n            return stage.prompt or \\\"\\\"\\n        if stage.runner is not None and stage.runner.executor == \\\"shell\\\":\\n            return stage.runner.command or \\\"\\\"\\n        return stage.prompt or \\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def _on_signal(self, sig: Signal) -> None:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Real-time callback from executors — display and collect signals.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        self._live_signals.append(sig)\\n        source = sig.attrs.get(\\\"source\\\")\\n        if self._handle_var_signal(sig, source=source):\\n            return\\n        if sig.name == \\\"update\\\":\\n            self.display.update(rich_escape(sig.content), source=source)\\n        else:\\n            self.display.domain_signal(\\n                sig.name, rich_escape(sig.content), source=source,\\n            )\\n\\n    def _run_step(self, label: str, raw_command: str,\\n                  show_output: bool = False) -> bool:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Run a shell command. Returns True on success.\\n\\n        Stdout is scanned for `<signal:var key=NAME>VALUE</signal:var>`\\n        tags so pre/post hooks can publish vars using the same protocol\\n        agents use. Vars are applied to both the vars file and os.environ\\n        immediately so the same round's executor can see them.\\n\\n        show_output: True for pipeline hooks (always visible),\\n                     False for stage pre/post steps (verbose only).\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        try:\\n            command = resolve_templates(raw_command, self.config_dir, self.vars_path)\\n        except TemplateError as e:\\n            self.display.warn(f\\\"{label} template error: {e}\\\")\\n            return False\\n\\n        self.display.info(f\\\"[dim]{label}[/]\\\")\\n        proc = subprocess.run(\\n            command,\\n            shell=True,\\n            executable=\\\"bash\\\",\\n            cwd=self.config_dir,\\n            capture_output=True,\\n            text=True,\\n        )\\n\\n        if proc.stdout:\\n            for sig in parse_signals(proc.stdout):\\n                self._handle_var_signal(sig)\\n\\n            for line in proc.stdout.strip().splitlines():\\n                if show_output:\\n                    self.display.info(f\\\"  [dim]{line}[/]\\\")\\n                else:\\n                    self.display.executor_output(line)\\n        if proc.returncode != 0:\\n            if proc.stderr:\\n                self.display.warn(f\\\"{label} stderr: {proc.stderr.strip()}\\\")\\n            return False\\n        return True\\n\\n    def _wait(self) -> None:\\n        time.sleep(self.delay)\\n\",\"numLines\":612,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":612}}}\nmedulla/cli-agent/pipelines/spar/runs/2026-07-15_13-03-50-6e2dcc0f/steps/002-panel/input-0003/attempt-1-claude-code.txt:53:[out] \\\"MEDULLA_INPUT_COUNT\\\": str(count),\\n580\\t            \\\"MEDULLA_INPUT_KEY\\\": key,\\n581\\t        }\\n582\\t        if isinstance(value, dict):\\n583\\t            for k, v in Found by the first\\n# live smoke: without it, agents cannot know the tag format -> __default__.\\nSIGNAL_PROTOCOL = \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n## Signal protocol (engine-provided)\\n\\nTo emit a signal, print this template on its own line in your final message,\\nsubstituting {name} with the signal's name and the body with a short message\\n(no backticks, no quotes, keep the angle brackets exactly as shown):\\n\\n<signal:{name}>short message</signal:{name}>\\n\\nFor example, a signal named finished would be printed as one line starting\\nwith \\\"<signal:\\\" then \\\"finished>\\\", the message, and the matching closing tag.\\nEmit a signal only when the task tells you to. Python's hash() is salted.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    import hashlib\\n    canonical = json.dumps(value, sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False)\\n    return hashlib.sha256(canonical.encode(\\\"utf-8\\\")).hexdigest()[:16]\\n\\n\\ndef _sniff_inputs(stdout: str, node_name: str) -> list:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"First non-blank byte decides: '[' JSON array, '{' JSON-lines, else plain lines.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    text = stdout.strip()\\n    if not text:\\n        return []\\n    if text.startswith(\\\"[\\\"):\\n        try:\\n            data = json.loads(text)\\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:\\n            raise EngineCrash(E_INPUTS, f\\\"inputs source: broken JSON array: {exc}\\\",\\n                              node=node_name)\\n        if not isinstance(data, list):\\n            raise EngineCrash(E_INPUTS, \\\"inputs source: JSON is not an array\\\", node=node_name)\\n        return data\\n    if text.startswith(\\\"{\\\"):\\n        rows = []\\n        for n, line in enumerate(text.splitlines(), start=1):\\n            line = line.strip()\\n            if not line:\\n                continue\\n            try:\\n                rows.append(json.loads(line))\\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:\\n                raise EngineCrash(E_INPUTS, f\\\"inputs source: broken JSON on line {n}: {exc}\\\",\\n                                  node=node_name)\\n        return rows\\n    return [line.strip() for line in text.splitlines() if line.strip()]\\n\\n\\n# ── structured signal scan (foundation for pool manifests) ──────────────────\\n\\n@dataclass\\nclass ScanResult:\\n    first_known: str | None = None\\n    first_body: str = \\\"\\\"\\n    vars: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)\\n    updates: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\\n\\n\\ndef scan_stdout(stdout: str, known: set[str] | None) -> ScanResult:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"stdout only — stderr never routes. Engine facts are excluded from `known`\\n    upstream: a body printing <signal:__failed__> must never route (namespace law).\\n\\n    known=None is pool mode: record the first ANY bare user signal (pool routing\\n    tables hold only dunders, yet body signals must reach the manifest).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    res = ScanResult()\\n    for name, attrs, body in extract_signals(stdout):\\n        if name == \\\"update\\\":\\n            res.updates.append(body)\\n            continue\\n        if name == \\\"var\\\":\\n            key = (attrs or {}).get(\\\"key\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n            if key and body:\\n                res.vars[key] = body\\n            continue\\n        if res.first_known is not None:\\n            continue\\n        if known is None:\\n            if name not in CHANNEL_SIGNALS and not name.startswith(\\\"__\\\"):\\n                res.first_known, res.first_body = name, body\\n        elif name in known:\\n            res.first_known, res.first_body = name, body\\n    return res\\n\\n\\n@dataclass\\nclass AttemptsOutcome:\\n    signal: str | None               # user signal | __failed__ | __default__ | None (pool silent ok)\\n    message: str\\n    attempts: int                    # total executions across phases (0 = pre decided)\\n    attempts_primary: int = 0\\n    attempts_fallback: int = 0\\n    rc: int | None = None\\n    timed_out: bool = False\\n    fallback_used: bool = False\\n    concluding_phase: str | None = None   # \\\"primary\\\" | \\\"fallback\\\" | None (pre decided)\\n    harness: str | None = None            # harness that produced the concluding outcome\\n    model: str | None = None\\n    guarded: bool = False                 # pre emitted a routing signal; body never ran\\n    failure_class: str | None = None      # for __failed__: \\\"pre\\\" | \\\"rc\\\" | \\\"timeout\\\" | \\\"post\\\"\\n    recorded_signal: str | None = None    # pool: first bare signal seen (data, never outcome)\\n    recorded_body: str = \\\"\\\"\\n    pending_vars: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)  # fold law: caller applies\\n    updates: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\\n\\n\\nclass Engine:\\n    def __init__(self, pipeline: Pipeline, store: RunStore, workdir: Path):\\n        self.p = pipeline\\n        self.store = store\\n        self.workdir = workdir\\n        self.vars: dict[str, str] = dict(pipeline.vars)\\n        self.last: dict = {}\\n        self.deadline: float | None = (\\n            time.monotonic() + pipeline.timeout if pipeline.timeout else None\\n        )\\n        self.steps = 0\\n        self.manifests: dict[str, Path] = {}   # node -> manifest path (engine map, not vars)\\n\\n    # ── deadline ──\\n    def _remaining(self) -> float | None:\\n        if self.deadline is None:\\n            return None\\n        return self.deadline - time.monotonic()\\n\\n    def _check_deadline(self) -> None:\\n        rem = self._remaining()\\n        if rem is not None and rem <= 0:\\n            raise EngineCrash(E_DEADLINE, f\\\"pipeline timeout ({self.p.timeout}s) exhausted\\\")\\n\\n    def _clamp(self, timeout_s: float) -> float:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Clamp a child timeout to the remaining budget (clamp, not crash).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        rem = self._remaining()\\n        if rem is None:\\n            return float(timeout_s)\\n        if rem <= 0:\\n            raise EngineCrash(E_DEADLINE, f\\\"pipeline timeout ({self.p.timeout}s) exhausted\\\")\\n        return min(float(timeout_s), rem)\\n\\n    # ── env ──\\n    def _base_env(self, vars_map: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> dict[str, str]:\\n        env = dict(self.vars if vars_map is None else vars_map)\\n        env[\\\"MEDULLA_RUN_ID\\\"] = self.store.run_id\\n        env[\\\"MEDULLA_RUN_DIR\\\"] = str(self.store.dir)\\n        for node_name, path in self.manifests.items():\\n            env[f\\\"MEDULLA_MANIFEST_{node_name.upper().replace('-', '_')}\\\"] = str(path)\\n        if self.last:\\n            env[\\\"MEDULLA_LAST_NODE\\\"] = str(self.last.get(\\\"node\\\", \\\"\\\"))\\n            env[\\\"MEDULLA_LAST_SIGNAL\\\"] = str(self.last.get(\\\"signal\\\", \\\"\\\"))\\n            env[\\\"MEDULLA_LAST_MESSAGE\\\"] = str(self.last.get(\\\"message\\\", \\\"\\\"))\\n            env[\\\"MEDULLA_LAST_RC\\\"] = str(self.last.get(\\\"rc\\\", \\\"\\\"))\\n            env[\\\"MEDULLA_LAST_EVENT_JSON\\\"] = json.dumps(self.last, ensure_ascii=False)\\n        return env\\n\\n    # ── vars (fold law application point) ──\\n    @staticmethod\\n    def _valid_var_key(key: str) -> bool:\\n        return bool(VAR_NAME_RE.match(key)) and key not in ENV_BLACKLIST_EXACT and \\\\\\n            not any(key.startswith(p) for p in ENV_BLACKLIST_PREFIX)\\n\\n    def _apply_vars(self, pending: dict[str, str]) -> None:\\n        if not pending:\\n            return\\n        for key, value in pending.items():\\n            if not self._valid_var_key(key):\\n                log(f\\\"warn: var '{key}' rejected (reserved/invalid name)\\\")\\n                continue\\n            self.vars[key] = value\\n        self.store.write_vars(self.vars)\\n\\n    # ── render helpers ──\\n    def _known(self, node: Node) -> set[str]:\\n        return self.p.known_signals(node) - set(CHANNEL_SIGNALS) - set(ENGINE_FACTS)\\n\\n    def _render_or_crash(self, text: str, node: Node, what: str, required: bool = True) -> str:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Decision-context render: any breakage is a pipeline bug -> E_RENDER.\\n        required=False: an optional field rendering empty counts as absent (contract).\\n        Part-3 pools pass their own render_fn with fail-the-input semantics.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        try:\\n            rendered = render(text, self.p.dir, self.vars, last=self.last)\\n        except RenderError as exc:\\n            raise EngineCrash(E_RENDER, f\\\"{what}: {exc}\\\", node=node.name)\\n        if required and not rendered.strip():\\n            raise EngineCrash(E_RENDER, f\\\"{what} rendered empty (broken template or empty field)\\\",\\n                              node=node.name)\\n        return rendered\\n\\n    # ── the attempts seam ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n    # Owns the FULL hook machinery (panel: pre and post live INSIDE the seam so\\n    # part-3 pool workers get identical semantics by calling this per input):\\n    #   pre -> [guard?] -> body attempts (primary -> fallback) with post per attempt.\\n    # The seam never mutates engine state: `apply_pre_vars` is the caller's policy\\n    # (decision: apply to self.vars so the body render sees them; pool at\\n    # max_parallel>1: record to manifest + local ctx), and body/post vars come\\n    # back as pending_vars for the caller to apply per the fold law.\\n    def _run_attempts(\\n        self,\\n        node: Node,\\n        step_dir: Path,\\n        render_fn,\\n        apply_pre_vars,\\n        attempt_ns: str,\\n        known: set[str] | None,\\n        env_fn=None,\\n        pool_mode: bool = False,\\n    ) -> AttemptsOutcome:\\n        # env_fn: callable -> dict, the base env for hooks and bodies. Decision nodes\\n        # default to self._base_env (pre vars land in self.vars and are picked up on\\n        # the next call); part-3 pool workers pass their own (base + input ctx + local\\n        # pre-vars overlay) so parallel inputs never touch shared engine state.\\n        if env_fn is None:\\n            env_fn = self._base_env\\n        action = node.action\\n\\n        pre_updates: list[str] = []\\n        primary_tag = \\\"shell\\\" if action.kind == \\\"shell\\\" else action.agent.harness\\n        if node.pre is not None:\\n            pre_rendered = render_fn(node.pre, \\\"pre\\\")\\n            hook_timeout = self._clamp(HOOK_TIMEOUT_S)\\n            pre_env = {**env_fn(),\\n                       \\\"MEDULLA_TIMEOUT_S\\\": _timeout_env(hook_timeout),\\n                       \\\"MEDULLA_HARNESS\\\": primary_tag}\\n            pre_res = proc_run(pre_rendered, self.workdir, hook_timeout,\\n                               extra_env=pre_env, log_path=step_dir / \\\"pre.txt\\\")\\n            pre_scan = scan_stdout(pre_res.stdout, known)\\n            pre_updates = pre_scan.updates\\n            # a known signal wins over rc — same grammar as everywhere else\\n            if pre_scan.first_known is not None:\\n                apply_pre_vars(pre_scan.vars)     # env prep applies before the guard routes\\n                return AttemptsOutcome(           # guard: body and post are skipped\\n                    signal=pre_scan.first_known, message=pre_scan.first_body,\\n                    attempts=0, rc=pre_res.rc, guarded=True, updates=pre_updates,\\n                )\\n            if pre_res.rc != 0:\\n                return AttemptsOutcome(\\n                    signal=SIG_FAILED,\\n                    message=f\\\"pre hook failed: rc={pre_res.rc}; stderr: {_tail(pre_res.stderr)}\\\",\\n                    attempts=0, rc=pre_res.rc, timed_out=pre_res.timed_out,\\n                    failure_class=\\\"pre\\\", updates=pre_updates,\\n                )\\n            apply_pre_vars(pre_scan.vars)   # env prep BEFORE the body renders\\n\\n        post_rendered = render_fn(node.post, \\\"post\\\") if node.post else None\\n\\n        current = action\\n        phase = \\\"primary\\\"\\n        fallback = self.p.action_fallback(action) if action.kind == \\\"agent\\\" else None\\n        fallback_used = False\\n        # contract: \\\"primary gets N, then fallback gets N\\\" — a fallback without its\\n        # own max_attempts inherits the primary's effective budget\\n        phase_budget = self.p.action_max_attempts(action)\\n\\n        invoke, prompt_text, agent_spec = self._prepare_body(\\n            current, node, step_dir, render_fn, phase)\\n        harness_name = agent_spec.harness if agent_spec else None\\n\\n        attempt = 0\\n        total = 0\\n        n_primary = 0\\n        n_fallback = 0\\n        last_failure_class: str | None = None\\n        while True:\\n            attempt += 1\\n            total += 1\\n            if phase == \\\"primary\\\":\\n                n_primary += 1\\n            else:\\n                n_fallback += 1\\n            self._check_deadline()\\n            eff = self._clamp(self.p.action_timeout(current))\\n            attempt_id = f\\\"{attempt_ns}.{phase[0]}{attempt}\\\"\\n            tag = \\\"shell\\\" if current.kind == \\\"shell\\\" else agent_spec.harness\\n            env = {**env_fn(),\\n                   \\\"MEDULLA_TIMEOUT_S\\\": _timeout_env(eff),\\n                   \\\"MEDULLA_ATTEMPT_ID\\\": attempt_id,\\n                   \\\"MEDULLA_HARNESS\\\": tag,\\n                   **invoke.env}\\n\\n            result = proc_run(invoke.argv, self.workdir, eff, extra_env=env,\\n                              log_path=step_dir / f\\\"attempt-{total}-{tag}.txt\\\",\\n                              stdin_data=invoke.stdin, env_remove=invoke.env_remove,\\n                              merge_stderr=invoke.merge_stderr)\\n\\n            raw_text = result.stdout\\n            if current.kind == \\\"agent\\\":\\n                raw_text = resolve_harness(agent_spec).filter_stdout(raw_text)\\n            body_scan = scan_stdout(raw_text, known)\\n\\n            post_rc = post_signal = None\\n            post_scan = ScanResult()\\n            if post_rendered is not None:\\n                hook_timeout = self._clamp(HOOK_TIMEOUT_S)\\n                post_env = {**env,\\n                            \\\"MEDULLA_TIMEOUT_S\\\": _timeout_env(hook_timeout),\\n                            \\\"MEDULLA_BODY_RC\\\": str(result.rc),\\n                            \\\"MEDULLA_BODY_SIGNAL\\\": body_scan.first_known or \\\"\\\"}\\n                post_res = proc_run(post_rendered, self.workdir,\\n                                    hook_timeout, extra_env=post_env,\\n                                    log_path=step_dir / f\\\"post-{total}.txt\\\")\\n                post_scan = scan_stdout(post_res.stdout, known)\\n                post_rc, post_signal = post_res.rc, post_scan.first_known\\n\\n            # Pool conjunction law: ok = rc==0 AND no timeout AND no post veto.\\n            # Signals are DATA in pools — they are recorded, they never classify\\n            # (\\\"echo <signal:x>; exit 7\\\" must not become ok). ignore_exit_code\\n            # never reaches pools, not even via defaults (min_success owns that).\\n            decision = classify_attempt(\\n                kind=current.kind, rc=result.rc, timed_out=result.timed_out,\\n                body_signal=None if pool_mode else body_scan.first_known,\\n                post_rc=post_rc,\\n                post_signal=None if pool_mode else post_signal,\\n                ignore_exit_code=(False if pool_mode\\n                                  else self.p.action_ignore_exit_code(current)),\\n            )\\n            move = next_move(\\n                decision, kind=current.kind, phase=phase, attempt=attempt,\\n                max_attempts=phase_budget,\\n                has_fallback=fallback is not None,\\n                pool_mode=pool_mode,\\n            )\\n            if decision.failure_class is not None:\\n                last_failure_class = decision.failure_class\\n\\n            if move.move is Move.RETRY_SAME:\\n                log(f\\\"attempt {attempt_id} failed (rc={result.rc}), retrying\\\")\\n                _retry_delay()      # a zero-delay retry on a 429 is a provider-ban request\\n                continue\\n            if move.move is Move.SWITCH_FALLBACK:\\n                log(f\\\"attempt {attempt_id} failed (rc={result.rc}), switching to fallback\\\")\\n                _retry_delay()\\n                current = fallback\\n                phase = \\\"fallback\\\"\\n                attempt = 0\\n                fallback_used = True\\n                if fallback.max_attempts is not None:\\n                    phase_budget = fallback.max_attempts\\n                invoke, prompt_text, agent_spec = self._prepare_body(\\n                    current, node, step_dir, render_fn, phase, inherited_prompt=prompt_text)\\n                harness_name = agent_spec.harness if agent_spec else None\\n                continue\\n\\n            # DONE — state signals are collected from a successful outcome only:\\n            # a ROUTED signal, or a pool's silent-ok (its vars ARE successful vars —\\n            # dropping them would make row.vars lie). A __default__ conclusion is a\\n            # communication failure: its vars must not leak (fold law).\\n            pending: dict[str, str] = {}\\n            updates = pre_updates + body_scan.updates + post_scan.updates\\n            silent_ok = pool_mode and decision.verdict is Verdict.SILENT\\n            if decision.verdict is Verdict.ROUTE or silent_ok:\\n                pending = {**body_scan.vars, **post_scan.vars}   # post wins on conflict\\n\\n            signal = move.signal\\n            if signal == SIG_FAILED:\\n                message = (f\\\"body died: rc={result.rc}, {total} attempt(s)\\\"\\n                           f\\\"{' (fallback tried)' if fallback_used else ''}; \\\"\\n                           f\\\"stderr: {_tail(result.stderr)}\\\")\\n                if current.kind == \\\"agent\\\":\\n                    # harness-mined failure detail (codex error/turn.failed lives in\\n                    # stdout JSON the signal filter rightly drops)\\n                    detail = resolve_harness(agent_spec).extract_error(result.stdout)\\n                    if detail:\\n                        message += f\\\"; {detail}\\\"\\n            elif signal == SIG_DEFAULT:\\n                message = f\\\"no known signal emitted; stdout: {_tail(result.stdout)}\\\"\\n            elif signal is None:\\n                message = \\\"\\\"                        # pool silent ok\\n            elif post_signal is not None and signal == post_signal:\\n                message = post_scan.first_body\\n            else:\\n                message = body_scan.first_body\\n            return AttemptsOutcome(\\n                signal=signal, message=message, attempts=total,\\n                attempts_primary=n_primary, attempts_fallback=n_fallback,\\n                rc=result.rc, timed_out=result.timed_out,\\n                fallback_used=fallback_used, concluding_phase=phase,\\n                harness=harness_name,\\n                model=agent_spec.model if agent_spec else None,\\n                failure_class=last_failure_class if signal == SIG_FAILED else None,\\n                recorded_signal=post_scan.first_known or body_scan.first_known,\\n                recorded_body=post_scan.first_body or body_scan.first_body,\\n                pending_vars=pending, updates=updates,\\n            )\\n\\n    def _prepare_body(self, action: Action, node: Node, step_dir: Path,\\n                      render_fn, phase: str, inherited_prompt: str | None = None):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Returns (Invoke, rendered_prompt_text_or_None, rendered_AgentSpec_or_None).\\n\\n        Every scalar agent field is a template (contract: an ensemble is just a pool\\n        with per-input harness/model). Optional fields rendering empty count as absent.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        from .harness import Invoke\\n        if action.kind == \\\"shell\\\":\\n            shell = os.environ.get(\\\"SHELL\\\", \\\"bash\\\")\\n            rendered = render_fn(action.shell, \\\"shell\\\")\\n            return Invoke(argv=[shell, \\\"-lc\\\", rendered]), None, None\\n\\n        spec = action.agent\\n        harness = render_fn(spec.harness, \\\"agent.harness\\\").strip()\\n        model = render_fn(spec.model, \\\"agent.model\\\", required=False) if spec.model else None\\n        effort = render_fn(spec.effort, \\\"agent.effort\\\", required=False) if spec.effort else None\\n        # an arg rendering empty is absent (never the literal template text back)\\n        args = [r for a in spec.args\\n                if (r := render_fn(a, \\\"agent.args\\\", required=False)).strip()]\\n        from .model import AgentSpec\\n        rendered_spec = AgentSpec(harness=harness, model=model or None,\\n                                  effort=effort or None, args=args)\\n\\n        adapter = resolve_harness(rendered_spec)\\n        adapter.prepare(rendered_spec, self.workdir)   # idempotent preflight (agy trust, opencode.json)\\n        if action.prompt is not None:\\n            prompt_text = render_fn(action.prompt, \\\"prompt\\\")\\n        elif inherited_prompt is not None:\\n            prompt_text = inherited_prompt      # fallback reuses the primary's rendered prompt\\n        else:\\n            raise EngineCrash(E_RENDER, \\\"agent action has no prompt\\\", node=node.name)\\n        prompt_file = step_dir / (\\\"prompt.md\\\" if phase == \\\"primary\\\" else \\\"prompt-fallback.md\\\")\\n        # the protocol must ride in whatever text actually reaches the agent —\\n        # file (claude), stdin (codex) AND argv (opencode/agy). Battle test t2\\n        # found it riding in the file only: stdin/argv harnesses never saw it.\\n        # inherited prompt_text stays clean so a fallback doesn't double-stamp.\\n        full_prompt = prompt_text + SIGNAL_PROTOCOL\\n        prompt_file.write_text(full_prompt, encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n        timeout_s = self._clamp(self.p.action_timeout(action))\\n        invoke = adapter.build(rendered_spec, prompt_file, full_prompt, timeout_s)\\n        return invoke, prompt_text, rendered_spec\\n\\n    # ── decision node: the seam + decision-node policy (fold law application) ──\\n    def _run_decision(self, node: Node, step_dir: Path, step_no: int):\\n        known = self._known(node)\\n        render_fn = lambda text, what, required=True: self._render_or_crash(\\n            text, node, what, required)\\n        outcome = self._run_attempts(\\n            node, step_dir, render_fn,\\n            apply_pre_vars=self._apply_vars,    # decision = max_parallel 1: vars apply live\\n            attempt_ns=f\\\"{step_no:03d}\\\", known=known,\\n        )\\n        if outcome.timed_out and (rem := self._remaining()) is not None and rem <= 0:\\n            # killed by the exhausted RUN budget, not its own timeout: a __failed__\\n            # here would be exit 2 (not resumable) — this is E_DEADLINE, same law\\n            # as deadline-killed pool inputs\\n            raise EngineCrash(E_DEADLINE,\\n                              f\\\"pipeline timeout ({self.p.timeout}s) exhausted during \\\"\\n                              f\\\"node '{node.name}'\\\", node=node.name)\\n        for u in outcome.updates:\\n            log(f\\\"update: {u}\\\")\\n        self._apply_vars(outcome.pending_vars)  # body/post vars: routed outcome only\\n        return outcome.signal, outcome.message, {\\n            \\\"attempts\\\": outcome.attempts, \\\"rc\\\": outcome.rc, \\\"timed_out\\\": outcome.timed_out,\\n            \\\"fallback\\\": outcome.fallback_used, \\\"harness\\\": outcome.harness,\\n            \\\"model\\\": outcome.model,\\n        }\\n\\n    # ── pool machinery ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n    def _materialize_inputs(self, node: Node, step_dir: Path) -> list:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Snapshot inputs into steps/NNN-<node>/inputs.json (resume foundation).\\n        An existing snapshot short-circuits everything: sources are NEVER\\n        re-executed on resume (contract), and the caps/kind checks already passed.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        snapshot = step_dir / \\\"inputs.json\\\"\\n        if snapshot.is_file():\\n            return json.loads(snapshot.read_text(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\"))\\n        spec = node.pool.inputs\\n        if spec.data is not None:\\n            inputs = list(spec.data)\\n        else:\\n            cmd = self._render_or_crash(spec.shell, node, \\\"inputs.shell\\\")\\n            res = proc_run(cmd, self.workdir, self._clamp(spec.shell_timeout),\\n                           extra_env=self._base_env(),\\n                           log_path=step_dir / \\\"inputs-source.txt\\\")\\n            if res.timed_out:\\n                rem = self._remaining()\\n                if rem is not None and rem <= 0:      # the run budget killed it, not its own limit\\n                    raise EngineCrash(E_DEADLINE,\\n                                      f\\\"pipeline timeout ({self.p.timeout}s) exhausted \\\"\\n                                      f\\\"while sourcing inputs\\\", node=node.name)\\n                raise EngineCrash(E_INPUTS, f\\\"inputs source timed out ({spec.shell_timeout}s)\\\",\\n                                  node=node.name)\\n            if res.rc != 0:\\n                raise EngineCrash(\\n                    E_INPUTS,\\n                    f\\\"inputs source exited rc={res.rc} (a broken producer is not an \\\"\\n                    f\\\"empty queue); stderr: {_tail(res.stderr)}\\\",\\n                    node=node.name)\\n            inputs = _sniff_inputs(res.stdout, node.name)\\n        if len(inputs) > INPUTS_HARD_CAP:\\n            raise EngineCrash(E_INPUTS_LIMIT,\\n                              f\\\"{len(inputs)} inputs (cap {INPUTS_HARD_CAP}) — \\\"\\n                              f\\\"this is almost certainly not what you wanted\\\",\\n                              node=node.name)\\n        if any(isinstance(v, list) for v in inputs):\\n            raise EngineCrash(E_INPUTS, \\\"array inputs are forbidden (wrap in an object)\\\",\\n                              node=node.name)\\n        kinds = {isinstance(v, dict) for v in inputs}\\n        if len(kinds) > 1:\\n            raise EngineCrash(E_INPUTS, \\\"mixed scalar/object inputs from source\\\",\\n                              node=node.name)\\n        (step_dir / \\\"inputs.json\\\").write_text(\\n            json.dumps(inputs, ensure_ascii=False, indent=1), encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n        return inputs\\n\\n    def _run_one_input(self, node: Node, step_dir: Path, step_no: int,\\n                       idx: int, value, count: int,\\n                       pool_vars: dict[str, str], sequential: bool) -> dict:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Execute one input through the seam; returns a manifest row.\\n        Never raises for input-level failures; deadline crashes propagate.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        t0 = time.monotonic()\\n        input_dir = step_dir / f\\\"input-{idx:04d}\\\"     # per-input namespace: no file races\\n        input_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\\n        key = f\\\"{idx}:{_input_hash(value)}\\\"\\n        local_ctx: dict[str, str] = {}\\n\\n        input_env = {\\n            \\\"MEDULLA_INPUT\\\": value if isinstance(value, str) else json.dumps(value, ensure_ascii=False),\\n            \\\"MEDULLA_INPUT_INDEX\\\": str(idx),\\n            \\\"MEDULLA_INPUT_COUNT\\\": str(count),\\n            \\\"MEDULLA_INPUT_KEY\\\": key,\\n        }\\n        if isinstance(value, dict):\\n            for k, v in value.items():\\n                if isinstance(v, (str, int, float, bool)) and VAR_NAME_RE.match(str(k)):\\n                    suffix = str(k).upper()\\n                    if suffix not in (\\\"INDEX\\\", \\\"COUNT\\\", \\\"KEY\\\"):\\n                        input_env[f\\\"MEDULLA_INPUT_{suffix}\\\"] = str(v)\\n\\n        def render_fn(text: str, what: str, required: bool = True) -> str:\\n            # pool-input render semantics: breakage fails THIS input, never the run\\n            merged = {**pool_vars, **local_ctx} if not sequential else self.vars\\n            rendered = render(text, self.p.dir, merged,\\n                              input_value=value, has_input=True,\\n                              input_index=idx, input_count=count, last=self.last)\\n            if required and not rendered.strip():\\n                raise RenderError(f\\\"{what} rendered empty\\\")\\n            return rendered\\n\\n        def env_fn() -> dict[str, str]:\\n            base = self._base_env(None if sequential else {**pool_vars, **local_ctx})\\n            return {**base, **input_env}\\n\\n        def apply_pre_vars(pending: dict[str, str]) -> None:\\n            if sequential:\\n                self._apply_vars(pending)             # fold: ordered, not transactional\\n            else:\\n                # same blacklist as the sequential path — a parallel pre emitting\\n                # <signal:var key=PATH> must not poison this worker's subprocess env\\n                for k, v in pending.items():\\n                    if self._valid_var_key(k):\\n                        local_ctx[k] = v\\n                    else:\\n                        log(f\\\"warn: var '{k}' rejected (reserved/invalid name)\\\")\\n\\n        row = {\\\"index\\\": idx, \\\"key\\\": key, \\\"input\\\": value}\\n        try:\\n            outcome = self._run_attempts(\\n                node, input_dir, render_fn, apply_pre_vars,\\n                attempt_ns=f\\\"{step_no:03d}.i{idx}\\\", known=None,\\n                env_fn=env_fn, pool_mode=True,\\n            )\\n        except RenderError as exc:\\n            row.update(ok=False, reason=\\\"render\\\", signal=None, message=str(exc),\\n                       rc=None,          return row\\n\\n        ok = outcome.signal not in (SIG_FAILED, SIG_DEFAULT)\\n        if outcome.guarded:\\n            reason = \\\"guard\\\"\\n        elif not ok:\\n            reason = outcome.failure_class or \\\"rc\\\"\\n        else:\\n            reason = \\\"ok\\\"\\n        # pool signals are data: surface the recorded bare signal in the row\\n        row_signal = outcome.signal if outcome.guarded else outcome.recorded_signal\\n        row_message = outcome.message if (outcome.guarded or not ok) else outcome.recorded_body\\n        pool_pre_vars = dict(local_ctx)               # >1: pre vars are row data too\\n        if sequential and ok:\\n            self._apply_vars(outcome.pending_vars)    # fold: next input sees them\\n updates=outcome.updates,\\n            duration_s=round(time.monotonic() - t0, 2),\\n            log=f\\\"input-{idx:04d}/\\\",\\n        )\\n        return row\\n\\n    def _run_pool(self, node: Node, step_dir: Path, step_no: int):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Returns (signal, message, stats).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        manifest_path = step_dir / \\\"manifest.jsonl\\\"\\n        manifest_path.touch()                          # empty pools still register a manifest\\n        self.manifests[node.name] = manifest_path\\n\\n        inputs = self._materialize_inputs(node, step_dir)\\n        total = len(inputs)\\n        pool = node.pool\\n        min_success = total if pool.min_success is None else pool.min_success\\n        if total == 0:\\n            return SIG_EMPTY, \\\"source returned 0 inputs\\\", {\\n                \\\"inputs_total\\\": 0, \\\"inputs_ok\\\": 0, \\\"min_success\\\": min_success}\\n\\n        pool_vars = dict(self.vars)                    # snapshot: workers never read live vars\\n        workers = min(total, pool.max_parallel or total)\\n        sequential = workers == 1\\n        deadline_hit = False\\n\\n        # resume: seed the done-mask from existing rows — identity is (index, key),\\n        # never index alone (a changed input at the same index must re-run)\\n        old_rows = self.store.read_manifest(manifest_path)\\n        done = {(r.get(\\\"index\\\"), r.get(\\\"key\\\")) for r in old_rows if r.get(\\\"ok\\\")}\\n        rows: list[dict] = list(old_rows)\\n        pending_inputs = [\\n            (i, v) for i, v in enumerate(inputs, start=1)\\n            if (i, f\\\"{i}:{_input_hash(v)}\\\") not in done\\n        ]\\n        if old_rows:\\n            log(f\\\"pool resume: {len(done)} inputs done, {len(pending_inputs)} to run\\\")\\n\\n        def guarded_run(idx: int, value):\\n            if self._remaining() is not None and self._remaining() <= 0:\\n                return None                            # never started: no row, resume re-runs it\\n            try:\\n                row = self._run_one_input(node, step_dir, step_no, idx, value,\\n                                          total, pool_vars, sequential)\\n            except EngineCrash as crash:\\n                if crash.code == E_DEADLINE:\\n                    return None                        # died of budget, not of its own timeout\\n                raise\\n            if row.get(\\\"timed_out\\\") and not row[\\\"ok\\\"] \\\\\\n                    and self._remaining() is not None and self._remaining() <= 0:\\n                # killed by the shrinking run budget, not its own timeout: recording\\n                # this as reason:timeout would stop resume from ever re-running it\\n                return None\\n            return row\\n\\n        if sequential:\\n            for i, value in pending_inputs:\\n                row = guarded_run(i, value)\\n                if row is None:\\n                    deadline_hit = True\\n                    break\\n                self.store.manifest_append(manifest_path, row)\\n                rows.append(row)\\n        else:\\n            import concurrent.futures\\n            first_crash: EngineCrash | None = None\\n            with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=workers) as pool_exec:\\n                futures = {pool_exec.submit(guarded_run, i, v): i\\n                           for i, v in pending_inputs}\\n                for fut in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures):\\n                    try:\\n                        row = fut.result()\\n                    except EngineCrash as crash:\\n                        # collect the rest before crashing — concluded inputs must not\\n                        # lose their manifest rows to an unrelated worker's crash\\n                        if first_crash is None:\\n                            first_crash = crash\\n                        continue\\n                    if row is None:\\n                        deadline_hit = True\\n                        continue\\n                    self.store.manifest_append(manifest_path, row)\\n                    rows.append(row)\\n            if first_crash is not None:\\n                raise first_crash\\n\\n        if deadline_hit or (self._remaining() is not None and self._remaining() <= 0):\\n            raise EngineCrash(E_DEADLINE,\\n                              f\\\"pipeline timeout ({self.p.timeout}s) exhausted mid-pool \\\"\\n                              f\\\"({len(rows)}/{total} inputs concluded; manifest rows survive)\\\",\\n                              node=node.name)\\n\\n        # join over old + new rows, keyed by input identity: an input is ok iff\\n        # ANY row matches its (index, key) with ok=true (stale/orphan rows inert)\\n        ok_keys = {(r.get(\\\"index\\\"), r.get(\\\"key\\\")) for r in rows if r.get(\\\"ok\\\")}\\n        input_keys = [(i, f\\\"{i}:{_input_hash(v)}\\\") for i, v in enumerate(inputs, start=1)]\\n        ok_count = sum(1 for ik in input_keys if ik in ok_keys)\\n        stats = {\\\"inputs_total\\\": total, \\\"inputs_ok\\\": ok_count, \\\"min_success\\\": min_success}\\n        if ok_count >= min_success:\\n            return SIG_DONE, f\\\"{ok_count}/{total} inputs ok\\\", stats\\n        by_class: ik in ok_keys:\\n                continue\\n            row = latest_by_key.get(ik)\\n            reason = row[\\\"reason\\\"] if row else \\\"missing\\\"\\n            by_class[reason] = by_class.get(reason, 0) + 1\\n        breakdown = \\\", \\\".join(f\\\"{k} x{v}\\\" for k, v in sorted(by_class.items()))\\n        ms_text = \\\"all\\\" if pool.min_success is None else str(min_success)\\n        return SIG_FAILED, (f\\\"{ok_count}/{total} inputs ok (min_success={ms_text}); \\\"\\n                            f\\\"failures: {breakdown}\\\"), stats\\n\\n    # ── The journal logs COMPLETED steps only,\\n        so the last row's `next` IS the interrupted/never-started node.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        rows = self.store.read_journal()\\n        saved_vars = journal\\\")\\n        log(f\\\"resume: {len(rows)} completed step(s), continuing at '{current}'\\\")\\n        return current\\n\\n    # ── main loop ──\\n    def run(self, start_override: str | None Notable inherited scars:\\n- agy: `--print` CONSUMES THE NEXT TOKEN as the prompt — it must be the last\\n  flag; an untrusted workspace makes `--dangerously-skip-permissions` hang\\n  forever, so trust is preflighted (outside Docker).\\n- codex: `--full-auto` is deprecated AND sandboxed — never use it; prompt goes\\n  via stdin (no ARG_MAX, no @file coupling); prefer the `cx` token-refreshing\\n  wrapper when present.\\n- claude: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is stripped (OAuth account must win); prompt is\\n  delivered as a system-prompt FILE, `-p \\\"Execute.\\\"` stays tiny.\\n- opencode: permissions/effort/timeout live in opencode.json, bootstrapped\\n  idempotently (never clobbering an author's config).\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport json\\nimport os\\nimport shutil\\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\n\\nfrom .errors import EngineCrash, E_HARNESS, E_INTERNAL\\nfrom .model import AgentSpec\\n\\nREAL_HARNESSES = (\\\"claude-code\\\", \\\"codex\\\", \\\"opencode\\\", \\\"agy\\\")\\n\\nimport re as _re\\n\\n_ANSI_RE = _re.compile(r\\\"\\\\x1b\\\\[[0-9;]*[A-Za-z]\\\")\\n\\n_LINE_START_SIGNAL_RE = _re.compile(\\n    r\\\"(?ms)^[ \\\\t]*(<signal:([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)[^>]*>.*?</signal:\\\\2>)\\\")\\n\\n\\ndef plain_text_signal_filter(stdout: str) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Defense for CLIs WITHOUT structured output (opencode, agy): keep only\\n    signal tags that START a line. Tool output echoing a tag mid-line\\n    (\\\"$ cat notes.md: <signal:done>...\\\") is dropped — an identity filter let\\n    any cat'd file route the graph (audit R3). Residual risk: a file whose\\n    line IS a bare tag still leaks; the structured filter (part-7 live logs)\\n    is the real fix, this is the best available heuristic until then.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    return \\\"\\\\n\\\".join(m.group(1) for m in _LINE_START_SIGNAL_RE.finditer(stdout))\\n\\n# extra slack for a CLI's INNER timeout so the engine's own timeout always\\n# fires first and the CLI limit is just a net (v1 convention)\\nINNER_SLACK_S = 300\\n\\n\\n@dataclass\\nclass Invoke:\\n    argv: list[str]\\n    stdin: str | None = None                      # piped to the child's stdin\\n    env: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)   # merged over the engine env\\n    env_remove: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # stripped from the child env\\n    merge_stderr: bool = False    # CLIs that talk on stderr (opencode); the filter\\n                                  # still gates what can route\\n\\n\\nclass HarnessAdapter:\\n    name = \\\"abstract\\\"\\n    binary = \\\"\\\"            # shutil.which target; \\\"\\\" skips the check (fake)\\n\\n    def __init__(self):\\n        if self.binary and not shutil.which(self.binary):\\n            raise EngineCrash(\\n                E_HARNESS, f\\\"harness '{self.name}': binary '{self.binary}' not on PATH\\\")\\n\\n    def prepare(self, spec: AgentSpec, workdir: Path) -> None:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Idempotent preflight/setup before a phase's first attempt. May raise\\n        E_HARNESS only for unresolvable conditions (the razor).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def build(self, spec: AgentSpec, prompt_file: Path, prompt_text: str,\\n              timeout_s: float) -> Invoke:\\n        raise NotImplementedError\\n\\n    def filter_stdout(self, stdout: str) -> str:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Reduce raw CLI output to signal-scannable assistant text.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        return stdout\\n\\n    def extract_error(self, stdout: str) -> str | None:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Harness-specific failure detail mined from raw stdout (NOT the signal\\n        channel — appended to the __failed__ message only). the OAuth account must win\\n\\n    def filter_stdout(self, stdout: str) -> str:\\n        parts: list[str] = []\\n        for line in stdout.splitlines():\\n            line = line.strip()\\n            if not line or not line.startswith(\\\"{\\\"):\\n                continue                    # non-JSON preamble is never assistant text\\n            try:\\n                event = json.loads(line)\\n            except json.JSONDecodeError:\\n                continue\\n            etype = event.get(\\\"type\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n            if etype == \\\"assistant\\\":\\n                for block in event.get(\\\"message\\\", {}).get(\\\"content\\\", []):\\n                    if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get(\\\"type\\\") == \\\"text\\\":\\n                        parts.append(block.get(\\\"text\\\", \\\"\\\"))\\n            elif etype == \\\"content_block_delta\\\":\\n                delta = event.get(\\\"delta\\\", {})\\n                if delta.get(\\\"type\\\") == \\\"text_delta\\\":\\n                    parts.append(delta.get(\\\"text\\\", \\\"\\\"))\\n            elif etype == \\\"result\\\":\\n                raw = event.get(\\\"result\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n                if isinstance(raw, str):\\n                    parts.append(raw)\\n                elif isinstance(raw, dict):         # dict-shaped final result (pilot scar):\\n                    parts.append(raw.get(\\\"output\\\", \\\"\\\"))  # dropping it = permanent __default__\\n            # user messages (tool_result), tool_use blocks, system events: SKIP\\n        return \\\"\\\\n\\\".join(p for p in parts if p)\\n\\n\\n# ── codex ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nclass CodexAdapter(HarnessAdapter):\\n    name = \\\"codex\\\"\\n    binary = \\\"codex\\\"\\n\\n    def __init__(self):\\n        # cx (the token-refreshing wrapper) alone is a valid install (audit G9):\\n        # a slim image with only cx must not crash E_HARNESS at boot\\n        if not (shutil.which(\\\"cx\\\") or shutil.which(\\\"codex\\\")):\\n            raise EngineCrash(E_HARNESS,\\n                              \\\"harness 'codex': neither 'cx' nor 'codex' on PATH\\\")\\n\\n    def build(self, spec, prompt_file, prompt_text, timeout_s):\\n        bin_ = shutil.which(\\\"cx\\\") or \\\"codex\\\"    # cx refreshes the token via the broker\\n        inner_ms = (int(timeout_s) + INNER_SLACK_S) * 1000\\n        argv = [bin_, \\\"exec\\\", \\\"--json\\\",\\n                \\\"--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox\\\",\\n                \\\"--skip-git-repo-check\\\"]\\n        if spec.model:\\n            argv += [\\\"-c\\\", f'model=\\\"{spec.model}\\\"']\\n        if spec.effort:\\n            argv += [\\\"-c\\\", f\\\"model_reasoning_effort={spec.effort}\\\"]\\n        argv += [\\\"-c\\\", f\\\"stream_idle_timeout_ms={inner_ms}\\\"]\\n        argv += spec.args                        # last -c wins: authors can override\\n        # stdin carries the COMPLETE prompt (no \\\"Execute.\\\" prefix needed — that was\\n        # v1's convention for @file references); no ARG_MAX, no @-expansion coupling\\n        return Invoke(argv=argv, stdin=prompt_text)\\n\\n    def extract_error(self, stdout: str) -> str | None:\\n        # codex reports real failure causes as stdout JSON events that the signal\\n        # filter rightly drops; without this, a turn.failed run yields a useless\\n        # __failed__ message (pilot scar: \\\"0 output, exit 1\\\")\\n        for line in stdout.splitlines():\\n            line = line.strip()\\n            if not line.startswith(\\\"{\\\"):\\n                continue\\n            try:\\n                event = json.loads(line)\\n            except json.JSONDecodeError:\\n                continue\\n            if event.get(\\\"type\\\") in (\\\"error\\\", \\\"turn.failed\\\") or event.get(\\\"error\\\"):\\n                detail = event.get(\\\"message\\\") or event.get(\\\"error\\\") or event\\n                if isinstance(detail, (dict, list)):\\n                    detail = json.dumps(detail, ensure_ascii=False)\\n                return f\\\"codex {event.get('type', 'error')}: {detail}\\\"\\n        return None\\n\\n    def filter_stdout(self, stdout: str) -> str:\\n        parts: list[str] = []\\n        for line in stdout.splitlines():\\n            line = line.strip()\\n            if not line.startswith(\\\"{\\\"):\\n                continue\\n            try:\\n                event = json.loads(line)\\n            except json.JSONDecodeError:\\n                continue\\n            if event.get(\\\"type\\\") != \\\"item.completed\\\":\\n                continue\\n            item = event.get(\\\"item\\\", {})\\n            if item.get(\\\"type\\\") == \\\"agent_message\\\":\\n                parts.append(item.get(\\\"text\\\", \\\"\\\"))\\n            # command_execution aggregated_output is TOOL OUTPUT — never scanned.\\n            # (v1's <500-char exception violated the contract; dropped.)\\n        return \\\"\\\\n\\\".join(p for p in parts if p)\\n\\n\\n# ── opencode ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nclass OpenCodeAdapter(HarnessAdapter):\\n    name = \\\"opencode\\\"\\n    binary = \\\"opencode\\\"\\n    _bootstrap_lock = __import__(\\\"threading\\\").Lock()\\n\\n    def prepare(self, spec, workdir):\\n        # The adapter is a cached singleton shared by pool workers: check-then-write\\n        # over a shared path must be locked, and the write atomic (tmp + replace) —\\n        # a concurrent opener must never see a truncated config. First writer wins;\\n        # per-input effort in one workdir shares one config by design (opencode\\n        # reads config per cwd — heterogeneous efforts need per-input workdirs).\\n        with self._bootstrap_lock:\\n            cfg = workdir / \\\"opencode.json\\\"\\n            if cfg.exists():\\n                return                           # never clobber an author's config\\n            data: dict = {\\\"$schema\\\": \\\"https://opencode.ai/config.json\\\", \\\"permission\\\": \\\"allow\\\"}\\n            if spec.model and \\\"/\\\" in spec.model:\\n                provider, model_id = spec.model.split(\\\"/\\\", 1)\\n                pblock: dict = {\\\"options\\\": {\\\"timeout\\\": 3600000}}   # default 5m kills long runs\\n                if spec.effort:\\n                    pblock[\\\"models\\\"] = {model_id: {\\\"options\\\": {\\\"reasoningEffort\\\": spec.effort}}}\\n                data[\\\"provider\\\"] = {provider: pblock}\\n            tmp = workdir / \\\".opencode.json.tmp\\\"\\n            tmp.write_text(json.dumps(data) + \\\"\\\\n\\\", encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n            os.replace(tmp, cfg)\\n\\n    def build(self, spec, prompt_file, prompt_text, timeout_s):\\n        argv = [\\\"opencode\\\", \\\"run\\\", \\\"--agent\\\", \\\"build\\\"]\\n        if spec.model:\\n            argv += [\\\"-m\\\", spec.model]\\n        argv += spec.args\\n        argv.append(prompt_text)                 # positional prompt (argv list, no shell)\\n        # opencode's run mode emits EVERYTHING (assistant text included) on\\n        # stderr with ANSI decoration; --format json is half-alive on 1.15.5\\n        # (single step_start event, rc 0 — probed live). parallel{cap_note}\\\", BOLD + GREEN))\\n    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=workers) as pool:\\n        futures = [pool.submit(run_item, item) for item in items]\\n         self.display.info(f\\\"[dim]round {round_num} · {self._fmt_duration(elapsed)}[/]\\\")\\n250\\t\\n251\\t            if result.exit_code != 0:\\n252\\t                consecutive_failures +=
