\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        from pilot.vars import read_vars\\n\\n        existing = read_vars(self.vars_path)\\n        for key, value in self.config.vars.items():\\n            if key not in existing:\\n                write_var(self.vars_path, key, value)\\n\\n        export_vars(self.vars_path)\\n\\n    def _apply_var(self, key: str, value: str) -> None:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Persist a var to disk and into os.environ in one atomic step.\\n\\n        Used for both agent-emitted <signal:var> tags and shell-step\\n        signal-scanning so vars become live for downstream consumers in\\n        the same round.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        write_var(self.vars_path, key, value)\\n        os.environ[key] = value\\n\\n    def _handle_var_signal(self, sig: Signal, source: str | None = None) -> bool:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"If `sig` is a `<signal:var key=K>V</signal:var>` tag, persist it\\n        and emit a display line. Returns True iff the signal was a var.\\n\\n        Centralises the var-handling logic shared by `_on_signal` (executor\\n        callbacks) and `_run_step` (shell-hook stdout scanning).\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        if sig.name != \\\"var\\\" or \\\"key\\\" not in sig.attrs:\\n            return False\\n        self._apply_var(sig.attrs[\\\"key\\\"], sig.content)\\n        key = rich_escape(sig.attrs[\\\"key\\\"])\\n        val = rich_escape(sig.content)\\n        prefix = f\\\"\\\\\\\\[{source}] \\\" if source else \\\"\\\"\\n        self.display.info(f\\\"{prefix}[dim]var[/] {key}[dim]=[/]{val}\\\")\\n        return True\\n\\n    @staticmethod\\n    def _fmt_duration(seconds: int) -> str:\\n        if seconds >= 3600:\\n            return f\\\"{seconds // 3600}h{(seconds % 3600) // 60:02d}m\\\"\\n        if seconds >= 60:\\n            return f\\\"{seconds // 60}m{seconds % 60:02d}s\\\"\\n        return f\\\"{seconds}s\\\"\\n\\n    def run(self) -> None:\\n        # Export config dir so scripts and prompts can reference it\\n        abs_config_dir = os.path.abspath(self.config_dir)\\n        os.environ[\\\"PILOT_CONFIG_DIR\\\"] = abs_config_dir\\n        os.environ[\\\"PILOT_PIPELINE_NAME\\\"] = os.path.basename(abs_config_dir)\\n        write_var(self.vars_path, \\\"PILOT_CONFIG_DIR\\\", abs_config_dir)\\n\\n        # Load .env (secrets) before anything\\n        self._load_env_file()\\n\\n        # CLI vars: applied before _sync_env so they take precedence over\\n        # pipeline-yaml `vars:` defaults (which _sync_env writes only when\\n        # absent). They remain mutable by pre_step / agent signals at runtime.\\n        for key, value in self.cli_vars.items():\\n            self._apply_var(key, value)\\n\\n        # Sync config vars so pre_pipeline can use them\\n        self._sync_env()\\n\\n        # Pipeline pre_pipeline\\n        if self.config.pre_pipeline:\\n            if not self._run_step(\\\"pre_pipeline\\\", self.config.pre_pipeline, show_output=True):\\n                raise PipelineError(\\\"pre_pipeline failed\\\")\\n\\n        self._pipeline_rounds = 0\\n        self._pipeline_last_stage = self.state.stage\\n        pipeline_start = time.monotonic()\\n\\n        exit_status = \\\"failed\\\"\\n        try:\\n            exit_status = self._main_loop()\\n        finally:\\n            # Expose pipeline metadata as env vars for hooks\\n            total = int(time.monotonic() - pipeline_start)\\n            os.environ[\\\"PILOT_EXIT_STATUS\\\"] = exit_status\\n            os.environ[\\\"PILOT_DURATION\\\"] = self._fmt_duration(total)\\n            os.environ[\\\"PILOT_ROUNDS\\\"] = str(self._pipeline_rounds)\\n            os.environ[\\\"PILOT_LAST_STAGE\\\"] = self._pipeline_last_stage\\n\\n            # Clear state before conditional hooks (avoid stale state if chaining)\\n            if exit_status == \\\"success\\\":\\n                clear_state(self.state_path)\\n                clear_vars(self.vars_path)\\n\\n            # Conditional hooks (notify / chain / cleanup)\\n            if exit_status == \\\"success\\\" and self.config.on_pipeline_success:\\n                if not self._run_step(\\\"on_pipeline_success\\\", self.config.on_pipeline_success, show_output=True):\\n                    self.display.warn(\\\"on_pipeline_success failed\\\")\\n            elif exit_status == \\\"failed\\\" and self.config.on_pipeline_failure:\\n                if not self._run_step(\\\"on_pipeline_failure\\\", self.config.on_pipeline_failure, show_output=True):\\n                    self.display.warn(\\\"on_pipeline_failure failed\\\")\\n\\n    def _main_loop(self) -> str:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Run the main pipeline loop. Returns 'success' or 'failed'.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        consecutive_failures = 0\\n        round_num = 0\\n        pipeline_start = time.monotonic()\\n\\n        while True:\\n            round_num += 1\\n            round_start = time.monotonic()\\n            self._sync_env()\\n\\n            stage = self.config.stages.get(self.state.stage)\\n            if not stage:\\n                raise PipelineError(f\\\"Unknown stage: {self.state.stage}\\\")\\n            self._pipeline_rounds = round_num\\n            self._pipeline_last_stage = stage.name\\n\\n            if stage.is_ensemble:\\n                runners_info = [(r.executor, r.model) for r in stage.runners]\\n                self.display.round_header_ensemble(round_num, stage.name, runners_info)\\n            else:\\n                self.display.round_header(\\n                    round_num,\\n                    stage.name,\\n                    stage.runner.executor,\\n                    stage.runner.model,\\n                )\\n\\n            # pre_step — shell command before main executor; may emit\\n            # <signal:var> tags that become live for the executor below.\\n            if stage.pre_step:\\n                pre_ok = self._run_step(\\\"pre_step\\\", stage.pre_step)\\n                if not pre_ok:\\n                    consecutive_failures += 1\\n                    self.display.warn(\\n                        f\\\"pre_step failed, \\\"\\n                        f\\\"consecutive failures {consecutive_failures}/3\\\"\\n                    )\\n                    if consecutive_failures >= 3:\\n                        raise PipelineError(\\\"3 consecutive round failures\\\")\\n                    self._wait()\\n                    continue\\n\\n            # Per-runner stages defer template resolution until each worker\\n            # spawns, so runner.vars overrides apply correctly.\\n            raw_prompt = self._get_raw_prompt(stage)\\n\\n            # Known signals for this stage. Ensemble stages capture every\\n            # signal name (informational tags like <signal:done verdict=...>\\n            # belong on disk even though routing always falls through to\\n            # 'default').\\n            if stage.is_ensemble:\\n                known = None\\n            else:\\n                known = set(stage.on_signal.keys()) | BUILTIN_SIGNALS\\n\\n            # Run executor(s)\\n            self._live_signals = []\\n            if stage.is_ensemble:\\n                result = self._run_ensemble(stage, raw_prompt, known, round_num)\\n            else:\\n                result = self._run_with_retries(stage, raw_prompt, known)\\n\\n            # post_step — shell command after main executor (always runs)\\n            if stage.post_step:\\n                post_ok = self._run_step(\\\"post_step\\\", stage.post_step)\\n                if not post_ok:\\n                    self.display.warn(\\\"post_step failed (continuing)\\\")\\n\\n            elapsed = int(time.monotonic() - round_start)\\n            self.display.info(f\\\"[dim]round {round_num} · {self._fmt_duration(elapsed)}[/]\\\")\\n\\n            if result.exit_code != 0:\\n                consecutive_failures += 1\\n                self.display.warn(\\n                    f\\\"Round failed, \\\"\\n                    f\\\"consecutive failures {consecutive_failures}/3\\\"\\n                )\\n                if result.error:\\n                    self.display.warn(result.error)\\n                if consecutive_failures >= 3:\\n                    raise PipelineError(\\\"3 consecutive round failures\\\")\\n                self._wait()\\n                continue\\n\\n            consecutive_failures = 0\\n\\n            # Signals already displayed in real-time via _on_signal callback\\n            signals = self._live_signals\\n\\n            # Find first domain signal for routing (ensemble stages route via 'default' only)\\n            domain = None\\n domain.name in stage.on_signal:\\n                transition = stage.on_signal[domain.name]\\n            elif \\\"default\\\" in stage.on_signal:\\n                transition = stage.on_signal[\\\"default\\\"]\\n            else:\\n                raise PipelineError(f\\\"No default in stage '{stage.name}'\\\")\\n\\n            # Transition — exit pipeline\\n            if transition.to is None:\\n                total = int(time.monotonic() - pipeline_start)\\n                summary = domain.content if domain else \\\"complete\\\"\\n                if transition.fail:\\n                    self.display.error(f\\\"Pipeline stopped: {summary}\\\")\\n                    return \\\"failed\\\"\\n                else:\\n                    self.display.done(summary, f\\\"{round_num} rounds, {self._fmt_duration(total)}\\\")\\n                    return \\\"success\\\"\\n\\n            # Transition — next stage\\n            old_stage = self._wait()\\n\\n        return \\\"failed\\\"  # unreachable, loop exits via return or exception\\n\\n    def _make_callbacks(self, source: str | None):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Build (on_output, on_signal) closures.\\n\\n        source: per-runner tag for ensemble stages (e.g. \\\"claude-code/opus\\\"),\\n                None for single-runner stages.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        def {**sig.attrs, \\\"source\\\": source}\\n            self._on_signal(sig)\\n\\n        return on_output, on_signal\\n\\n    def _resolve_runner_vars(self, runner: Runner) -> dict[str, str]:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Resolve each runner.vars value as a template against global vars.\\n\\n        This lets ensemble runners reference pre_step-emitted vars\\n        (`{{var:ROUND_DIR}}`) inside their per-runner config without any\\n        new template namespace.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        if not runner.vars:\\n            return {}\\n        resolved: dict[str, str] = {}\\n        for k, v vars_overrides=self._resolve_runner_vars(runner) or None,\\n        )\\n\\n    def _run_with_retries(self, stage: Stage, raw_prompt: str,\\n                          known: set[str] | None) -> ExecutorResult:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Try primary runner twice, then fallback runner twice.\\n\\n        Returns the first successful result, or the last failed result.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        runners = [(stage.runner, \\\"primary\\\")]\\n        if stage.fallback_runner:\\n            runners.append((stage.fallback_runner, \\\"fallback\\\"))\\n\\n        on_output, on_signal = runner)\\n            except TemplateError as e:\\n                raise PipelineError(f\\\"Template error: {e}\\\") from e\\n\\n            executor = self.executors.get(runner.executor)\\n args=runner.args,\\n                )\\n                if result.exit_code == 0:\\n                    return result\\n\\n                tag = f\\\"{runner.executor}/{runner.model}\\\" if runner.model else runner.executor\\n                self.display.warn(\\n                    f\\\"{tag} failed \\\"\\n                    f\\\"(attempt {attempt}/2, exit {result.exit_code})\\\"\\n       # Switch to fallback\\n            if label == \\\"primary\\\" and stage.fallback_runner:\\n                fb = stage.fallback_runner\\n                fb_tag = self.display.warn(f\\\"[{source}] template error: {e}\\\")\\n                return source, ExecutorResult(\\n                    output=\\\"\\\", exit_code=1, error=str(e), signals=[],\\n     timer = threading.Timer(float(timeout), cancel.set)\\n                timer.daemon = True\\n                timer.start()\\n\\n            worker_start = time.monotonic()\\n            run_result:        raise ValueError(f\\\"stage '{name}' has invalid target '{st}' for signal '{sig}'\\\")\\n    elif \\\"runner\\\" in step:\\n        _validate_runner(step[\\\"runner\\\"], name, f\\\"signal '{sig}' runner\\\")\\n    elif \\\"reset_iterations\\\" in step:\\n        pass\\n    else:\\n        raise ValueError(f\\\"stage '{name}' signal '{sig}' step must define 'runner', 'stage', or 'reset_iterations'\\\")\\n\\n\\ndef _resolve_stage_target(target):\\n    if isinstance(target, str):\\n        return target\\n    if isinstance(target, dict):\\n        return target.get(\\\"stage\\\")\\n    if isinstance(target, list):\\n        for item in target:\\n            if isinstance(item, dict) and \\\"stage\\\" in item:\\n                return item[\\\"stage\\\"]\\n    return None\\n\\n\\ndef validate_pipeline(data: dict) -> None:\\n    if \\\"starting\\\" not in data or \\\"stages\\\" not in data:\\n        raise ValueError(\\\"pipeline must contain 'starting' and 'stages'\\\")\\n    stages = data.get(\\\"stages\\\")\\n    if not isinstance(stages, dict) or not stages:\\n        raise ValueError(\\\"stages must be a non-empty mapping\\\")\\n    start = data.get(\\\"starting\\\")\\n    if start not in stages:\\n        raise ValueError(f\\\"starting stage not found: {start}\\\")\\n    pipeline_fallback = data.get(\\\"fallback_runner\\\")\\n    if pipeline_fallback is not None:\\n        _validate_runner(pipeline_fallback, \\\"pipeline\\\", \\\"fallback_runner\\\")\\n    for name, stage in stages.items():\\n        if not isinstance(stage, dict):\\n            raise ValueError(f\\\"stage '{name}' must be a mapping\\\")\\n        runner = stage.get(\\\"runner\\\")\\n        if runner is None:\\n            raise ValueError(f\\\"stage '{name}' must define runner\\\")\\n        _validate_runner(runner, name)\\n        fallback = stage.get(\\\"fallback_runner\\\")\\n        if fallback is not None:\\n            _validate_runner(fallback, name, \\\"fallback_runner\\\")\\n        on_signal = stage.get(\\\"on_signal\\\")\\n        if not isinstance(on_signal, dict):\\n items must be objects\\\")\\n                    _validate_signal_step(item, name, sig, stages)\\n                    if \\\"stage\\\" in item:\\n                        has_stage = True\\n missing 'loop_done' in on_signal\\\")\\n\\n\\ndef render_text(text: str, base_dir: Path, vars_map: dict[str, str]) -> str:\\n    def expand_files(src: str, resolve_dir: Path, depth: int = 0) -> str:\\n        if depth > 10:\\n            return src\\n\\n        def repl(m: re.Match) -> str:\\n            rel = m.group(1).strip()\\n            target = (resolve_dir / rel).resolve()\\n            content = target.read_text(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n            return expand_files(content, target.parent, depth + 1)\\n\\n        return FILE_TAG_RE.sub(repl, src)\\n\\n    text = expand_files(text, base_dir)\\n\\n    def var_repl(m: re.Match) -> str:\\n        key = m.group(1)\\n        default = m.group(2) if m.group(2) is not None else \\\"\\\"\\n        value = vars_map.get(key)\\n        if value is None or value == \\\"\\\":\\n            return str(default)\\n        return str(value)\\n\\n    text = VAR_TAG_RE.sub(var_repl, text)\\n    text = LOOP_ITEM_TAG_RE.sub(lambda m: str(vars_map.get(\\\"__item__\\\", \\\"\\\")), text)\\n    text = LOOP_LIST_ITEM_TAG_RE.sub(lambda m: str(vars_map.get(\\\"__list_item__\\\", \\\"\\\")), text)\\n Strongest bet / most likely regret\\n\\n**Strongest: bet 2, the signal-contract switch.** Pool bodies write to a\\nmanifest, only `min_success` at the join routes (`engine.py:368-379`,\\n`contract.py:212-216` rejects bare signals in pool `on_signal`). This closes\\na real v1 failure mode: v1's `_run_loop_parallel` decided per-item success by\\nscanning for `next_item_signals` membership *or* `rc == 0`\\n(`/tmp/v1_runner.py:180-186`) — an agent that emitted a stray signal-shaped\\nstring in tool output could silently flip an item to done. v2's law (\\\"pool\\nsignals are DATA, never route\\\", enforced at load time, not by convention) is\\na structural fix, not a lint rule. (R)\\n\\n**Most likely to be regretted: bet 3, agent silence retries primary-only,\\nnever falls back** (`classify.py:80-89`, README:117). The stated reason —\\n\\\"another model drops the tag just as often; blind fallback duplicates side\\neffects\\\" — is a real risk, but it's asymmetric: it protects against a\\ndouble-write on a *stateful* body while guaranteeing that a *primary model\\nhaving a bad day* (rate-limited, degraded, mis-following instructions) drains\\nits entire `max_attempts` budget on itself and then hard-fails via\\n`__default__`, even though the whole reason `fallback` exists is \\\"primary is\\nunreliable, try someone else.\\\" A budget-gate retry-node can route around\\n`__failed__`, but `__default__` is a distinct terminal with its own edge\\n(README:340, `defaults.on_signal: {__failed__: notify, __default__: notify}`)\\n— so silence-driven exhaustion needs its *own* handling, which most authors\\nwill forget to add, because `fallback:` visually reads as \\\"the safety net for\\nthis action\\\" and it silently isn't one for the single most common agent flake\\nclass (README:117 calls silence \\\"the most common agent flake\\\"). One year of\\nproduction use with prompts that drift or model updates that change tagging\\nhabits will surface pipelines that fail hard on a fixable communication\\nglitch, precisely the class of failure `fallback` was built to catch. (R for\\nthe code path; G for the year-out prediction)\\n\\n## B. What v2 lost that tests can't show\\n\\n1. **Live terminal UX during a run.** v1's `output.py` (imported by\\n`runner.py:9-13`) gave colored round banners, transition arrows, per-item\\nparallel status lines (`ansi(..., BOLD+GREEN)`, `round_banner`,\\n`round_stats`). v2's `log()` (`engine.py:56-57`) is one bare\\n`[medulla] step N | node -> signal -> target` line to stderr. Functionally\\nequivalent for the journal; materially worse for a human babysitting a long\\nrun at a terminal — no coverage in the unit or live-test suite because none\\nof it asserts terminal *output*, only structured outcomes. (R)\\n\\n2. **Cross-invocation persistent vars + real SIGTERM cleanup.** v1's\\n`vars_map` synced to `.medulla/vars.<task>.yaml` on disk independent of any\\nrun directory (`load_vars`/`save_var`, `runner.py`), and registered\\n`atexit`/`SIGTERM` handlers (`_cleanup_bridge_bg`, `runner.py:200-270`) to\\nkill background dev servers, stop docker containers, and clean bridge\\nsockets on a *graceful* kill, not just SIGKILL. v2 explicitly deletes this\\n(\\\"no finally... `kill -9` makes exit hooks an illusion\\\", README:306) and\\nonly catches `KeyboardInterrupt` (`engine.py:941-944`) — SIGTERM is\\nunhandled, so a pipeline that legitimately backgrounds a process (dev\\nserver, emulator) and gets `docker stop`/systemd-SIGTERM'd leaks it. The\\ncrash-only philosophy is defensible against `kill -9`, but SIGTERM was\\ngraceful in v1 and is now treated identically to SIGKILL. The live-test\\nsuite only checks SIGINT (`t14-interrupt`), not SIGTERM, so this gap is\\ninvisible to it. (R)\\n\\n3. **Inline per-signal steps.** v1 allowed a transition to carry inline\\n`runner:` steps or `reset_iterations` alongside the stage jump\\n(`pipeline.py:70-79`, `_resolve_signal_target`). v2 forces every such action\\ninto its own node+edge (README's \\\"handlers are ordinary nodes\\\"). Cleaner\\ncontract, but it inflates small pipelines' node count for what used to be a\\none-line hook — not tested either way since it's a authoring-ergonomics\\ndelta, not a behavior delta.\\n\\n## C. One change before it calcifies\\n\\nSplit `__default__`'s routing from `__failed__`'s: **let silence use the\\nfallback runner, gated behind an explicit opt-out**, rather than hard-wiring\\n\\\"primary only, never fallback\\\" as the only mode. The manifest/journal already\\nrecord `reason` per failure class (`recorded_signal`, `failure_class` in\\n`AttemptsOutcome`), so an author who genuinely needs the current\\nduplicate-avoidance guarantee (mutating body, side-effect-heavy) could opt in\\nto \\\"no fallback on silence,\\\" while the default serves the common case\\n(idempotent/read-mostly agent bodies) where blind fallback on silence is\\nobviously better than a hard stop. Right now the contract picked the\\nconservative branch for everyone and calls it done in README's frozen\\ncontract — that's the one clause I'd unfreeze.\\n\\n## D. Verdict: deleting v1 outright\\n\\n**Right call, not hubris — for the actual conditions stated.** Single\\nmaintainer, one production workflow, and a *documented* migration table\\n(README:317-337) mapping every v1 primitive to its v2 equivalent is the\\nopposite of hubris; it's the correct cost/benefit call when running a\\ncompatibility shim (dual engines, dual test suites, dual mental models)\\ncosts more than porting the one live pipeline by hand once. The panel-review\\nprocess (audit references littered through the code — \\\"audit R1\\\", \\\"audit\\nR3\\\", \\\"audit G7\\\", \\\"audit G8\\\", \\\"audit G9\\\" in `harness.py`, `procrun.py`,\\n`engine.py`) shows the deletion was preceded by actually re-deriving v1's\\nscars (agy `--print` token-eating, opencode's dead `--format json`, the\\n`kill -9` orphan bug from `t14`) rather than dropping them. The one place\\nthis reads as overconfidence rather than diligence is **bet 3** above: the\\ncontract document calls itself \\\"frozen\\\" while shipping a debatable one-way\\ndoor on silence-handling that the authors themselves flag as \\\"the most\\ncommon agent flake\\\" — freezing a contract *before* a full production season\\nunder real model-drift conditions is the part that could bite. (R)\\n\\n**Most likely to be regretted: bet 3, agent silence retries primary-only,\\nnever falls back** (`classify.py:80-89`, README:117). The stated reason —\\n\\\"another model drops the tag just as often; blind fallback duplicates side\\neffects\\\" — is a real risk, but it's asymmetric: it protects against a\\ndouble-write on a *stateful* body while guaranteeing that a *primary model\\nhaving a bad day* (rate-limited, degraded, mis-following instructions) drains\\nits entire `max_attempts` budget on itself and then hard-fails via\\n`__default__`, even though the whole reason `fallback` exists is \\\"primary is\\nunreliable, try someone else.\\\" A budget-gate retry-node can route around\\n`__failed__`, but `__default__` is a distinct terminal with its own edge\\n(README:340, `defaults.on_signal: {__failed__: notify, __default__: notify}`)\\n— so silence-driven exhaustion needs its *own* handling, which most authors\\nwill forget to add, because `fallback:` visually reads as \\\"the safety net for\\nthis action\\\" and it silently isn't one for the single most common agent flake\\nclass (README:117 calls silence \\\"the most common agent flake\\\"). One year of\\nproduction use with prompts that drift or model updates that change tagging\\nhabits will surface pipelines that fail hard on a fixable communication\\nglitch, precisely the class of failure `fallback` was built to catch. (R for\\nthe code path; G for the year-out prediction)\\n\\n## B. What v2 lost that tests can't show\\n\\n1. **Live terminal UX during a run.** v1's `output.py` (imported by\\n`runner.py:9-13`) gave colored round banners, transition arrows, per-item\\nparallel status lines (`ansi(..., BOLD+GREEN)`, `round_banner`,\\n`round_stats`). v2's `log()` (`engine.py:56-57`) is one bare\\n`[medulla] step N | node -> signal -> target` line to stderr. Functionally\\nequivalent for the journal; materially worse for a human babysitting a long\\nrun at a terminal — no coverage in the unit or live-test suite because none\\nof it asserts terminal *output*, only structured outcomes. (R)\\n\\n2. **Cross-invocation persistent vars + real SIGTERM cleanup.** v1's\\n`vars_map` synced to `.medulla/vars.<task>.yaml` on disk independent of any\\nrun directory (`load_vars`/`save_var`, `runner.py`), and registered\\n`atexit`/`SIGTERM` handlers (`_cleanup_bridge_bg`, `runner.py:200-270`) to\\nkill background dev servers, stop docker containers, and clean bridge\\nsockets on a *graceful* kill, not just SIGKILL. v2 explicitly deletes this\\n(\\\"no finally... `kill -9` makes exit hooks an illusion\\\", README:306) and\\nonly catches `KeyboardInterrupt` (`engine.py:941-944`) — SIGTERM is\\nunhandled, so a pipeline that legitimately backgrounds a process (dev\\nserver, emulator) and gets `docker stop`/systemd-SIGTERM'd leaks it. The\\ncrash-only philosophy is defensible against `kill -9`, but SIGTERM was\\ngraceful in v1 and is now treated identically to SIGKILL. The live-test\\nsuite only checks SIGINT (`t14-interrupt`), not SIGTERM, so this gap is\\ninvisible to it. (R)\\n\\n3. **Inline per-signal steps.** v1 allowed a transition to carry inline\\n`runner:` steps or `reset_iterations` alongside the stage jump\\n(`pipeline.py:70-79`, `_resolve_signal_target`). v2 forces every such action\\ninto its own node+edge (README's \\\"handlers are ordinary nodes\\\"). Cleaner\\ncontract, but it inflates small pipelines' node count for what used to be a\\none-line hook — not tested either way since it's a authoring-ergonomics\\ndelta, not a behavior delta.\\n\\n## C. One change before it calcifies\\n\\nSplit `__default__`'s routing from `__failed__`'s: **let silence use the\\nfallback runner, gated behind an explicit opt-out**, rather than hard-wiring\\n\\\"primary only, never fallback\\\" as the only mode. Flat scalars only — never merged deep |\nmedulla/README.md:117:| `max_attempts` | attempts per runner, default 1. Primary gets N, then fallback gets N. Retryable outcomes: non-zero exit, timeout (recorded as rc 124), and **silence** — an agent body exiting 0 with no known signal (the most common agent flake: the work happened, the tag didn't). Silence retries on the **primary only** and never triggers fallback (another model drops the tag just as often; blind fallback duplicates side effects); after all attempts it classifies as `__default__`. Shell silence is deterministic and is not retried. **Silence handling is decision-node behavior**: in pools the body is not expected to signal — silence at rc 0 is the normal ok outcome (see the pool rows) |\nmedulla/README.md:118:| `fallback` | alternate agent action after primary attempts are exhausted. Agent-only (a fallback for shell is meaningless); a fallback has no fallback |\nmedulla/README.md:119:| `ignore_exit_code` | rc != 0 doesn't classify the body as failed; outcome comes from signals. **Forbidden in pool nodes** — `min_success` already owns that role |\nmedulla/README.md:132:| exits non-zero | the **attempt failed** → body+post retry within `max_attempts`, then fallback (\"try until the artifact exists\": `post: test -s out.md`) |\nmedulla/README.md:136:In pools both run per input: a `pre` guard skips already-done inputs (manifest `ok: true` with the guard's signal); a `post` check is the truth channel for agent inputs — CLIs exit 0 even when the model did nothing, so without `post` a pool's `min_success` counts air.\nmedulla/README.md:144:| `min_success` | join threshold: `all` (default) \\| N ≥ 1. Input ok = rc == 0. No short-circuiting ever — all inputs run (side effects have value) |\nmedulla/README.md:160:| `__done__` | pool join | successes ≥ `min_success` | **none — must be routed explicitly** (it's a real edge) |\nmedulla/README.md:161:| `__failed__` | engine | decision node: body died after all attempts+fallback. Pool: join below threshold | `__exit_fail__` |\nmedulla/README.md:163:| `__default__` | nobody (matcher) | body exited rc 0 with no known signal (\"said nothing\") | `__exit_fail__` |\nmedulla/README.md:179:Signals are read from **stdout only**; stderr goes verbatim to the attempt log (a traceback that happens to echo signal text must never route the graph). A known signal emitted before a non-zero exit wins over the exit code. Pool bodies' signals never route — they are recorded in the manifest (law of layers: inputs produce data, joins produce transitions). Bare `done`/`failed` on decision nodes are ordinary user signals with no special meaning. User nodes may not be named `__*__` or `on/off/yes/no/true/false` (YAML 1.1 boolean traps).\nmedulla/README.md:192:Render model: phase 1 — file inclusion; phase 2 — one simultaneous, **inert** pass of var/input substitution. **Files are code, values are data**: mustache inside included files resolves fully; mustache inside var/input *values* stays literal (injection-safe by construction). Every scalar field of an action is a template (that's why an ensemble is just a pool with per-input `harness`/`model`). A field rendering to an empty string counts as absent — for optional agent fields only; empty `shell`/`prompt`/`harness` is an error. Rendering happens **once per node run**, before attempt 1; retries reuse the same rendered text. A render error on a decision node (phantom input) is an engine crash (`E_RENDER`) — the template itself is broken. A render error for **one pool input** fails that input only (manifest row `reason: render` with the template path and input index) — one malformed row of producer data must not kill the other inputs.\nmedulla/README.md:202:| `MEDULLA_LAST_NODE` / `_SIGNAL` / `_MESSAGE` / `_RC` | outcome of the previously completed node, published before every transition (after a pool, `_RC` is empty — a join has no single rc; read the manifest); `MEDULLA_LAST_EVENT_JSON` carries the same as one JSON object. Bodies emit **signals**; signals route the graph. Built in: retries, fallback to another model, pools with a success threshold, and crash-safe resume.\nmedulla/README.md:7:graph = nodes + on_signal edges + two terminals (__exit_ok__ / __exit_fail__)\nmedulla/README.md:10:**One machine.** Internally every node is a pool: a node without `inputs:` runs over a single phantom input. Policy (timeouts, attempts, fallback) works identically in both modes. The only thing `inputs:` switches is the **signal contract**: without inputs the body talks to the graph (decision node); with inputs the body writes to the manifest and only the join result routes (pool node).\nmedulla/README.md:19:medulla -w ... --node <name>                      # dev: start from a specific node\nmedulla/README.md:39:  # decision node: body signals route the graph\nmedulla/README.md:179:Signals are read from **stdout only**; stderr goes verbatim to the attempt log (a traceback that happens to echo signal text must never route the graph). A known signal emitted before a non-zero exit wins over the exit code. Pool bodies' signals never route — they are recorded in the manifest (law of layers: inputs produce data, joins produce transitions). Bare `done`/`failed` on decision nodes are ordinary user signals with no special meaning. User nodes may not be named `__*__` or `on/off/yes/no/true/false` (YAML 1.1 boolean traps).\nmedulla/README.md:213:Two failure classes. The test: *can it be fixed by changing data/prompts/retrying?* → workflow failure, the graph decides. *Does it require editing the pipeline/environment?* → engine crash.\nmedulla/README.md:219:| 2 | workflow failure | the graph routed to `__exit_fail__` — fix the task/inputs |\nmedulla/README.md:228:#### Handling errors in the graph\nmedulla/README.md:243:A node-level edge beats the defaults catch-all, which beats built-ins — a three-tier supervision chain. The validator rejects a defaults-inherited edge that points at its own node (`notify` failing into `notify` would loop); explicit self-loops remain legal (retry pattern). A pool's `__failed__` message is pre-aggregated: `\"2/5 inputs ok (min_success=3); rc!=0 x2, timeout x1, render x0\"` — the manifest has the rest. Class A is not catchable in the graph by definition: the graph itself is what's broken.\nmedulla/README.md:256:**Boot** — parse CLI → load yaml (`version: \"2\"` or a friendly migration error) → validate everything (`E_VALIDATION` before any run dir exists) → create `runs/<ts>-<run_id>/`, snapshot the config, seed vars, prune old runs → set the deadline.\nmedulla/README.md:275:    journal.jsonl          # graph chronology, append-only (step, node, rc, signal, message, duration)\nmedulla/README.md:335:| `--stage` | `--node` |\nmedulla/cli-agent/tests/test_resume.py:209:def test_cli_flag_based_run_and_validate(tmp_path, monkeypatch, capsys):\nmedulla/cli-agent/tests/test_resume.py:220:    assert cli_main([\"-w\", str(path.parent), \"--validate\"]) == 0\nmedulla/cli-agent/tests/test_resume.py:239:    assert cli_main([\"-w\", str(path.parent), \"--dry-run\"]) == 0\nmedulla/cli-agent/tests/test_resume.py:252:        [\"-w\", str(path.parent), \"--resume\", \"--node\", \"a\"],    # node is fresh-only\nmedulla/cli-agent/medulla/v2/contract.py:1:\"\"\"Load + validate + normalize pipeline.yaml into the v2 model.\nmedulla/cli-agent/medulla/v2/contract.py:228:def _validate_var_name(key: str, where: str) -> None:\nmedulla/cli-agent/medulla/v2/contract.py:315:    # graph checks\nmedulla/cli-agent/medulla/v2/contract.py:341:        _validate_var_name(key, \"vars\")\nmedulla/cli-agent/medulla/v2/errors.py:13:    \"\"\"Class A: the pipeline itself is broken. '35,90p;106,115p'\"","aggregated_output":"    35\t## CLI Reference\n    36\t\n    37\t`medulla` supports two invocation styles (the `medulla` command comes from pip; from a source checkout use the repo-root `./medulla` launcher).\n    38\t\n    39\t- Workflow mode: `medulla -w <workflow> [options]`\n    40\t- Direct pipeline mode: `medulla <run|validate|graph> <pipeline.yaml> [options]`\n    41\t\n    42\t### All CLI keys\n    43\t\n    44\t- `-w`, `--workflow <name>` - resolve `.medulla/workflows/<name>/pipeline.yaml` and run that installed workflow.\n    45\t- `run <pipeline.yaml>` - run a pipeline by explicit file path.\n    46\t- `validate <pipeline.yaml>` - validate a pipeline and exit without running stages.\n    47\t- `graph <pipeline.yaml>` - generate and open a graph for a pipeline file.\n    48\t- `--var KEY=VALUE` - override a pipeline variable; repeatable; values split on the first `=` only.\n    49\t- `--docker` - run through the Docker wrapper instead of directly on the host.\n    50\t- `--mount PATH` - Docker mode only; mount an existing extra directory **read-only** at `/workspace/<dirname>`.\n    51\t- `--mount-rw PATH` - Docker mode only; mount an existing extra directory **read-write** at `/workspace/<dirname>`.\n    52\t- `--host` - auto-start `.medulla/scripts/host-builder.sh`, the host bridge for native tools such as `xcodebuild`, `cargo`, `gradle`, `java`, and `maestro`.\n    53\t- `--stage STAGE` - skip to a specific stage (bypass earlier rounds; requires vars from a prior run or `--var`).\n    54\t- `--dry-run` - load/resolve the pipeline without executing stage commands.\n    55\t- `--verbose` - enable verbose logging and executor diagnostics.\n    56\t- `--build` - Docker mode only; force image rebuild via `scripts/docker.py` with `--no-cache`.\n    57\t- `graph` generates a graph file for the pipeline.\n    58\t- If neither `-w` nor a valid `<command> <pipeline>` pair is provided, `medulla` exits with usage help.\n    59\t\n    60\t### Common command patterns\n    61\t\n    62\t```bash\n    63\t# Run an installed workflow\n    64\tmedulla -w <workflow>\n    65\t\n    66\t# Run a workflow with variables\n    67\tmedulla -w <workflow> \\\n    68\t  --var KEY=VALUE \\\n    69\t  --var OTHER_KEY=OTHER_VALUE\n    70\t\n    71\t# Dry-run a workflow locally\n    72\tmedulla -w <workflow> --dry-run --verbose\n    73\t\n    74\t# Validate a raw pipeline file\n    75\tmedulla validate path/to/pipeline.yaml\n    76\t\n    77\t# Generate a graph for a raw pipeline file\n    78\tmedulla graph path/to/pipeline.yaml\n
